Thumbnail & Editorial Image Designer Nee.t: Thumbnail & Editorial Image Designer Needed for Newsletter
Thumbnail & Editorial Image Designer Nee.d: I’m looking for a talented thumbnail designer / graphic designer to help create a consistent visual style for my business newsletter. Each email will need one main hero thumbnail plus 3–4 additional supporting images based on the content inside the issue. The style I’m looking for is similar to Vox-style editorial explainer graphics: textured paper backgrounds, grayscale cutout photography, bold typography, yellow highlight blocks, hand-drawn arrows, charts, documents, collage elements, grid lines, halftone texture, and smart visual storytelling. The graphics should feel like a mix of a documentary explainer, investigative magazine cover, and scroll-stopping YouTube thumbnail. I will provide the newsletter content, topic, and general direction for each issue, but I’m looking for someone who can turn the written ideas into strong visual concepts. The main thumbnail should summarize the biggest story or idea in the email, while the supporting images should help break up the newsletter and make each section feel more premium, visual, and engaging. Topics will usually be around side hustles, business ideas, founder stories, trends, tools, money-making opportunities, startups, and niche markets. Beyond creating individual graphics, I’d also like this person to help develop a repeatable brand visual guideline for the newsletter over time. That means building a consistent design system around color, typography, composition, image treatment, textures, thumbnail layouts, supporting image styles, and reusable templates. The goal is to create a recognizable visual identity that can be used across the newsletter, website, social posts, and future content assets. The ideal person has strong experience with thumbnails, editorial graphics, collage-style design, or content visuals. You should understand visual hierarchy, typography, click-worthy composition, and how to make graphics feel smart but still attention-grabbing. I’m open to whatever tools you prefer, including Photoshop, Illustrator, Figma, Canva, Midjourney, DALL-E, Firefly, or a mix of design and AI tools, as long as the final graphics look polished, consistent, and on-brand. This would start as a test project for one newsletter issue, which includes one main thumbnail and 3–4 supporting images. If it’s a good fit, this could become ongoing weekly work. When applying, please share examples of thumbnail, editorial, collage, newsletter, or social graphics you’ve created, your estimated price per issue, your turnaround time, and how you would approach building a consistent Vox-style visual guideline for the brand. See attached files as example images that I like.

Video Editor + Motion Graphics Creator —.t: Video Editor + Motion Graphics Creator — American Football Educational Course (30 Videos)
Video Editor + Motion Graphics Creator —.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Video Editor + Motion Graphics Creator —.d: ⚠️ READ THIS ENTIRE POST BEFORE APPLYING. THERE IS A REQUIRED WORD IN YOUR APPLICATION (see below). ABOUT THE PROJECT I’m the creator of an online course that teaches people how to understand, watch, and love American football. The course covers everything from basic rules to offensive and defensive formations, play types, player positions, game strategy, and more. The course has approximately 30 video lessons. Each lesson features me on camera, presenting the material directly to students. My job is done — the on-camera footage is recorded and the script is delivered. Your job is to transform that raw footage into a polished, engaging lesson video. WHAT YOU’LL DO Each video will require one or both of the following: 1. Slide Integration — I will provide pre-made slides. You will cut to the slide at the right moment in my script, hold it on screen as I explain the concept, and then cut back to me on camera. Clean, professional timing. 2. Motion Graphics / Animation / Explainer Overlay — Some concepts require motion to understand. For example: a receiver running a route, a defensive line shifting, a play developing in real time. You will create motion graphics, animated diagrams, or short explainer animations that visually demonstrate the concept while my voice plays over it. NON-NEGOTIABLE: YOU MUST UNDERSTAND AMERICAN FOOTBALL This is a hard requirement, not a preference. You cannot create accurate motion graphics for a play-action pass, a cover-2 defense, or a pick route if you don’t know what those things are. We are not in a position to teach you the game while you’re building the videos. You must be able to: • Name and explain basic offensive formations (e.g., I-formation, shotgun, spread) • Understand what “down and distance” means and how game flow works • Animate a receiver route accurately (go route, slant, out, curl, etc.) • Identify player positions on both sides of the ball • Recognize the difference between offensive and defensive schemes at a conceptual level If you’re not sure whether you qualify — you probably don’t. Please do not apply. WHAT I PROVIDE • My on-camera lesson video (cleaned, trimmed, and properly lit) • Full written script with timestamps for where slides and graphics appear • Pre-designed slide assets (PowerPoint or similar) • Brand colors, fonts, and style guide (after test phase) • Clear notes on what each graphic needs to show SKILLS REQUIRED • Video editing (Premiere Pro, Final Cut, or DaVinci Resolve) • Motion graphics and animation (After Effects strongly preferred) • Sports animation experience (any sport is a plus; American football is ideal) • Experience with educational or explainer video format • Strong communication and ability to meet deadlines consistently PROJECT SCOPE This project will begin with a paid test phase of 2 videos. If the quality, communication, and workflow are excellent, this becomes a 30-video ongoing project. • Total project: approximately 30 videos • Turnaround expectation: 1-2 business days per video once assets are delivered for the initial test phase. Total project will need to be completed in 2 - 4 weeks. • Revisions: 2 rounds included per video HOW TO APPLY To be considered, your application must include ALL of the following: 1. The word “Touchdown” at the very start of your cover letter. Applications without this word will not be read. 2. 2–3 portfolio links that are relevant — motion graphics, sports animation, or educational video examples. 3. Answer to this question: Name 3 offensive formations used in American football and briefly explain when a team would use each one. 4. Your software stack (editing + motion graphics). Creating AI videos that look realistic are fantastic, as long as they are accurate. Some concepts may work for realistic AI and some may need to be animation. 5. Your realistic turnaround time per video. IDEAL CANDIDATE You’re the right person for this project if you: • Actually watch football and get excited by the idea of making it visual and accessible • Have created sports-related or educational motion graphics before • Are detail-oriented and take pride in clean, polished final products • Communicate proactively and meet deadlines without being chased • Are looking for an ongoing project — not just a one-off gig This is a meaningful project. Students across the country use this course to finally understand a game that connects them to their families, friends, and communities. The videos need to be excellent. If that excites you, I’d love to hear from you.

Short-Form Video Editor for Aftermarket.t: Short-Form Video Editor for Aftermarket Automotive Parts Company
Short-Form Video Editor for Aftermarket.d: # Short-Form Video Creator / Editor Needed for Automotive Brand We're looking for a talented video creator/editor to turn our existing drone footage, photos, and product imagery into engaging short-form content for our website and social media channels. Our company designs and manufactures premium lift kits and off-road accessories for Toyota and Lexus vehicles. We need someone who can make our products feel premium, exciting, and desirable. We're not simply looking for someone who can assemble clips and add transitions. We're looking for a creator who can tell a story, create emotion, and build content that captures attention quickly. This will begin with a paid trial project. If we're a good fit, we're looking for an ongoing creative partner to help produce content on a regular basis. ## Project Scope Using footage and photos that we provide, create: * Horizontal (16:9) videos for website hero banners * Vertical (9:16) versions for social media ads and organic content * 15-30 second videos with strong pacing and attention-grabbing openings ## Style We're Looking For * Premium and professional * Clean and modern * Automotive/adventure lifestyle feel * Strong hook within the first few seconds * Cinematic without feeling overproduced * Designed to keep viewers watching ## Ideal Experience Experience with any of the following is a plus: * Automotive brands * Trucks and off-road vehicles * Outdoor brands * Adventure/travel content * Product marketing * Performance marketing ads ## To Apply Please include: 1. 3-5 examples of your best short-form video work. 2. Project Overview & Branding Guidelines The organization provides premier, high-quality case management services, with a significant focus on complex motor vehicle accident (MVA) cases. Our work is professional, meticulous, and deeply supportive. All design deliverables must reflect this premium standard. For branding reference, color palettes, typography, and visual tone, please utilize our website. The aesthetic should be clean, modern, trustworthy, and polished. 2. Detailed Deliverable Specifications Deliverable 1: Branded Two-Pocket Presentation Folder • Purpose: To serve as the primary physical package handed to attorneys, medical providers, and clients containing our marketing materials, assessments, and reports. • Physical Specifications: Standard 9" x 12" presentation folder with two interior pockets. The right-hand interior pocket must feature standard die-cut slits to hold a business card. • Design Requirements: • Front Cover: Minimalist, clean, and high-impact. Must prominently feature the logo, brand colors, and core tagline/aesthetic. • Back Cover: Clean design containing essential company contact information (Website, Phone, Email, Office Address) and a clean layout. • Interior Pockets: Can utilize a complementary brand color or subtle geometric/abstract pattern that aligns with the website's visual assets. • Execution Note: Please deliver print-ready files (PDF/X-1a) Deliverable 2: One-Page Business Service Overview • Purpose: A high-end, scannable "leave-behind" sheet focused specifically on our Motor Vehicle Case Management Services. • Format: Single-sided or double-sided 8.5" x 11" flyer/sell sheet (Layout choice left to designer's discretion for optimal whitespace and impact). • Key Content Blocks to Include (Placeholder text is acceptable for draft): • Header: Company branding and a compelling title (e.g., Navigating Recovery: Expert Motor Vehicle Case Management). • The Company Advantage: A brief section highlighting our professional, meticulous approach to managing complex auto injury cases. • Core Services Bullet Points: * Coordination of medical care, rehabilitation, and specialized therapies. • Attendant care assessments and documentation. • Seamless communication between medical providers, legal teams, and insurance adjusters. • Comprehensive discharge planning and home modifications tracking. • Call to Action (CTA): Prominent placement at the bottom directing readers to contact us for referrals or consultations. • Design Style: Utilize clean grids, professional iconography for service bullets, and excellent typographical hierarchy to ensure it can be scanned in under 30 seconds. Deliverable 3: Branded Sample Case Notes Document (Refinement) • Purpose: A polished, professional document used to showcase a "mock case" to attorneys and adjusters, demonstrating the exceptional detail, clarity, and quality of clients clinical documentation. • Format: Multi-page 8.5" x 11" document template (Word or editable PDF format requested for final delivery). • Project Scope: We will provide an existing, real-world sample case notes document to use as a structural baseline. Your role is not to write content, but to completely overhaul the visual design, typography, and layout to transform it into a marketing-professional, highly branded corporate asset. • Design Requirements: • Header/Footer: Professional, unified running header featuring a clean variant of the logo. Footer should include page numbering ‬and a standard confidentiality notice. • Client Information Block: Convert the existing top-page data into a beautifully structured, clean table or grid to display fields such as Client Name, Date of Injury, Claim Number, Case Manager Name, and Report Date. • Body Layout: Upgrade the typography with distinct, professional styling for headings (e.g., Clinical Assessment, Care Coordination Activities, Progress Summary, Recommended Action Plan) to ensure maximum readability. • Visual Elements: Utilize subtle brand colors for section dividers, callout boxes, or table headers to turn standard clinical notes into an executive-level report.

Social Media Publishing Coordinator - Na.t: Social Media Publishing Coordinator - Native Posting on TikTok, Meta, YouTube, LinkedIn, GBP
Social Media Publishing Coordinator - Na.d: Fixed monthly: $800/month, roughly 1-2 hours per weekday on average; weeks with active ad campaigns run heavier. Month one is the audition. We're a legal marketing team, and you'll work on behalf of one of our law firm clients, identity shared after hire under NDA. We're looking for a long-term operator who keeps the machine running every day. One thing to know before you read further: all posting is native, directly on each platform. No Buffer, Hootsuite, Later, or any third-party publishing tool. If your workflow depends on one, this is not your project. You bring your own creation tools: a paid ChatGPT account (or a similar AI image tool) and Canva. We provide the brand templates and the account access for posting. The ongoing work: 1) Publishing. Publish approved posts from a weekly calendar directly on each platform: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, and LinkedIn. The client runs multiple handles per platform for different markets, and you post to every handle we designate (account map shared at kickoff). Native tools only (Meta Business Suite, TikTok's own scheduler, YouTube Studio with titles, descriptions, and tags). Posts go out during business hours, platform collaborations get accepted the same day, nothing sits in drafts. 2) Google Business Profile posting. Multiple posts per week on the profiles we designate, using content we approve. 3) Calendar and copy. Plan the weekly posting calendar and draft post copy for our review. Produce simple graphics in Canva or with AI image tools (ChatGPT or similar) using our brand templates. Finished video comes from our editors, you publish it. 4) Comment and message sweep. Daily sweep of comments and DMs across every platform and every handle, organic posts and ad comments both, completed between 8am and 6pm US Pacific. Hide spam, reply from the approved response library, and flag anything that looks like a potential client to our team the same day. Legal questions never get answered publicly. Publish-time checklist. Every post passes a short compliance checklist we provide (ad-disclosure labels on video, correct tags and links) before it goes live. Month one ($800, the audition): Weeks 1-2: inventory every existing post on the client's social channels with recommendations for what can be repurposed for an active regional campaign, run a caption-compliance sweep of existing video posts, get the weekly calendar live and first posts published. Weeks 3-4: full publishing cadence running, Google Business Profile at three per week, daily moderation sweep with an escalation log, weekly written status. Strong month one leads to the ongoing engagement at the same rate. Ground rules: All work runs through account access we provide; you never post from your own accounts and never move our content through a third-party publishing tool. Nothing publishes outside the approved calendar without written approval on the specific post. Weekly written status, including a simple log of your hours. To apply, answer these four: 1) Which of these platforms have you published on professionally: Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, LinkedIn, Google Business Profile? For each, tell us whether you worked natively in the platform (Meta Business Suite, TikTok, YouTube Studio) or through a third-party tool. 2) Describe a professional services client (legal, medical, financial) you published for. How did approvals work, and what happened when a commenter asked something you couldn't answer publicly? 3) A comment on one of our ads asks about the commenter's own legal situation. Walk us through exactly what you do. 4) Look at any law firm's Facebook or Instagram and tell us one thing you'd fix about how they publish. One paragraph. Include your weekly availability, your overlap with US Pacific business hours, and confirm you have your own paid ChatGPT account or similar for AI image work.

The successful candidate must be able to adapt content naturally for a French audience while preserving technical accuracy, instructional clarity, and industry-specific terminology. ## Key Responsibilities * Translate and localize English-language technical and training content into French (France). * Adapt e-learning materials, presentations, subtitles, on-screen text, assessments, and supporting documentation. * Review and edit localized content to ensure consistency, quality, and adherence to terminology standards. * Collaborate with project managers, instructional designers, and other localization team members to meet tight project deadlines. * Localize images, graphics, and visual assets, including replacing and formatting translated text within images. * Conduct quality assurance reviews to ensure content is linguistically accurate, culturally appropriate, and technically precise. * Identify terminology, context, or localization issues and communicate recommendations to the project team. ## Required Qualifications * Native-level French (France) proficiency with excellent written communication skills. * Professional experience translating and localizing content from English into French (France). * Demonstrated experience with technical, instructional, e-learning, or training content. * Familiarity with terminology related to one or more of the following industries: * Electrical * HVAC * Plumbing * Facilities Maintenance * Construction * Experience working with Google Workspace tools, including: * Google Slides * Google Drive * Shared folder management and organization * Ability to maintain formatting and layouts within presentation files during localization. * Experience localizing images and visual assets. * Basic video editing experience, including updating localized captions, subtitles, and on-screen text. * Strong attention to detail and ability to work effectively with context-heavy content. ## Project Requirements * Must be available to start immediately. * Must be comfortable working under tight deadlines and managing a high-volume workload. * Must be responsive and able to communicate effectively throughout the project. * Must be able to work independently while maintaining high-quality standards. * Previous experience with technical training, e-learning, or skilled trades content is strongly preferred. ## Preferred Qualifications * Experience localizing simulation-based learning content. * Familiarity with localization quality assurance (LQA) processes. * Experience working with terminology databases, style guides, and translation memory tools. * Background in the skilled trades industry or technical education. ## What We're Looking For We are looking for a localization professional who understands the importance of context, technical accuracy, and instructional clarity. The ideal candidate can confidently work with complex training materials, adapt content naturally for a French audience, and consistently deliver high-quality work within a compressed project timeline. That kind of impact doesn’t happen alone; it’s built through disciplined execution, thoughtful decision-making, intentional collaboration, and operational excellence. About the Project: The Legal Agreement & Policy Optimization Project is a strategic cross-functional initiative designed to create a centralized, legally governed framework for all agreements, policies, acknowledgements, disclosures, and related documentation used throughout the family office ecosystem. The project will establish consistency, governance, accountability, and compliance across employment, vendor, applicant, contractor, investment, event, and operational documentation while reducing legal, operational, reputational, and compliance risk. Working closely with internal stakeholders and outside counsel, this role will oversee the review, revision, creation, organization, approval, implementation, training strategy and long-term maintenance associated with these documents. This engagement is anticipated to last approximately 2–3 months, with the possibility of extension or assignment to future projects. Who You Are: - You are an experienced project leader with a strong background managing complex, cross-functional initiatives involving legal, compliance, risk management, HR, operations, and business process improvement. - You excel at bringing structure to ambiguity, coordinating diverse stakeholders, driving accountability, and ensuring projects move forward efficiently. - You understand how to balance legal requirements with practical business operations and can effectively facilitate conversations between attorneys, executives, department leaders, HR, operations, and administrative teams. - You are highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable handling sensitive and confidential information. - Experience supporting UHNW individuals, family offices, legal departments, professional services firms, or highly regulated organizations is strongly preferred. What You’ll Do: Project Leadership & Governance - Lead the Legal Agreement & Policy Optimization Project from initiation through implementation. - Develop and manage project plans, timelines, milestones, dependencies, and deliverables. - Establish project governance, decision-making frameworks, and accountability structures. - Facilitate cross-functional collaboration between Legal, Human Resources, Operations, Business Processes, Finance, Household - Operations, and executive stakeholders. - Ensure project objectives remain aligned with organizational priorities and risk management goals. - Identify and proactively mitigate project risks and roadblocks. - Legal Documentation Optimization - Coordinate with outside counsel throughout the review, revision, and creation of agreements and policies. Ensure documentation reflects: Single Family Office operational realities UHNW principal considerations Domestic and office staff environments California employment laws and applicable federal regulations Multi-entity organizational structures Oversee document inventory, gap analysis, version control, and prioritization efforts. Ensure consistency across agreements, acknowledgements, policies, and procedures. Support alignment of all employment-related documentation with the Employee Handbook as the organization's primary source of truth. Process Design & Implementation - Design standardized review, approval, and maintenance workflows. - Establish document ownership, governance responsibilities, and escalation pathways. - Recommend sustainable processes for future legal and policy updates. - Partner with Business Process and Knowledge Management teams to ensure proper document organization and accessibility. Training & Change Management - Coordinate rollout and communication strategies for finalized documents. - Develop implementation plans and accountability mechanisms. - Ensure employees receive training and acknowledgements for applicable agreements and policies. - Support adoption through communication, documentation, and stakeholder engagement. What You'll Bring: - 5+ years of project management experience leading complex cross-functional initiatives. - Experience working with legal, compliance, HR, operations, or risk management projects. - Proven ability to manage multiple stakeholders and competing priorities. - Strong facilitation, communication, and executive presentation skills. - Experience creating project plans, governance frameworks, and implementation strategies. - Exceptional organizational and follow-through skills. - Strong writing, documentation, and analytical capabilities. - Experience with project management platforms and collaboration tools. - Ability to handle highly sensitive and confidential information. - Experience supporting UHNW family offices, private offices, legal firms, or professional services environments strongly preferred. - PMP, CAPM, Agile, Lean, or related certifications are a plus. Success Metrics: - Complete inventory and assessment of all in-scope agreements and policies. - Successful legal review and standardization of documentation. - Establishment of approved governance and review workflows. - Reduction of documentation inconsistencies and compliance gaps. - Successful implementation and stakeholder adoption. - Completion of employee training and acknowledgement requirements. Physical Requirements/Work Environment: - Prolonged periods of sitting or standing and working on a computer for 8+ hours/day Engagement Details: Contract/Freelance Position Approximately 20–40 hours per week Anticipated duration: 2–3 months Hybrid preferred; occasional meetings will be required during Pacific Time business hours Opportunity for future project engagements upon successful completion

Development of a complete visual identit.t: Development of a complete visual identity for my coaching practice,
Development of a complete visual identit.d: Graphic Designer Brief – Cothú Coaching Project Overview I am seeking a freelance graphic designer to support the development of a complete visual identity for my coaching practice, Cothú Coaching. The name Cothú comes from the Irish language and speaks to the ideas of nurturing, cultivating, and sustaining growth. It reflects a belief that lasting development is not something that can be rushed or imposed, but something that grows through reflection, understanding, and purposeful action. Cothú Coaching provides a supportive space for individuals and professionals to think deeply, explore possibilities, and build the confidence and clarity needed to navigate change and realise meaningful, sustainable change and growth. The brand should feel authentic, professional, calm, human, and grounded rather than corporate, transactional, or overly "self-help" focused. The primary audience includes professionals, educators, leaders, individuals navigating change or transition, and people seeking personal or professional development through coaching. ________________________________________ Brand Values The visual identity should reflect the following core values: • Growth • Development • Learning • Reflection • Authenticity • Sustainability • Wellbeing • Trust • Connection • Empowerment The brand is heavily influenced by the interconnected Irish concepts of: • Cothú (Nurturing) • Athrú (Change) • Fás (Growth) • Foghlaim (Learning) • Forbairt (Development) ________________________________________ Desired Brand Personality The brand should feel: Professional but approachable Credible and trustworthy without feeling overly corporate. Calm but optimistic Reflecting growth and possibility without becoming clichéd or overly energetic. Contemporary but timeless Modern enough to appeal to professionals while avoiding trends that may quickly date. Irish-rooted but not stereotypically Irish Subtle cultural influence is welcome, but I want to avoid clichés such as shamrocks, leprechaun imagery, Celtic knot overload, etc. ________________________________________ Visual Direction Inspiration Themes Potential visual themes include: • Nature and organic growth • Trees and root systems • Rings of growth • Seeds and cultivation • Pathways and journeys • Circles and interconnected systems • Flow and movement • Balance and sustainability • Subtle Celtic influences through form rather than symbolism Visual Style I am drawn towards: • Clean, minimalist design • Organic shapes • Soft textures • Simple but meaningful symbolism • High-quality contemporary branding • Elegant use of white space Examples of brands whose aesthetic appeals to me include wellness, education, leadership, and sustainability-focused organisations rather than corporate consulting firms. ________________________________________ Colour Palette Direction Current thinking is centred around: Primary Colours • Deep Forest Green • Sage Green • Moss Green Secondary Colours • Warm Stone • Soft Sand • Cream • Warm Grey Accent Colours • Muted Copper • Terracotta • Soft Gold (used sparingly) The palette should communicate growth, grounding, warmth, and professionalism. I would welcome alternative suggestions that align with the overall brand values. ________________________________________ Typography The typography should communicate: • Professionalism • Warmth • Readability • Simplicity Potential direction: • Elegant serif paired with a clean sans-serif • Modern humanist fonts • Fonts suitable for web, social media, presentations, and printed materials ________________________________________ Deliverables Required Brand Identity • Primary logo • Secondary logo variations • Icon/brand mark • Favicon/social media icon Brand System • Colour palette with HEX, RGB and CMYK values • Typography recommendations • Brand guidelines document Visual Assets • Graphic elements and patterns • Icons • Background textures • Social media graphics/templates • Website visual direction Social Media Assets Templates for: • Quotes • Reflections • Educational content • Promotional posts • Testimonials Website Assets Visual concepts for: • Homepage • About page • Services page • Contact page ________________________________________ Practical Requirements The branding must work effectively across: • Website • Instagram • LinkedIn • Facebook • Presentation slides • Workshop materials • PDFs and reports • Business cards • Pull-up banners and printed materials ________________________________________ Audience The ideal client may include: • Teachers and school leaders • Educational professionals • Individuals experiencing career transition • Emerging leaders • Professionals seeking greater fulfilment and clarity • People interested in personal growth and reflective practice Typically adults aged 30–60 who value authenticity, learning, and meaningful development. ________________________________________ What I Want People to Feel When someone encounters the Cothú Coaching brand, I would like them to feel: "I am in a safe, supportive and professional space where I can reflect, grow, and move forward with confidence." The visual identity should evoke: • Trust • Calm confidence • Growth • Possibility • Reflection • Human connection • Authentic development ________________________________________ Please provide: • Portfolio examples • Proposed process and timeline • Estimated costs • Number of concepts/revisions included • Final file formats supplied ________________________________________ Summary Statement Cothú Coaching is not about fixing people. It is about creating the conditions in which people can nurture their own growth, navigate change, deepen learning, and realise their potential. The visual identity should embody this philosophy through a brand that is calm, authentic, professional, and deeply rooted in the concept of sustainable development and growth. ________________________________________ Optional Additional Deliverable: Brand Introduction Video I would be interested in discussing the creation of a short animated brand video that introduces the ethos and purpose of Cothú Coaching. Purpose The video would be used to: • Introduce the Cothú Coaching brand • Communicate the meaning of "Cothú" • Explain the philosophy underpinning the coaching practice • Build awareness and trust with prospective clients • Support social media marketing and website engagement Style The video should feel: • Calm and reflective • Professional and authentic • Warm and human • Visually aligned with the overall brand identity I am drawn towards a simple, elegant motion-graphics approach rather than highly polished corporate advertising. Possible Themes Visual storytelling could explore concepts such as: • Seeds becoming growth • Tree roots and branches • Pathways and journeys • Cycles of learning and development • Connection between reflection, change and growth • The progression from potential to purposeful action Suggested Length • 30–60 second social media version • Optional extended version (60–90 seconds) for website use Intended Platforms • Instagram • LinkedIn • Facebook • Website homepage • Presentation and workshop introductions Potential Narrative The video does not need to be heavily promotional. Instead, it should communicate the underlying philosophy of the brand: Growth is rarely instant. It is nurtured through reflection, learning, challenge, support and purposeful action. Cothú Coaching creates the conditions for individuals to explore change, cultivate their strengths and move forward with confidence. Deliverables • Vertical format (Instagram Reels/Stories) • Square format (social posts) • Landscape format (website and presentations) • Editable source files where possible

China-Based Apparel Sourcing Support - O.d: I run a UK-based custom sportswear and merch brand working with teams, clubs and communities. We are looking for China-based support to help us find and vet better factories for cotton jersey / lifestyle garments. Sportswear is part of our business, but the main focus for this project is improving our cotton and lifestyle product options. We mainly work by finding strong base products, then customising them for different clubs and teams through print, branding, labels and small fit tweaks. We are not looking to develop completely new products from scratch. We are mainly looking for good ODM factories with existing blanks/templates that we can slightly adjust and repeat across different designs. Products we are interested in include: * Cotton jersey garments * T-shirts * Hoodies * Tracksuits / lifestyle sets * Other streetwear-style blanks * Potentially caps or merch accessories later The right factories need to: * Accept low MOQs, ideally around 10–20 units per design/team * Offer good quality existing products/templates * Allow small tweaks to fit, labels, print and branding * Be suitable for repeat orders across different clubs and designs * Communicate clearly and quickly in English - Ideally whatsapp * Be open to long-term work * Offer better quality than basic promo merchandise We are aiming for a standard/streetwear feel rather than cheap basic blanks. For this first trial, we mainly need help finding and vetting factories. If it goes well, there may be more work later helping with samples, production coordination or QC, but the first priority is supplier research and recommendation. What we need from you: * Find suitable ODM factories in China * Speak to/check factories in Chinese * Confirm MOQ, pricing, sample cost, lead time and customisation options * Collect example product images from the factory * Compare the strongest options * Recommend the best factories for our needs * Introduce us to the best options so we can continue direct conversations The ideal output would be a clear recommendation sheet including: * Factory name/contact * Location * Product examples/images * MOQ * Price range (inc shipping) * Sample cost * Lead time (production & sample and inc shipping) * Customisation options * Communication quality * Why you recommend or do not recommend them * Best next steps We would like to start with a small hourly trial around 1–2 product categories first, likely focused on cotton jersey / lifestyle garments. Please include in your application: 1. Your experience sourcing apparel factories in China 2. Whether you have worked with ODM clothing factories before 3. Whether you have experience with cotton jersey, tees, hoodies, tracksuits or streetwear blanks 4. How you would approach finding factories for low-MOQ repeatable products 5. How many hours you think a useful first trial would take 6. Confirmation that you can provide example product images and factory comparison details, not just Alibaba links We are looking for someone practical, clear and factory-facing who can help us find proper long-term production partners. Thanks!

This is not a simple theme swap; we need a professional who understands how to safely migrate a high-traffic site, structure Custom Post Types, and build an ad-sales infrastructure. Scope of Work: Backend Cleanup: Audit the existing site and remove bloated, outdated, or unused plugins to improve site speed and security. UI/UX Redesign: Design and develop a modern, professional, and visually appealing site that fits the romance genre while remaining clean and easy to navigate. Book Catalog Integration: Build a structured book catalog using Custom Post Types (CPTs) and Custom Taxonomies so our books can be easily sorted by author, genre, trope, etc. Ad Infrastructure Setup: Integrate and configure an ad management system (such as Advanced Ads, AdSanity, or AdRotate) to allow us to sell and manage advertising/promotional placements for other romance authors. Staging & Migration: All development must be done on a staging environment. The live site must remain fully operational until the new site is approved and ready to deploy. SEO Preservation: Implement proper 301 redirects and preserve our existing permalink structures to ensure we do not lose our current search engine rankings. Required Skills & Qualifications: Proven experience as both a UI/UX Designer and an advanced WordPress Developer. Deep knowledge of Custom Post Types (CPTs), Custom Fields (ACF), Elementor, and Taxonomies. Experience setting up ad management plugins for direct ad sales. Strong understanding of SEO best practices and traffic preservation during site migrations. To Apply, Please Answer the Following: Please share 2-3 links to WordPress sites you have both designed and developed (preferably in the publishing, e-commerce, or entertainment space). Briefly explain your process for ensuring a site with 15k monthly visits does not lose its SEO rankings during a redesign. What plugin or method would you recommend for setting up our direct ad-sales and promotional placements, and why? Confirm that you are able to build and test this project entirely on a staging site before pushing it live.

This is a PART-TIME role starting at $3/hour. If you are not comfortable with this rate, please do not apply. I prefer someone interested in LONG-TERM work and growing with the team over time. BOOK DETAILS (Main Starting Task) Usually takes about 1 hour or less. Tasks may include: * Book titles & subtitles * Table of Contents (TOC) * Expanded TOC * Image descriptions/details * Book structure organization I will provide: * Video training * Prompts * Previous book examples * Formatting guidance 2. BOOK CONTENT (Additional Work) If you perform well, I may assign: * Introduction * Chapter 1 * Conclusion * Appendices Later, I may also train you for: * Different nonfiction book styles * AI publishing workflows * AI image generation (vector illustration style) WHAT I’M LOOKING FOR You must be: * VERY detail-oriented * Able to follow instructions exactly * Good at learning quickly * Careful with formatting, style, and word counts * Able to match examples closely without sounding robotic * Comfortable using ChatGPT for work * Interested in long-term work You MUST carefully pay attention to: * ALL video training * ALL prompts * ALL examples * ALL formatting instructions * ALL word count requirements The final work should closely match my examples and brand style. However, I do NOT want robotic copy-paste writing. You should follow the formula and structure while naturally adjusting wording based on the specific book topic. You should also have enough judgment and flexibility to make smart adjustments when needed while still following my system. If I constantly have to fix repeated mistakes or repeat instructions over and over again, this job will NOT be a good fit. Second Paid Test (1 Hour) You may work on: * Intro * Chapter 1 * Conclusion * Appendices Strong performers may receive: * Ongoing book projects * Long-term work * Additional training * More responsibilities over time LONG-TERM OPPORTUNITY I start with simpler books first. Once you consistently produce high-quality work, I can train you on: * Different nonfiction book styles * Advanced AI publishing workflows * AI-generated vector illustration images for books I’m looking for someone reliable, coachable, detail-oriented, and interested in growing with the team long term. WORK RELATIONSHIP I prefer team members to call me “Sir” as part of maintaining a professional employer-employee relationship, as the rest of my team does. Framer Website Designer Needed for Home.t: Framer Website Designer Needed for Home Pickup & Recycling Service
Framer Website Designer Needed for Home.d: # Framer Website Designer Needed – Goodbye Stuff I'm looking for an experienced Framer designer/developer to help turn my existing website concept into a polished, professional website ready for launch. I currently have a homepage draft built from a Framer template, along with completed branding, logo design, page structure, and written copy. The existing draft is intended to communicate the concept and content—not to dictate the final design. I am completely open to redesigning the website from scratch if you believe a different layout, structure, or visual approach would create a stronger user experience and better represent the brand. My goal is to work with someone who has a strong eye for design, branding, and user experience, and can transform the concept into a memorable, consumer-facing website that feels modern, trustworthy, and fun. ## About the Business Goodbye Stuff is a for-profit recycling company that provides free pickup of reusable items such as clothing, shoes, household goods, electronics, books, toys, and small furniture. Our mission is simple: **Declutter. Recycle. Make Impact.** We help keep reusable items out of landfills by connecting households with reuse, resale, and recycling channels. ##Background I have worked in the clothing recycling, reuse, and resale industry for several years through my work with another similar company Goods Recycling, a nonprofit organization that operates clothing collection bins, recycling programs, and online resale operations throughout the Washington, DC metropolitan area. Goodbye Stuff is a separate brand being developed to make it easier for households to schedule pickups of reusable items while helping keep valuable materials out of landfills. The business concept, operations, and industry side are already well understood. My background is in recycling, resale, and operations—not web design or development. I have a clear vision for the brand and customer experience, but I need someone with strong design and Framer expertise to help bring that vision to life and create a professional, memorable website. ## Current Status I already have: • Brand name and logo completed • Color palette established • Homepage drafted in Framer • Written copy completed for all pages • Basic website structure planned I am not looking for someone to create the business concept or write the content. I need someone who can take what already exists and elevate it into a professional consumer-facing website. ## Pages • Home • About • What We Accept • FAQ • Service Area • Contact • Schedule a Pickup For the Schedule a Pickup page, I only need the page layout, design, and overall user experience created at this stage. The actual pickup request form, backend functionality, automation, and scheduling system will be developed separately in a future phase. For now, I am simply looking for a professional, conversion-focused page design with placeholder form elements and clear calls-to-action that fit the overall website design. The About page will include sections such as: • Making Recycling Easier • Goodbye, Not Goodwill • Stuff, Not Junk • Our Network • Partnership Opportunities • Our Vision ## Project Scope I need help with: • Overall visual design and branding consistency • Professional page layouts • Typography and spacing improvements • Mobile responsiveness • Navigation and user experience improvements • Icons, graphics, illustrations, and imagery recommendations • Framer implementation and polish • Basic SEO setup • Calls-to-action and conversion optimization ## Important At this stage I am NOT looking for: • Custom software development • Scheduling systems • Pickup logistics tools • CRM integrations • Mobile app development The Schedule Pickup button and Schedule Pickup page can simply be placeholders for now. The goal of this project is to create a professional, trustworthy, modern website that is ready for launch while the pickup system is developed separately. ## Design Direction I do not want a typical recycling, charity, or nonprofit website. Most competitors in this space feel outdated, corporate, or overly focused on donations. I want Goodbye Stuff to feel modern, friendly, memorable, and visually engaging. And heavily focused on recycling and reuse rather than "donation". The brand is built around our mascot, **Bagsy**, and I would like the website to lean heavily into that personality. The site should feel approachable and fun while still being professional and trustworthy. I envision something that combines: • The friendliness and personality of Duolingo • The ease of use of 1-800-GOT-JUNK • The environmental mission of a modern recycling company I would like to use custom illustrations, mascot graphics, icons, and playful visual elements throughout the website rather than relying solely on stock photography. Examples of similar businesses: https://www.gogreendrop.com/ https://projectbgreen.com/ https://pickupplease.org/ While these companies provide a useful reference for the business model, I believe the visual design can be significantly more engaging and memorable. My goal is to create a brand that people remember and enjoy interacting with—not just another recycling website. We all provide the same "service" but I want goodbye stuff to stand out as something different. I am willing to give the designer creative freedom if they believe a different layout, page structure, or visual approach would improve the website. ## Deliverables • Fully polished Framer website • Mobile and desktop optimized • Consistent design system throughout all pages • Ready for launch ## When Applying Please include: • Framer portfolio • Examples of service-based websites you've built • Estimated timeline • Fixed-price quote I will provide access to the current Framer project, branding assets, logo files, website copy, and existing design work. My biggest challenge is that I'm not a designer. I have the business concept, content, and structure figured out, but I need someone with a strong eye for design and user experience to make the website look professional, trustworthy, and memorable.

Logos and favicon for open-source app.d: I'm seeking for an artist, illustrator, logo designer to help me with the creation of a logo for an open-source, free-to-use app that I have/am built/building. The app is an RSS reader and aggregator (more info on what this means here[1]), which helps users organize news and events from multiple websites, on a single platform. The app is called Wyrm, which can have a double meaning. One is the fantasy/folklore dragon-like creature: https://rpg.stackexchange.com/questions/55698/what-is-a-wyrm-in-dd Another one is it can be an acronym for What You Read Matters, which I think makes sense given what the app does. What I need is the following: - A logo for the app's GitHub README page that includes the app name and acronym. - A smaller version of the logo (just the wyrm itself) for use within the app. - A favicon (16x16/32x32) for the website. The app doesn't really have a "color identity". Apart from light and dark mode, users are also able to pick different colors within the app. Therefore, I think the logo should have a more neutral color. I would also like give artists the freedom to create and use their creativity. The style of the art could be more detailed and "realistic" or more cartoonish. I can send screenshots of the app in private. [1] https://rss.com/blog/how-do-rss-feeds-work/

Performance Video Editor (Meta & TikTok.t: Performance Video Editor (Meta & TikTok Ads) - DTC eCommerce - Full-Time, Long-Term
Performance Video Editor (Meta & TikTok.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, 30+ hrs/week
Performance Video Editor (Meta & TikTok.d: JOB DESCRIPTION: We are a fast-growing European direct-to-consumer brand in the women's intimate apparel space (leakproof underwear & swimwear for menstruation and bladder leakage). We sell through paid social and are scaling our ad creative output. We are looking for one full-time performance video editor to own our Meta ad creative now and our TikTok creative as we launch there. This is not a wedding/YouTube/corporate editing role. This is direct-response ad editing: UGC-style ads, hooks, captions, fast iteration, and editing with conversion as the only goal. What you'll do - Edit UGC-style and static-motion video ads for Meta (and soon TikTok) from raw footage + a written brief. - Build scroll-stopping hooks (first 3 seconds), tight pacing, pattern interrupts, b-roll, and clean burned-in captions. - Produce AI-generated and animation-based ad creative — combining AI tools and motion/animation to build ads that don't rely only on live footage. - Produce multiple variations per concept for testing (hook swaps, different intros, ratios 9:16 / 4:5 / 1:1). - Sync captions and edits to Dutch and English voice-overs — including Dutch, which you will not speak (you match exactly to a provided script, frame by frame). - Iterate quickly based on performance feedback (hook rate, hold rate, CTR). - Deliver 2-4+ video ads per day. Skills & Experience - Proven experience editing paid Meta and/or TikTok ad videos — with examples you personally edited. - A clear eye for conversion: you understand hooks, retention, and why an ad performs, not just "nice cuts." - Hands-on experience with AI creative tools (e.g. AI video/image generation, AI voice, AI b-roll) and a sense of how to use them to produce ads faster and at scale. - Ability to create animation ads — motion graphics, animated text, kinetic typography, and animated explainer-style creative. - Daily fluency in CapCut and/or Adobe Premiere Pro (After Effects strongly preferred for animation work). - Experience with UGC ad editing (captions, zooms, sound effects, fast pacing). - Experience editing in a language you don't speak (matching captions/timing exactly to a script). - Strong written English for understanding briefs and communicating. - Reliable, organized, and fast at iterating — you hit deadlines without dropping quality. Must-haves (do not apply without these) - Proven paid Meta and/or TikTok ad editing experience, with a portfolio. - A genuine eye for conversion. - Working knowledge of AI creative tools and the ability to make animation ads. - Daily fluency in CapCut and/or Adobe Premiere Pro (After Effects is a plus). - Experience with UGC/VSL ad editing. - Strong written English. - Full-time availability with at least 4 hours of overlap with Amsterdam time (CET). - Reliable computer + stable, fast internet for downloading/uploading large video files. Nice to have - Experience editing ads for women's / intimate / health / beauty / wellness products. - Advanced motion graphics / After Effects skills. - Experience scaling ad creative with AI workflows. What We Offer - A stable, full-time, long-term role with a growing international brand - not one-off projects. - Competitive monthly salary: 800 USD, based on experience and proven ad results. - Performance bonuses when your ad creative becomes a winning, scaled ad. - Clear, detailed written briefs, you never have to guess what to make. - Fast feedback loops and direct communication with the team (no agency layers). - The chance to grow into a lead creative role as we scale across Meta and TikTok. - 100% remote, working your own hours as long as you overlap ≥ 4 hours with Amsterdam time (CET). Logistics - Type: Full-time, long-term (this is a stable, ongoing role — not a one-off project). - Salary: 800 USD, or tell us your expectation. - Start: asap Comfort note: Our product is about menstruation, bladder leakage and the female body. The content is always tasteful and professional, but you must be fully comfortable editing creative on these topics. If that is not for you, please don't apply. HOW TO APPLY — read carefully (applications that skip steps are deleted) This is a two-step process: Step 1 — Application To prove you actually read this, start your message with the exact words: "Hooks that convert." Then answer all of the following: - Share 3 links to Meta/TikTok ad videos you personally edited. State exactly what your role was on each. - Which brands and niches have you edited paid ads for? Have you worked on women's, intimate, health or beauty products? - What editing software do you use every day? - Which AI creative tools have you used, and what did you use them for? Share an example of an AI-assisted or animated ad you made. - Realistically, how many high-quality ad variations can you deliver per week? - Explain in 2–3 sentences how you approach the first 3 seconds of a paid ad. Give a concrete example. - Have you ever edited a video in a language you don't speak? How did you make sure the translations, captions and timing were accurate? - Confirm: are you comfortable editing content about menstruation, bladder leakage and the female body? - Your full-time availability. - Your internet speed (Mbps) and computer specs. - Your expected monthly salary in USD. Applications without portfolio links will not be considered. Step 2 — Test video edit Shortlisted candidates will receive raw footage and b-roll plus a short brief, and will be asked to edit one short ad video from it. This shows us directly how you handle a real edit, your hook, pacing, captions and conversion instinct. The test is paid and is intentionally small (one short ad, not a batch). Only complete Step 1 first, we'll invite the strongest applicants to Step 2.

We are looking for someone who can take vendor-provided product information and turn it into polished, accurate, well-structured Shopify product pages using a mix of manual work and AI-assisted writing. This is not a simple copy/paste product upload job. Our products are nuanced, often expensive, and require good judgment. We need someone who can understand product information, organize it clearly, and create pages that feel appropriate for a premium design retailer. What You’ll Be Doing You will create new product pages in Shopify using information from vendor websites, catalogs, PDFs, spreadsheets, images, and other product materials. Tasks may include: * Creating new Shopify product pages * Using AI tools to draft polished product descriptions from vendor-provided information * Editing AI-generated copy so it sounds natural, accurate, and appropriate for a high-end furniture/design retailer * Structuring product specs, dimensions, materials, finishes, care information, lead times, and shipping details * Creating and organizing product variants/options * Entering SKUs, prices, tags, vendor information, product types, collections, and other Shopify fields * Uploading and organizing product images * Making sure product pages are accurate, consistent, and customer-friendly * Identifying missing, inconsistent, or confusing vendor information * Asking smart questions when needed, but also making reasonable judgment calls when appropriate * Potentially communicating with vendors to request missing product details * Helping improve our product upload process over time * Using Matrixify or CSV imports if helpful, though most work will likely be done directly in Shopify What We’re Looking For The ideal candidate has strong Shopify experience, excellent English, and a good understanding of US ecommerce standards. Experience with furniture, lighting, home decor, interior design, or premium retail is strongly preferred. We are especially looking for someone who is: * Detail-oriented * Organized * Comfortable working independently * Able to use AI tools thoughtfully and efficiently * A strong English writer * Familiar with Shopify product page creation * Comfortable working with product variants, specs, dimensions, materials, finishes, and images * Able to understand what information matters to a customer buying premium furniture or lighting online * Able to spot inconsistencies or missing information * Comfortable making reasonable inferences instead of needing hand-holding for every small decision * Interested in improving and streamlining manual processes over time Preferred Qualifications * Shopify product page experience * Experience with furniture, lighting, home decor, luxury retail, design, or architecture/interiors * Familiarity with US ecommerce expectations * Experience using ChatGPT or other AI tools for ecommerce content * Experience with Matrixify, CSV imports, spreadsheets, or bulk Shopify editing * Basic SEO understanding * Experience communicating with vendors or suppliers Important: This Role Requires Judgment Many of our products are complex. A single product may have multiple sizes, finishes, upholstery options, bulb requirements, lead times, materials, technical specs, and image variations. Vendor information is often incomplete or inconsistent. We need someone who can look at the available information and create a product page that makes sense. You should be able to think through questions like: * Is this product description accurate and polished? * Are the variants organized in a logical way? * Are the specs complete and easy to understand? * Do the product images match the available options? * Does anything look inconsistent or incorrect? * What information is missing? * Can this be solved with the available information, or does it need to be escalated? About Us We are a curated online retailer specializing in authentic modern furniture, lighting, and home decor. We work with premium design brands and care deeply about accuracy, presentation, and customer experience. Our product pages need to feel polished, trustworthy, and appropriate for customers shopping for high-end design products. Location / English Requirements US-based candidates are preferred because familiarity with the US furniture, design, and ecommerce market is helpful. However, we are open to exceptional international candidates with near-native English and strong experience with US ecommerce. Budget We are open to hourly rates based on experience. Please include your hourly rate in your application. To Apply Please include: 1. A brief summary of your Shopify/ecommerce experience 2. Any experience you have with furniture, lighting, home decor, interior design, or premium retail 3. Examples of product pages you have created or improved, if available 4. A short explanation of how you would use AI tools to help create product pages without sacrificing quality or accuracy 5. Your hourly rate 6. Your weekly availability 7. Whether you are comfortable doing a small paid trial project We will likely start with a small paid trial assignment. If the work is strong, this can become an ongoing role.

Senior Engineer, Publisher Operations (P.t: Senior Engineer, Publisher Operations (Part-or-Full Time, Remote, AI-Augmented)
Senior Engineer, Publisher Operations (P.i: Hourly: $25.00 - $50.00 Expert Est. time: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/w
Senior Engineer, Publisher Operations (P.d: Senior Engineer, Publisher Operations (Part-or-Full Time, Remote, AI-Augmented) We run a portfolio of digital news and content properties. We are hiring a senior engineer to own the technical operations layer of the web-publishing operation - the WordPress theme builds, website maintenance, the server fleet, ads.txt changes, ad-code placements, the CloudFlare system, etc. This is a part-or-full time, remote, 1099 / contractor / EOR-flexible role. We pay competitively for senior engineering experience. We work in an AI-first operating mode: agentic tooling is not a nice-to-have, it is how we ship. We are open to part-time that becomes full-time or starting at full-time, depending on candidate strength and proven track record. WHAT YOU WILL OWN Day-one scope is the tactical operations layer of a multi-brand publisher: - WordPress production work across roughly 20 brand sites (themes, ACF, Yoast, custom plugins) - Cloudflare at fleet scale (DNS, WAF rules, bot defense, IVT remediation rollouts, rule deployment against a designed architecture) - Ad-stack execution across multiple ad networks (ads.txt, sellers.json, publisher_id hygiene, vendor ad-code deployment, in-email ad code) - Server fleet operations on Linux hosts (SSH, nginx, log analysis, deployment tooling, Linode + Forge) - Vendor email coordination with ad-network reps (technical context, ad-code shipping, status follow-up) - Tactical revenue-per-thousand work (placement audits, fill-rate diagnostics, before/after evidence packages) - Internal operational reporting on what shipped, what is in flight, and what is blocked The work is wide-surface, not deep-single-stack. You will touch many properties in any given week, not one product. WHAT YOU GROW INTO Once the tactical backlog stabilizes, the role expands into the engineering-tools and AI-agentic-systems lane in collaboration with our extremely experienced fractional CTO and our growth lead. Concrete examples: - Building scheduled-agent and MCP-style tooling for internal workflow automation - Helping ship internal publisher tools (newsletter assembly, fraud-detection substrate, lead pipelines) - Sharing the architecture work on traffic-integrity systems as they mature This is a structural senior role on a small team where AI leverage decides what one person can ship. REQUIRED - Active daily use of AI development tooling. Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, or equivalent, in your current workflow. You should be able to walk us through how you use agentic tools to ship in your interview. You must actively utilize AI systems in your daily workflow. - 5+ years of professional engineering experience, with a meaningful slice in web operations, publisher operations, or ad-tech. - Familiarity with Cloudflare (DNS at minimum, WAF or workers a plus). Depth builds on the job under our CTO. - Comfortable on a Linux server fleet over SSH. nginx, log analysis, basic systemd, cron, ufw, ssh-key hygiene. - Strong written English for vendor email and internal documentation. - Ability to attend a daily 9:30am Central Time video standup. Time zones that overlap meaningfully with US business hours preferred. - Pattern of taking ownership of a wide surface and shipping without architectural hand-holding on every task. STRONGLY PREFERRED - WordPress depth: theme dev, ACF, custom plugin work, performance and SEO posture. - Ad-tech operational exposure: ads.txt / sellers.json, publisher_id structure, vendor ad-code deployment, AdSense / programmatic / native ad networks from the publisher side. - IVT / bot-defense or ad-fraud forensics experience: reading Cloudflare firewall events, ASN-level traffic patterns, distinguishing real human traffic from datacenter / commercial-crawler / headless-browser patterns. - Experience working under a senior architect where you execute on architecture you did not design. - History of shipping internal tools or automations that compressed your own role. THE SHAPE OF THE ENGAGEMENT - If Part-time, 15 to 25 hours per week, flexible week-by-week. Some weeks land at the upper end during rollouts or crises; some weeks at the lower end during steady state. - If Full-time, 40+ hours per week, set hours other than emergencies. - Remote. Time-zone overlap with US Central is required for standup; otherwise async-first. - Engagement structure flexible. 1099 (US), agency placement, or EOR (Deel / Remote / Oyster) for non-US. We are open to whichever shape works on your side. - Reporting line. Daily work direction from the CEO at standup. Senior technical collaboration and architecture review from our CTO. Daily stand-down to review bottlenecks and status. - We heavily use AI for task management, project management, etc. Proficiency in building extreme context for your AI-enhanced engineering suite of tools and workflows is fundamental. WHAT WE ARE NOT LOOKING FOR - Engineers who treat AI tooling as an experiment rather than a daily driver - Architecturally restless candidates looking to redesign systems they have not yet operated - Engineers whose recent work was at companies adversarial to our editorial posture (we will share the editorial posture at first-call stage) - Rigid workers who can’t be flexible if the situation requires it - Engineers who need someone to teach them anything “new” - this is a self-education oriented position and culture. HOW TO APPLY Reply with: 1. A short note on how AI development tooling has changed how you ship in the last 12 months. One paragraph, concrete, no marketing language. 2. Resume or a public profile that captures recent work. 3. Two or three links to work you would point to as representative of the surface above (ad-stack, CF, publisher tools, internal automations, anything in the neighborhood). 4. Your time zone and your earliest realistic start window. We screen within a week. Technical screen runs with our CTO. Final conversation with the CEO. Decision and offer within two weeks of first reply for candidates who clear screening.

when needed. * Help maintain clean documentation in project management and ticketing systems. ### QA & Process Improvement * Test website changes across desktop and mobile devices. * Review forms, checkout flows, inventory pages, navigation, search, and key conversion paths. * Identify recurring support issues and recommend process improvements. * Help create or improve internal documentation, support guides, and client-facing instructions. * Contribute to better support workflows, escalation processes, and quality standards. ## Required Experience * 4+ years of experience supporting WordPress websites. * Strong hands-on experience with WooCommerce. * Experience troubleshooting plugins, themes, forms, redirects, and website performance issues. * Familiarity with HTML, CSS, basic PHP, and browser developer tools. * Experience working in a ticketing system such as Freshdesk, Zendesk, Jira, Asana, ClickUp, or similar. * Ability to write clear internal notes, client updates, bug WooCommerce payment gateways, shipping plugins, tax settings, and order workflows. * Familiarity with hosting environments, DNS, SSL, caching, CDN, backups, and staging sites. * Experience supporting dealership, inventory, parts, equipment, or automotive websites. * Basic understanding of SEO, analytics, redirects, and website accessibility. * Experience managing client-facing website projects or support retainers. * Familiarity with APIs, integrations, feeds, or third-party systems is a plus. ## Skills & Traits * Strong problem-solving ability. * Excellent written and verbal communication. * Calm and professional under pressure. * Detail-oriented and organized. * Comfortable owning issues from start to finish. * Able to balance urgency, quality, and client expectations. * Technically curious and willing to keep learning. * Team-oriented, dependable, and proactive. ## Success in This Role Looks Like * Client issues are resolved quickly and clearly. * Developers receive well-documented tickets with minimal back-and-forth. * WooCommerce and WordPress problems are diagnosed accurately. * Projects and support requests stay organized and on track. * Clients feel heard, informed, and taken care of. * Recurring problems are identified and turned into better processes. * The support team becomes more efficient because of your leadership and technical judgment. ## WordPress and WooCommerce experience, and examples of websites or projects you have supported.

Frontend Specialist for AI Automated wor.i: Hourly: $15.00 - $30.00 Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30
Frontend Specialist for AI Automated wor.d: Overview I publish presentation / landing-page-style decks online and need a clean, reliable way for non-technical colleagues and clients to edit them, mainly text and images, without touching code or breaking the layout. Edits should save to a single shared version so everyone sees the same up-to-date page live. Before any building happens, I want an expert to recommend the best approach. Scope of This Engagement This is consultation-first. The initial deliverable is your expert recommendation on the right architecture and tooling for the goal above, along with the trade-offs. If your recommendation is a good fit, there's a strong possibility of a follow-on build engagement to implement it, but the first priority is sound advice, not code. What I'd Like You to Advise On The best overall approach for making published, landing-page-style web pages editable by non-technical people. How to let users edit content only (text and images) while keeping the design, layout, and styling locked so they can't break it. Where the editable content should be stored, and how a single shared "source of truth" is maintained so everyone sees the same version. How edits get published and go live (instant vs. review/approval, draft vs. published states). Realistic cost: setup, any licensing/subscription fees, and ongoing running costs. Ongoing maintenance and reliability: how much upkeep this needs and who can do it. WordPress is one option I'm considering, but I'm open to others. Compare WordPress against the alternatives (e.g. headless CMS, inline/visual editing tools, page builders, SaaS deck/site tools, static sites with a lightweight editor) and tell me what you'd choose and why. Required Skills & Experience Strong front-end background (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and an eye for keeping published pages pixel-stable. Hands-on experience building or configuring systems where non-technical users edit live web content. Working knowledge of multiple CMS / editing approaches, including WordPress and at least one headless or inline-editing alternative. Solid grasp of content storage, publishing workflows, and "single shared version" mechanics. Ability to weigh cost, maintenance, and ease-of-use trade-offs and explain them in plain language. Clear written and verbal communication; comfortable giving a straight recommendation. Nice-to-Have Experience with non-technical client handover and writing simple editing guides or documentation. Familiarity with image handling/optimization for web (uploads, sizing, performance). Exposure to access control / user permissions for editors. A portfolio of live sites or decks that non-technical people actively maintain themselves.

Urgent: Proofreader/Editor for WWII Hist.t: Urgent: Proofreader/Editor for WWII History Project
Urgent: Proofreader/Editor for WWII Hist.d: Job Description: We are seeking an experienced publishing professional to perform a strict error-correction pass on a high school student's project book focusing on the Doolittle Raid and the subsequent China rescue during World War II. Important Note: We are not looking for stylistic rewriting, developmental editing, or polishing. The primary goal is to correct objective errors while preserving the student's original voice and narrative structure. Project Scope & Structure: This project consists of two distinct phases spaced a few weeks apart. Phase 1: Strict Text Error Correction (Immediate) Task: Correct all errors in grammar, punctuation, spelling, and typos in the raw manuscript (~13,300 words). Ensure consistency in historical names and terms. Timeline: Strict 3-day turnaround from the official hire date. Phase 2: Final PDF Page Proof Review (Approx. 20+ days later) Task: A final, quick proofreading pass on the designed page proofs (PDF layout) to check for formatting, and to ensure the 20+ illustrations and captions are correctly aligned and error-free before going to print. Timeline: This will happen at least 20 days later once the book layout is complete. (Turnaround for this final pass: 24–48 hours upon receiving the PDF). Budget & Milestones ($450 Fixed Price): The total budget will be split into two Upwork milestones to reflect the timeline: Milestone 1 ($350): Released upon successful delivery of the clean Phase 1 manuscript within the 3-day deadline. Milestone 2 ($100): Activated and released upon completion of the final PDF page proof review (20+ days later). Required Qualifications: 5+ years of experience in the book publishing industry. An eagle eye for typos, grammar, and punctuation mistakes. Familiarity with standard publishing style guides (e.g., Chicago Manual of Style) and final layout inspection. Immediate availability for Phase 1 and highly responsive communication. If you are a detail-oriented professional who can deliver clean, accurate results quickly and is willing to sync back up for the final print check in a few weeks, please apply. Please briefly state your availability for both phases in your proposal.

Logo and small website design (2-3 pages.d: Hi there. I am a solo esthetician who will be opening a brand new business within a salon soon, so I'm looking for a short (maybe 4 pages) website as well as a new logo. The site will contain a homepage, a bio, a list of services, a page where folks can purchase products, and likely some stock photos spread throughout, although I do have a few of my own pics we can use, too. Obviously the site needs to have an e-commerce element since I'll be selling products locally and they'll be available for shipment, too. I also need be sure my website is SEO optimized so people can find me easier as there's a lot of competition where I live. I'm looking for it to be designed in Square, if possible, as that's easy for me to edit as necessary in the future. My colors are gold, black, white, and gray. I have several gold accents/decorations and my blankets are gray, but the whole salon is black and white. I don't want my colors/aesthetic to be ONLY black and white. My site and logo just need to look like they "fit in" with the salon's b&w ambiance. I'd just like my stuff to have a bit more warmth and "emotion" to it. Does that make sense? I'm anticipating this project could be done in a week, but am open to it taking two weeks if necessary. The company is called: JLVBeautyCo. and I already own jlvbeautyco.com. I'm thinking the logo will incorporate the letters but also perhaps a makeup brush and/or something else that "says" that I do facials? I'm offering both skincare and makeup services. A tagline I've used before is: "A fresh approach to skincare." I don't have to use that again, but I can if you think it makes sense. I AM doing things a bit different than most estheticians as I only offer ONE facial that is customizable. I'm also going to offer services like dermaplaning and microdermabrasion, and in the future lash lifts & some light waxing. I'm also going to use Korean skincare almost exclusively. As part of my process, I have long, detailed conversations with my clients up front so I understand what their concerns are and as I'm moving through the facial I usually take the time to educate them on products and ingredients, etc. I get a LOT of compliments from people who say no one's ever done a facial like that before, no one's ever explained it like that before, etc. Just including all of this information so you get a better picture of who I am and how I'm different as I know that helps in the logo and website design process. Hopefully this gives you enough of a background for a proposal.

Graphic Designer to edit graphics needin.t: Graphic Designer to edit graphics needing additional work, and/or create new graphics
Graphic Designer to edit graphics needin.d: Provide three to five iterations of each photo based on the descriptions provided to edit or recreate the graphics in the attached files. Some are subtle changes and some may require starting from scratch and incorporating the stated edits. We are on a short timeline and would like to receive the work within days rather than weeks. The designer will work independently, and our team will be available for questions and discussion as needed. Questions and requests for clarification are encouraged. The existing photos depict what we are attempting to accomplish but need to be scaled appropriately and details polished to tell the story more succinctly. 1) The first photo depicts a military troop interrogating a room using our technology (enclosed in the logoed case) from outside the building. In the existing photo, the concrete wall looks like a window. It should appear as a solid wall that we can see through to the contents inside the room. The suitcase should be shorter and longer to look like it's 5'-6' long and should be positioned on the ground outside the building near the center of the solid wall. Add spherical waves to depict a pulse emitting from the case, through the wall into the room. In addition to the existing device on the table, add some brown bricks on the table (to represent fentanyl or other types of drugs/contraband) or a jar of uranium. The soldier should appear to be positioned 3' to 6' from the case holding the computer, as shown. 2) The existing photo is of a golden dome that spans the entire United States, coast to coast. Include in the graphic some of the ocean surrounding the coastlines. Remove the city in the background. The dome should hover over the entire US and there should be a gap between the bottom of the dome and the map of the US. Do not include the dark strip at the bottom of the dome. The gap between the bottom of the dome and the map is intentional (since it cannot reach all the way to the land or ocean). The semi shown underneath provides context and should be included. We want to demonstrate that the gap creates a void that can be breached. This tells story of one of the capabilities of our technology. 3) This graphic shows the inside of the hull of a ship and the contents therein. It is being interrogated from the outside of the ship. The colored text corresponds to the colors assigned to the cargo in the hull and identifies each material. This graphic needs to be recreated. The contents can be anything from bananas to drugs, explosives, bombs, missiles, equipment, etc. Include a variety of cargo and overlay with a different color for each type of item.
