Food Delivery App | React native (User a.t: Food Delivery App | React native (User app + Vendor app + admin panel)
Food Delivery App | React native (User a.d: React Native Food Delivery App – Single Vendor (iOS + Android) We are a restaurant owner looking to build a custom branded food delivery app for our single restaurant chain operating across 3 locations. We want full ownership of the platform — no third-party marketplace like Swiggy or Zomato. Our customers order directly through our app. This is a fixed-scope project. We are not looking for hourly engagements. --- **What we're building:** Three apps + one admin panel: **Customer App (iOS + Android)** - Onboarding and OTP-based login (phone number) - Location detection and delivery radius check - Menu browsing by category with item customization (size, add-ons, spice level) - Cart management - Real-time order tracking with live driver location on map - Multiple payment methods — UPI, card, cash on delivery - Order history and reorder - Loyalty points system — earn points per order, redeem on next - Push notifications for order status updates - Ratings and reviews per order **Delivery Partner App (Android only)** - Login and profile setup - Accept or reject incoming orders - Navigation to restaurant and customer - Earnings summary per day/week - Availability toggle (online/offline) **Restaurant Dashboard (Web)** - Incoming order management — accept, prepare, ready - Menu management — add/edit/remove items, mark out of stock - Delivery partner assignment - Order history and revenue reports - Push notification controls **Admin Panel (Web)** - Manage all three locations - Customer management - Delivery partner onboarding and management - Promo codes and discount management - Revenue and order analytics across locations - Commission or payout management for delivery partners --- **Technical requirements:** - React Native for both customer and delivery apps - Web dashboard in React.js - Node.js backend with REST APIs - PostgreSQL database - Firebase for push notifications and OTP - Google Maps API for live tracking - AWS or equivalent cloud hosting - Payment gateway integration (Razorpay or Stripe) --- Here's that section updated: --- **Scope of work expected from the developer:** - Complete UI/UX design (we have brand guidelines — logo and color palette) - Frontend and backend development - API integrations (maps, payments, notifications) - App Store and Google Play submission - Full source code ownership transferred on final payment **Post-Launch Support & Maintenance:** We expect the developer to provide a clearly defined maintenance plan covering the following: - Bug fixes and crash resolution — response time expectations for critical vs minor issues - OS compatibility updates — keeping the app functional as iOS and Android release new versions - Third-party API maintenance — handling breaking changes from Google Maps, Firebase, payment gateway updates - Performance monitoring — server uptime, API response times, error tracking - Security patches — regular dependency updates and vulnerability fixes - Feature enhancement requests — how additional features outside original scope are scoped and priced - App Store compliance — handling policy changes from Apple and Google that require app updates **Please include in your proposal:** - Your monthly retainer cost for ongoing maintenance - What is covered under retainer vs billed separately - Your SLA (response time) for critical bugs affecting live orders - Whether the first 30/60/90 days post-launch are included in the project price or billed separately **Timeline expectation:** 12–14 weeks from kickoff **Required experience:** - React Native apps shipped to App Store and Google Play (send links) - Food delivery or marketplace app experience preferred - Firebase and Google Maps integration experience - Payment gateway integration experience --- **When applying please answer:** 1. OpenPLC & Node-RED Kiosk Image for Raspb.t: OpenPLC & Node-RED Kiosk Image for Raspberry Pi 3B+ (Industrial Farm Deployment)
OpenPLC & Node-RED Kiosk Image for Raspb.d: I am seeking an experienced Automation / Embedded Systems Engineer to develop a robust, custom, production-ready Linux operating system image for a Raspberry Pi 3B+. The goal of this project is to create a 100% open-source, license-free "Gold Master" image that mimics a Unitronics smart relay/PLC workflow. This image will eventually be cloned across hundreds of offline poultry houses across a large-scale agricultural infrastructure. 🛠️ Hardware Stack (Target Environment) Controller: Raspberry Pi 3B+ booted completely offline (no internet access). Shield Stack: Sequent Microsystems Building Automation HAT + Sequent Stackable Smart UPS HAT (communicating via I2C). Display: Industrial HDMI Touchscreen. Power: 220V AC input stepped down via DIN-rail Mean Well power supplies (24V DC feeding the UPS HAT, separate line feeding the HDMI monitor). Outputs: On-board pilot relays triggering heavy-duty 220V AC industrial contactors/SSRs for high-voltage motors, heating, and lighting. 📋 Software & System Requirements Zero-Licensing Architecture: The entire system must utilize an open-source automation stack. We are using OpenPLC for the IEC 61131-3 visual ladder logic execution engine and Node-RED for the drag-and-drop HMI user interface. OS Hardening & Kiosk Mode: Built on Raspberry Pi OS Lite. The system must boot directly into a full-screen, fullscreen Chromium --kiosk browser pointing to the local Node-RED dashboard interface. There must be zero access to the underlying Linux terminal or desktop for farm operators. Display Power Management (DPMS): The system must utilize Linux screen blanking. The touchscreen backlight must completely power down after 5 minutes of inactivity and wake instantly back to the active HMI screen upon a physical touch event. Hardware Timekeeping (Offline): Synchronize the Linux system clock seamlessly on boot from the Sequent board’s battery-backed DS3231 Hardware RTC chip (hwclock) so the system maintains absolute real-world time accuracy without internet connectivity. Power Fail Resiliency & Retentive State: The software must listen to the Sequent UPS HAT. Upon 220V main power drop, a script must cleanly save current timer registers to retentive storage, write an event log, and execute a safe Linux shutdown to prevent SD card corruption. On power restoration, it must read the file and resume the cycle state seamlessly. HMI Password Protection: Implement a supervisor password/PIN keypad screen on the Node-RED interface, preventing unauthorized workers from modifying the timer settings. Data Logging & Audit Trail: Program local data logging (CSV or SQLite) to the SD card. It must record boot events, power losses, user logins, relay actions (start/stop), and track old vs. new values when configurations are edited. File writes must be buffered to optimize RAM and minimize SD card wear. Mass Replication Design: The image must be completely abstracted. All local variables (e.g., unique House ID, specific timer intervals) must be read from a local config file. Supervisors must be able to change these local configurations via the password-protected HMI screen so that the exact same master .img can be flashed onto hundreds of identical devices without code changes. 📦 Required Deliverables A single, universal flashable .img file ready to be duplicated onto standard SD cards. Clean, well-documented source code files for the OpenPLC logic and Node-RED flows. A highly clear, visual electrical wiring schematic showing exactly how the Pi, the Sequent HAT stack, the Mean Well power supplies, and the 220V contactors interconnect safely.

The story follows two young brothers on a mission to ensure their Granny's birthday party is completely gluten-free and safe. The tone is educational, heartwarming, and adventurous. You will not be starting from scratch. I will provide a comprehensive asset package on Day 1. This includes a completed 16-Panel storyboard detailing the exact narrative flow, page spreads, and initial compositions. I'll provide fully established character reference keys and high-quality reference illustrations showing the exact designs, expressions, and clothing for the 4 characters. Style Target: A cozy, warm, and expressive digital watercolor or clean digital style with beautiful linework that perfectly matches our established character models. You will have the full manuscript, text layout preferences, and visual keys from the very beginning. When you submit a milestone, I provide fast, consolidated, actionable bullet-point feedback so you can make quick adjustments without endless revision loops. Respect for technical layouts: Because this book will be published on Amazon KDP, I place a high priority on technical layout integrity. Text placement must be fluidly intertwined within the scenes (think The Gruffalo style), leaving clean, light "quiet spaces" for the text while strictly respecting center gutter and print safety margins. Zero-Tolerance for AI: No generative AI workflows or asset-flipping tools are permitted. Project Timeline & Milestones (3-Week Sprint). The total project duration is 3 weeks (21 days) from the contract start date. The budget will be funded upfront into Upwork Escrow and released across 4 strict sequential milestones: Milestone 1: Progress Batch 1 (Pages 1–8) | Due: Day 5 Deliverable: The first 8 pages fully illustrated and colored to verify style alignment, text placement fluidly integrated into the backgrounds, and correct character age proportions. Payout: 25% of total budget. Milestone 2: Progress Batch 2 (Pages 9–16) | Due: Day 10 Deliverable: The next 8 pages fully illustrated, ensuring perfect character consistency across scenes. Payout: 25% of total budget. Milestone 3: Progress Batch 3 (Pages 17–24) | Due: Day 15 Deliverable: Complete illustrations for pages 17 to 24. Payout: 25% of total budget. Milestone 4: Final Batch, Cover & Handovers (Pages 25–32) | Due: Day 21 Deliverable: The final illustration batch, front/back cover formatting, final technical verification of KDP print margins, and delivery of high-resolution, print-ready files (layered source files + PDF). Payout: 25% of total budget. Requirements to Apply A link to your portfolio demonstrating consistent children's book character rendering (specifically maintaining young child proportions). Confirmation that you can begin immediately and comfortably commit to this 3-week milestone schedule.A brief note on your preferred software (Procreate, Photoshop, etc.). Technical Specifications & Deliverables for Amazon KDP To ensure a seamless upload to Amazon KDP, the final deliverables must strictly adhere to the following publishing standards: Color Space: All files must be built and delivered in CMYK color mode for crisp, accurate physical printing (not RGB). Resolution: Minimum 300 DPI for all final artwork. Final Deliverables Required: Interior File: One single, completely compiled, print-ready PDF containing all 32 interior pages in sequential order, with text embedded and bleed margins correctly applied. Cover File: One single, flattened, print-ready PDF wrap (Back Cover + Spine + Front Cover combined into a single spread) designed precisely to Amazon KDP's cover template dimensions based on the final page count. Source Files: Fully layered source files (layered PSD, Procreate, or AI files) with all text layers left editable so I can make minor textual adjustments in the future if needed.

Odoo.sh Developer: Fix Product Catalog U.t: Odoo.sh Developer: Fix Product Catalog UI/Logic to Allow Duplicate Products in Multiple Sections
Odoo.sh Developer: Fix Product Catalog U.i: Hourly Expert Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Odoo.sh Developer: Fix Product Catalog U.d: We are looking for an experienced Odoo Developer to fix an edge case in our sales quotation flow on Odoo.sh (Custom Plan). The Issue: We customized our quotation view by removing the standard "Add a Product" inline link and forcing users to use the "Catalog" button instead (so they can view product images while ordering). However, our clients require quotes broken down by rooms/sections. Currently, if a user adds "Product A" (e.g., 4 quantities) under Section 1 using the Catalog, and then tries to add "Product A" again under Section 2 via the Catalog, Odoo remembers the previous selection. Instead of creating a new line item under the new section, it just points back to the first line or updates its quantity. What We Need: Modify the Odoo Catalog UI/UX logic (OWL/JavaScript & Python backend) so that it allows users to add the same product multiple times as separate line items under different section lines. Ensure the catalog resets or differentiates selections based on the active section the user is currently under. Requirements: Proven experience with Odoo backend (Python) and frontend (OWL/JS framework) customization. Experience working with Odoo.sh environments and git workflows. Strong understanding of the sale.order and sale.order.line models and the standard Catalog wizard functionality. Please reply with your experience handling Odoo Catalog/Wizard customizations and your estimated timeline for this fix.

Illustrator for Wedding Stationery & The.d: We are building a premium wedding-planning platform. Everything a couple makes inside the app (their wedding website, their printed invitations, their save-the-dates, their guest emails) shares one visual theme, so it all feels like one designed whole. The themes are the heart of the product, and the illustration quality is what will make or break it. We need an illustrator (or a small team) to create the illustration assets for 12 distinct wedding themes. To be clear about what we are NOT asking: we do not want finished, fixed invitation cards. We have built our own rendering engine that places text and lays out the design. What we need from you is the raw illustration elements that our engine arranges, so the same theme can flow responsively across a tall invitation, a wide website header, a square format, and an email. Think of it the way Canva works: you create a library of beautiful elements, and our software composes them. That separation is essential. What we need per theme A set of LOOSE, individual illustration elements (not a single locked composition): 2 to 4 corner ornaments (e.g. top-left, bottom-right) 1 full border / wreath arrangement 3 to 5 individual sprigs, branches or floral stems that can be placed freely 1 to 2 divider / flourish line elements Any small accent marks that fit the theme Plus a short style sheet per theme: the recommended display font, body font and script/accent font (Google Fonts or clearly licensed), and the color palette (hex values). We already have draft palettes and can share them, you refine and confirm. The 12 themes span a wide stylistic range, including (examples, final list shared on start): airy botanical watercolor, fine pen-line florals, minimalist sage with large serif type, art-deco geometric, wildflower meadow, romantic peony, celestial / night, and a hand-drawn playful style. We are open to using more than one illustrator if no single person covers the full range, so it is fine to apply for the styles you are strongest in.

WordPress / WooCommerce Developer & UX/U.t: WordPress / WooCommerce Developer & UX/UI Designer for Premium Restaurant, Catering & Events Website
WordPress / WooCommerce Developer & UX/U.i: Hourly Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
WordPress / WooCommerce Developer & UX/U.d: We are looking for an experienced WordPress / WooCommerce developer, UX/UI designer, or small development team to refine, finalize and implement a premium hospitality website for Berg Blick Restaurant, Catering & Events. The goal is to build a refined, responsive and technically maintainable WordPress website with a premium visual frontend, modular backend structure, SEO-ready architecture and a practical catering order or inquiry workflow. The first phase should remain lean, practical and operationally realistic, using third-party integrations where appropriate. The project is based on an existing Figma file that already contains the core visual direction. This file should not be redesigned from scratch. Instead, it should be carefully reviewed, completed, polished and elevated into a consistent, responsive, prototype-linked and development-ready design system. Existing Figma file: https://www.figma.com/design/2SuHjkW9ELQRAtVeYYGLvo/BERG-BLICK-RESTAURANT?node-id=3706-61106&t=DZhnMaIBjKC6tHSs-1 Project Objective The final website should feel like a premium digital hospitality experience, not like a standard restaurant website. It should combine: Restaurant presentation Catering offers and catering order/inquiry flow Event inquiries Takeaway information or ordering integration Table reservation integration Help / Contact areas SEO landing pages Newsletter / community elements Multilingual-ready content structure The website should feel: premium modern warm animated structured fluid hospitality-focused mobile-first conversion-oriented easy to manage internally Important Application Requirement Before starting implementation, the contractor must provide an initial technical analysis. This technical analysis should include: recommended WordPress setup recommended plugin stack assessment of whether the catering flow should be built as WooCommerce order flow, inquiry flow or hybrid flow assessment of required third-party integrations assessment of payment and invoice setup performance and SEO considerations risks, limitations and recommended implementation approach Applicants must also provide relevant references. Relevant references should ideally include: premium WordPress websites restaurant or hospitality websites WooCommerce order flows catering, food ordering or booking systems Figma-to-WordPress projects websites with animations, GSAP or premium scrolling effects multilingual WordPress websites projects with payment, invoice or booking integrations Applications without a short technical assessment and relevant references will not be prioritized. Project Scope The project consists of two main work packages: Design refinement Technical WordPress implementation The existing design should be refined and elevated first. Only once the design direction, responsive layouts, animations and prototype logic are approved should the development phase begin. Design Scope The design phase is a key part of the project. The objective is not only to make the current Figma file responsive, but to turn it into a polished, premium and development-ready design system. The current design should be treated as a strong draft. It already contains elements that should be preserved where they work well, especially the overall visual direction, typography, header approach, selected images, small moving image elements and the premium atmosphere already visible in parts of the file. At the same time, some sections still need refinement. Several areas feel unresolved, too static, visually inconsistent or unfinished. These sections should be reworked and brought into a more cohesive, modern, elegant and fluid design language. The design should feel like an animated, informative visual story. It should feel more alive, more premium and more contemporary. Sections should become more dynamic, with a strong sense of storytelling, layout rhythm, modern composition and subtle interaction. Design Requirements The contractor should: review the existing Figma file in detail identify which sections should remain and which need refinement improve layout rhythm and visual hierarchy create a more polished and premium overall design language complete unfinished sections and subpages refine desktop layouts refine tablet layouts rethink the mobile experience prepare a clean, app-like mobile version create a consistent component logic improve spacing, typography and readability ensure the design feels coherent across all pages prepare the final Figma file for development handoff Color System The following color palette should be applied consistently: #E7E0D2 – Bone #BAB59E – Khaki Beige #FFE4DD – Soft Blush #FF8551 – Coral Glow #FF6529 – Tiger Flame The stronger orange tones should be used selectively for CTAs, highlights and important interactive moments. They should not dominate the entire website. The design should use calm background areas, reduced color blocks and clear visual distinction between base sections, highlighted sections, buttons and interactive elements. Mobile UX The mobile version is especially important. It should not simply be a compressed desktop version. Instead, the mobile experience should feel: clearer more reduced more app-like easier to navigate faster to understand more conversion-focused It is acceptable to simplify, shorten, rearrange or omit content on mobile if this improves usability. Animation, Interaction & Prototyping Requirements Animations are an important part of the desired premium experience, but they must support the user journey and not overload the website. The design phase should include a clear animation and interaction concept. Desired animation and interaction elements: subtle scroll-based storytelling smooth section transitions elegant fade-ins image movement or parallax where appropriate hover effects for buttons, cards and navigation elements micro-interactions for CTAs animated transitions between key sections premium but lightweight motion language mobile-friendly interaction behavior The contractor should define in Figma how these interactions should work and prepare them for developer handoff. The Figma prototype should clearly communicate: main user journey navigation logic catering entry points event inquiry flow mobile menu behavior button states hover states where relevant scrolling logic section transitions interactive elements handoff notes for animations Only after the static design has been resolved in a consistent and polished way should animations, transitions and prototype links be finalized. The final Figma file must be visually consistent, content-complete, responsive across relevant screen sizes, prototype-linked and ready for implementation. Catering Workflow Requirement A key part of the project is a simplified but professional catering order or inquiry workflow. The preferred logic is inspired by online grocery ordering systems such as REWE: customers should first browse products or catering offers, select items or packages, and only later choose delivery or pickup details. The catering flow should support the following customer journey: Customer browses catering categories such as breakfast, lunch, snacks, drinks or event catering. Customer selects products, packages or inquiry options. Customer adds items to the cart or structured inquiry form. Relevant upselling options are shown, for example smoothies with breakfast or desserts with lunch. The system checks whether the minimum order value is reached. Customer selects delivery or pickup. Customer selects a delivery or pickup date. The system enforces a minimum lead time of two days. Customer enters company, contact, delivery and invoice details. Customer adds allergies, dietary requirements and special instructions. Customer submits the order or inquiry. The internal team receives a structured request or order summary. If payment is included, the customer can pay online, for example via PayPal or credit card. If invoicing is enabled, an automatic invoice should be generated and sent to the customer. Catering Business Rules The catering workflow must support: minimum lead time: 2 days in advance minimum order value: €350 delivery or pickup slot selection structured customer and company data allergy and dietary request fields optional payment by PayPal, credit card or invoice automatic invoice generation internal order or inquiry notification possibility to generate production or packing information internally The exact setup may be implemented as: direct WooCommerce order flow inquiry-based process hybrid system The chosen solution must be intuitive for the customer and operationally practical for the Berg Blick team. Recommended Technical Foundation The website should be built with a clean, maintainable and performance-conscious WordPress setup. Recommended technical stack: WordPress Custom WordPress theme based on Figma ACF Pro for modular backend content management WooCommerce for catering order functionality, if a direct order flow is implemented Fluent Forms or Gravity Forms for inquiry-based workflows Rank Math SEO for SEO structure WPML or multilingual-ready setup GSAP or custom JavaScript for animations and transitions WP Rocket or LiteSpeed Cache for performance optimization Imagify or similar for WebP / image optimization Cloudflare for CDN and security WP Mail SMTP for reliable email delivery Google Analytics 4 and Google Search Console Mollie Payments for WooCommerce for payment processing, including PayPal Germanized for WooCommerce and/or PDF Invoices & Packing Slips for German checkout compliance and automatic invoice creation Existing third-party systems may be integrated where operationally useful: Lightspeed for menu / takeaway ordering CentralPlanner or another reservation system for table reservations ActiveCampaign for newsletter and CRM workflows The setup should avoid unnecessary technical complexity in the first phase and use connected third-party systems or iframe integrations where they are operationally more practical. Recommended WooCommerce Setup for Catering If WooCommerce is used for the catering flow, the recommended plugin stack would be: WooCommerce Order Delivery Date Pro for WooCommerce WooCommerce Product Add-Ons or Product Options and Fields WooCommerce Product Bundles Cart and Checkout Upsell Checkout Field Editor for WooCommerce Mollie Payments for WooCommerce Germanized for WooCommerce PDF Invoices & Packing Slips Custom development may be required for: €350 minimum order value rule 2-day advance order validation B2B checkout fields conditional invoice payment for approved business customers internal production or packing list formatting category-based product availability, for example breakfast only in the morning Development Requirements The developer should implement: custom WordPress theme based on the approved Figma design responsive frontend for desktop, tablet and mobile reusable content modules via ACF Pro clean navigation, header and footer structure restaurant pages catering pages event pages contact/help pages SEO-friendly page structure animations and transitions catering inquiry or order flow third-party integrations where required multilingual-ready structure performance optimization analytics and tracking setup testing and launch support The backend must remain easy to manage for the internal team. Texts, images, recurring sections, catering offers, event content and SEO fields should be editable without developer support. SEO Requirements The website must be built with strong technical SEO fundamentals from the beginning. Requirements include: clean URL structure editable meta titles and descriptions Rank Math SEO setup clear heading hierarchy structured and indexable content internal linking logic fast loading times mobile optimization image optimization XML sitemap multilingual SEO readiness Google Search Console setup Google Analytics 4 setup The structure should also support future SEO landing pages for catering, events, restaurant and local search topics. Payment and Invoice Requirements The recommended payment provider is Mollie Payments for WooCommerce, because it supports relevant German and European payment methods, including PayPal, credit card and Apple Pay. The system should support: PayPal credit card Apple Pay optional Klarna optional SEPA or bank transfer invoice payment for approved B2B customers prepayment or deposit logic for larger catering orders For invoices, the recommended setup is Germanized for WooCommerce Pro and/or PDF Invoices & Packing Slips. The system should be able to: automatically generate invoices attach invoice PDFs to order emails make invoices available in the customer account include company name, invoice address, VAT, cost center and PO number where relevant generate packing slips or internal order documents support manual invoice correction if an order changes Milestone Structure The project should be delivered in structured milestones. Milestone 1 – UX/UI Design Refinement & Design Finalization This first milestone replaces a generic kickoff milestone. The first real project output should be the refined, completed and development-ready design. Scope: full review of the existing Figma file UX/UI review and design direction alignment assessment of existing sections and visual logic layout, hierarchy and spacing refinement color system implementation completion of unresolved or missing sections Help and Contact page completion responsive desktop, tablet and mobile refinement mobile-first UX improvement animation concept definition prototype linking interaction logic developer handoff preparation Figma cleanup and component organization Expected result: a refined, complete, responsive, prototype-linked and development-ready Figma file. Milestone 2 – Technical Analysis & Architecture Planning Scope: technical analysis of the required WordPress setup recommendation of plugin stack assessment of WooCommerce vs. inquiry-based catering flow definition of catering workflow logic definition of 2-day lead-time logic definition of €350 minimum order value logic definition of payment and invoice setup third-party integration planning SEO structure review backend architecture planning performance and security concept staging/project environment preparation Expected result: a clear technical implementation plan before development starts. Milestone 3 – Frontend Development & Core WordPress Structure Scope: WordPress environment setup custom theme development global frontend structure header/footer/navigation development responsive page structure reusable component system homepage implementation restaurant pages event pages catering pages SEO-focused structural setup ACF-based backend content architecture Milestone 4 – Catering Flow, Animations & Integrations Scope: simplified catering inquiry or order flow 2-day lead-time logic €350 minimum order value logic WooCommerce or form-based implementation structured request/order handling upselling logic payment integration if applicable automatic invoice setup if applicable Lightspeed integration or embedding where applicable reservation system integration GSAP/custom interaction development scroll transitions and storytelling sections responsive interaction optimization multilingual-ready structure implementation Milestone 5 – SEO, Optimization, Testing & Go-Live Scope: technical SEO setup meta structure and SEO field setup performance optimization mobile optimization final content integration QA testing across devices checkout/order testing form testing payment testing invoice testing bug fixing and refinements analytics and tracking setup deployment and launch support final backend cleanup Ideal Candidate We are looking for someone who has experience with: custom WordPress development WooCommerce setup and customization ACF Pro responsive frontend development Figma-to-WordPress implementation GSAP or premium web animations technical SEO performance optimization multilingual WordPress setups payment provider integrations German WooCommerce compliance tools structured form or order workflows hospitality, restaurant, catering or booking websites Experience with the following tools is a plus: Mollie Germanized for WooCommerce PDF Invoices & Packing Slips Order Delivery Date Pro WooCommerce Product Add-Ons WooCommerce Product Bundles Fluent Forms or Gravity Forms WPML Rank Math ActiveCampaign Lightspeed CentralPlanner Cloudflare Kinsta Expected Result The final result should be a premium, responsive and technically stable WordPress website for Berg Blick Restaurant, Catering & Events. The website should: look and feel premium follow the approved Figma design work smoothly on desktop and mobile include a simple and user-friendly catering workflow enforce a 2-day catering lead time support a €350 minimum order value integrate relevant third-party tools allow online payment where required support automatic invoice creation remain easy to edit internally support multilingual and SEO-ready growth provide a solid base for future extensions The first phase should remain lean, practical and maintainable. The priority is to create a strong hospitality platform with a polished frontend, clear user journey and reliable operational workflows.

Develop AR Virtual T-Shirt Try-On WordPr.d: WearLens – AR Virtual T-Shirt Try-On WordPress Plugin Development Project Overview We are looking for an experienced developer or development team to build a professional and user-friendly WordPress/WooCommerce plugin called WearLens. The goal of this plugin is to provide a real-time Augmented Reality (AR) virtual try-on experience for clothing stores, especially for T-shirts. Customers should be able to click a “View in AR” button on a product page and instantly preview the selected T-shirt design on their body in real time using their device camera, similar to modern AR filter experiences. The plugin should be modern, lightweight, responsive, optimized, and compatible with a wide range of devices and browsers. Core Features Required 1. Real-Time AR T-Shirt Try-On Customer opens a T-shirt product page A “View in AR” button appears below the product gallery When clicked: Device camera opens instantly Selected T-shirt design loads in AR mode T-shirt aligns with the customer’s chest/body area User can move naturally and preview the shirt in real time Experience should feel smooth and interactive 2. Dynamic Product Integration The plugin should automatically work with WooCommerce T-shirt products. Requirements: Selected design should display correctly in AR Product colors should reflect accurately Product variations should sync automatically Multiple designs should be supported 3. Seller/Admin Dashboard A professional admin dashboard is required where store owners can: Upload T-shirt designs Upload or manage 3D shirt models Adjust: Position Scaling Alignment Color settings AR fitting settings Configure product-specific settings Preview AR results before publishing Easily manage products Dashboard UI should be clean, modern, and beginner-friendly. 4. Device Compatibility Plugin should work smoothly on: Android devices iPhones/iPads Desktop browsers (where supported) Browser compatibility preferred: Chrome Safari Edge Firefox (if supported) Performance optimization is extremely important. The plugin should remain smooth and stable without lag or crashes. 5. WooCommerce Integration The plugin should integrate properly with WooCommerce features including: Variable products Product galleries Product variations Cart compatibility Checkout compatibility 6. UI/UX Requirements Brand Name: WearLens Theme Colors: Yellow Black Design style should feel: Premium Futuristic Clean Fashion-tech inspired Animations and transitions should feel modern and smooth. 7. Preferred Features AI body tracking Auto chest alignment Real-time resizing Multiple size previews AR screenshot feature Social sharing Analytics tracking CDN optimization Lightweight loading system One-click setup wizard 8. Technical Expectations Preferred experience with: WebAR Three.js TensorFlow.js 8thWall / MindAR / ZapWorks / WebXR WordPress Plugin Development WooCommerce APIs 3D rendering optimization Code should be: Clean Secure Scalable Well-documented 9. Deliverables Expected deliverables: Fully functional plugin Complete source code Installation support Documentation Bug fixing support Deployment assistance 10. Long-Term Opportunity Successful completion may lead to long-term collaboration for: Feature upgrades Maintenance Updates Additional AR features Requirements for Applicants Please include: Portfolio Similar AR/WebAR projects Estimated timeline Budget estimate Technologies/frameworks you plan to use Developers are free to use the technology stack or AR solution they believe is best suited for the project. Our priority is a reliable, high-quality, and optimized final product that delivers a smooth AR virtual try-on experience across devices. Thank you.

Ecom Video Editor for Meta & TikTok Ads.t: Ecom Video Editor for Meta & TikTok Ads
Ecom Video Editor for Meta & TikTok Ads.d: About Us: We are a dynamic and rapidly growing 7-figure women's jewelry brand powered by Shopify. We are passionate about great design that speaks for itself, drawing inspiration from everyday fashion and influencers, and genuinely caring about the products we create and the relationships we build. Our marketing is performance-driven, and high-converting video creative is central to our scaling strategy. The Role: We're seeking a skilled and agile E-commerce Response Video Editor (full time / 40 hours per week) who understands how to create video ads that don't just look good but convert. You will work in lockstep with our Creative Strategist, taking raw footage (UGC, potentially studio shots) and briefs to produce engaging, platform-optimized video ads primarily for Meta (Facebook/Instagram) and TikTok. Your job is to execute rapidly, iterate effectively based on performance insights (provided by the strategist), and ensure the final output is engineered for maximum engagement and conversions. Forget long-form content; this is about short, punchy, direct-response video. IMPORTANT: This is not a role for someone juggling multiple jobs, working for or running a freelancing agency, or managing several clients at once. We are looking for a dedicated full-time video editor who will work exclusively with us and be fully committed to our brand, team, and creative output. Key Responsibilities: • Edit High-Performing Video Ads: Transform raw footage and strategist briefs into compelling video ads optimized for Meta and TikTok feeds/stories/reels. • Rapid Iteration: Quickly execute multiple variations of winning ad concepts provided by the Creative Strategist (testing different hooks, visuals, text overlays, CTAs, music). Understand that iteration based on data is key. • Platform Optimization: Expertly edit for platform best practices – strong hooks within the first 3 seconds, fast pacing, engaging captions/text overlays, appropriate aspect ratios (9:16, 1:1, 4:5), safe zones, and effective sound design. • Integrate Copy & CTAs: Seamlessly weave in primary text concepts, headlines, and clear calls-to-action as guided by the strategist. • Collaborate Closely: Maintain clear, fast communication (Slack, etc.) with the Creative Strategist to receive feedback, discuss edits, and ensure alignment with performance goals. • Asset Management: Organize and manage video files and project assets efficiently. • Meet Deadlines: Deliver high-quality edits reliably and on schedule in a fast-paced environment. Who We're Looking For: • Proven Social Ad Editing Experience: Minimum 2+ experience specifically editing short-form video advertisements for performance marketing campaigns on Meta & TikTok. A portfolio showcasing direct-response style video ads is required. • Technical Proficiency: Highly skilled in video editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, CapCut is a major plus). Proficiency in basic motion graphics (e.g., After Effects for text overlays) is desirable. • Platform Native: Deep understanding of what works visually on TikTok and Meta feeds/stories/reels – you know the trends, the hooks, the pacing, the sounds that grab attention immediately. • Performance-Oriented Mindset: You understand the goal is conversion, not just aesthetics. You are receptive to feedback driven by ad metrics and can adapt edits accordingly. (Links to Strategist's "Data Mastery," "Creativity That Converts"). • Speed and Agility: Ability to work quickly, execute variations efficiently, and adapt to changing requirements without sacrificing quality. (Links to Strategist's "Adaptability & Agility"). • Collaborative Spirit: Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively and receive direction from a Creative Strategist. (Links to Strategist's "Collaboration is Key," "Clear Communication"). • Strong Work Ethic & Accountability: Reliable, proactive, meets deadlines, and takes ownership of edit quality – no babysitting needed. (Links to Strategist's "Work Ethic"). • Detail-Oriented: Ensures brand consistency, correct aspect ratios, and error-free text/graphics. Nothing sloppy makes the cut. • Basic understanding of direct response marketing principles. • Experience editing UGC content. Compensation: $2,000 monthly base salary, with the opportunity to earn monthly performance-based bonuses tied to creative contributions. Total monthly compensation can reach up to $4,000 based on performance. How to Apply: In your proposal, you MUST include (give as much detail as possible): 1. Provide your Resume/CV and link to your portfolio showcasing ECOM VIDEO ADS for Meta/TikTok.Other type of ads will not be looked at or considered. 2. Briefly describe your process for editing a typical 15-45 sec. video ad. 3. How do you handle feedback and rapid iteration requests from creative strategists? 4. List your primary editing software and mention your experience with CapCut. 5. What AI tools or software have you used to create video ads? Provide as much detail as possible and share any previous AI work if any.

Finish Writing/Editing a book then Kindl.t: Finish Writing/Editing a book then Kindle Publish
Finish Writing/Editing a book then Kindl.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Finish Writing/Editing a book then Kindl.d: This is the feedback I have received that is needed for the book. Specific areas to strengthen: • The intro / opening. o Consider the "Why What Who How Now" framework, where the CERI process is the HOW (the NOW is the CTA). o More building out of the WHY up front -- why this matters to the reader, to their family, to their community, to the world) o I'd love to see the WHAT (AKA: PROBLEM / SOLUTION) be fleshed out more -- the symptoms and struggles this addresses. And presenting the solution as a clear directive: "Make friends with money" or something like that (maybe that's not it, but some sort of very simple "command" statement that encapsulates your "big sexy idea." o How you came to know the solution (WHO) -- your origin story which can be woven throughout the book, but at least share your credibility in the beginning somewhere -- AKA: how did you come to know this? Not a resume, but a reason to trust your advice. How many clients have you worked with? What do you actually do with them? What have you witnessed, over and over, that made you realize this was a pattern worth writing about? And YOUR story -- right now you have stories that illustrate the point but not an ORIGIN story. You have stories that illustrate the concept beautifully. But I find myself wondering: how did Joan come to know this? What happened in your own life or work that made you see the vision/money confusion so clearly? That will create connection with the reader. I think the answer might already be in the book. The capital gains story is close. • Chapters -- I think the internal chapters would also benefit from following a modified "Why / What / Who / How / Now" process so that each chapter has it's own Problem -- Solution. I am more a "talker" than writer. To work with me it would help to have you "interview" me about the parts that need to be written. You then write them and I review them.

Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealist.t: Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealistic Beauty Subscription Box
Luxury Packaging Designer — Hyperrealist.d: We're creating a beauty subscription box that features multiple brands in every delivery, and we're looking for a designer to create the packaging ... the physical box, structure, materials, and finishes ... at a true luxury level. The gold standard we keep coming back to is Gucci: clean, confident, impeccably finished, and luxurious without trying too hard. That's the feeling we want to capture and make our own. For clarity, this is a packaging design project. We're hiring someone to design a beautiful, premium box — its form, its structure, its materials, and how it presents multiple beauty products inside. A note on who we're looking for: We're specifically looking for designers with beauty packaging experience. Beauty is its own world — the aesthetics, the materials, the way products are showcased — and we've found it really does take someone who has worked in this space to get it right. If your background is in beauty or cosmetics packaging, we'd love to hear from you. If it isn't, this likely isn't the right fit, and that's okay. The packaging we're after We want a box that feels unmistakably luxury from the outside in. Here's what matters most to us: -A refined, premium exterior. A box that signals luxury before it's even opened — beautiful proportions, clean lines, impeccable finish. -Elevated materials and finishes. Smooth, soft, modern luxury. (A note on taste: we're not fans of embossing or hot foil stamping, so please lean away from that look.) -Thoughtful interior structure. Inserts and dividers that hold each brand's product securely and present them like they matter — organized, elegant, with room to breathe rather than crowded. -Considered details. A satisfying closure, a ribbon pull, a branded seal, a thoughtful card — small touches that make the whole thing feel intentional and polished. -A premium opening. The box should open in a way that feels special — a clean, satisfying reveal of the products inside. Throughout, we're drawn to restraint: beautiful materials and elegant finishes over clutter or heavy ornamentation. What you'll be designing toward: We'll provide our vision and reference imagery for the look and feel. The designer we award the project will take that direction and develop an original design — so we want someone who can elevate inspiration into something distinctly their own. You have real creative latitude here; we're giving you the feeling, not a rulebook. Deliverables — a collaborative, two-phase process: The project moves in two phases: Phase 1 — Concepts. At least three distinct design directions (storyboards, mockups, concept boards — whatever suits your process), each a different take on the packaging. We'd love a short walkthrough of your thinking with each one: the choices behind the structure, the materials and finishes, how the products are arranged inside, the closure and opening, and any special touches you'd build in (a magnetic close, a ribbon pull, a hidden compartment, an interior video screen, and so on). We'll review together, share feedback, and approve a direction to take forward. Phase 2 — Final renders. Once we've aligned on a concept, you'll produce the finished hyperrealistic imagery: -3 hyperrealistic images of the box closed -3 hyperrealistic images of the box open, showing the interior structure and how the multi-brand products are presented inside These final images should read as real product photography — photorealistic renders, not flat mockups. To apply, please include: Examples of beauty packaging you've designed (portfolio, past concepts, or relevant work). Please note, food & beverage, apparel, etc will not be considered samples will not be considered. If this sounds like you, we'd love to see your work.

Mandarin-English Technical Interpreter f.d: Job Title: Mandarin-English Technical Interpreter for Product Development Calls Job Description: We’re looking for a Mandarin-English technical interpreter to join live product development calls with our engineering team in China. This role is specifically for technical communication during product development, not general translation. We need someone who can help our English-speaking team communicate clearly with Chinese engineers and manufacturers, especially around beauty / personal care devices such as LED face masks, IPL devices, RF devices, and similar at-home electronic beauty devices. The ideal person has experience interpreting in technical, engineering, manufacturing, or product development settings and is comfortable asking clarifying questions in real time when something is unclear. You will help interpret discussions related to: -Product specifications -Engineering updates -Component changes -Firmware or device behavior -Testing requirements -Quality control issues -Manufacturing constraints -Safety and usage details -Packaging, manuals, and labeling -Product development feedback between teams We need someone who can do more than translate sentence by sentence. The Visual Concept: The branding consists of two distinct elements: 1. The Chest Emblem: An interlocking "VV" inside a subtle, minimalist shield. This will be flat-embroidered onto the left chest of the garments. 2. The Wordmark / Neck Tag: The word "VEYLMONT" in a timeless, elegant serif or geometric sans-serif font, accompanied by the sub-text "TIMELESS MEN" and "BENGALURU • EST. 2026". Neck Label Layout: Assemble the typography for the woven neck labels to fit a standard layout (2.5 inches wide x 1.5 inches high, end-fold format). Final File Handoff: Deliver all editable source files (.AI, .EPS), print-ready PDFs for the factory, and high-resolution PNGs with transparent backgrounds. The Ideal Freelancer: Expert proficiency in Adobe Illustrator. Understands the technical limitations and requirements of translating digital designs into physical apparel manufacturing (specifically small-scale embroidery and woven labels). Have you previously designed or optimized logos specifically for small-scale garment embroidery? 2. Senior UX/UI Product Designer for Web Ap.d: Job Summary / Duties I’m looking for an expert-level UX/UI product designer with excellent judgment, strong web app design instincts, and an unusually good aesthetic sense. The ideal person can quickly understand an existing web application by reviewing both the live URL and the GitHub repository, then produce clear, actionable recommendations for improving the interface, user experience, visual hierarchy, usability, information architecture, and overall polish. This role is not just about making things “look nicer.” I want someone who can think like a senior product designer: identify friction points, simplify workflows, improve clarity, spot inconsistencies, propose better interaction patterns, and explain the reasoning behind each recommendation. The work will typically involve: * Reviewing an existing web app through the live URL and GitHub repo * Auditing the current UX/UI against modern web app best practices * Producing clear markdown files that document proposed changes, rationale, priorities, and implementation notes * Creating visual mockups, ideally interactive prototypes, that show the recommended improvements * Using tools like Figma/Adobe CC/whatever-you-prefer, GitHub, Codex, Claude Code / Claude-based coding workflows, or similar AI-assisted development tools to inspect the app and develop recommendations * Suggesting practical changes that a developer can actually implement * Prioritizing improvements by impact, effort, and user value * Noticing subtle design issues around spacing, typography, layout, affordances, responsiveness, accessibility, and interaction design * Helping turn rough functional web apps into clean, intuitive, polished, professional products I am happy to pay more for the right person. I care much more about expertise, speed, taste, and quality of output than finding the lowest rate. A great fit would be someone who can accomplish a lot in a focused hour and produce thoughtful, usable deliverables without needing heavy direction. Please include examples of web apps, dashboards, admin tools, SaaS products, or internal tools you have improved. Bonus points if you can show before/after UX work, product audits, Figma prototypes, GitHub-based workflows, or examples of markdown-based design specs you have created for developers.

Brand Identity Designer — Logo and Style.t: Brand Identity Designer — Logo and Style Kit for Business Program
Brand Identity Designer — Logo and Style.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Brand Identity Designer — Logo and Style.d: The Three-Hour BD Week — Logo and Brand Identity Project Overview I need a freelance brand designer to create a logo and brand identity kit for a business program called **The Three-Hour BD Week**. This is a standalone brand (not connected to my consulting firm, Bartlett Strategies) with its own domain. The logo and brand assets will be used on a sales landing page, a PDF program guide, social media graphics, and a buyer-facing app currently in development. The brand identity is largely defined — colors, fonts, and tone are locked. Your job is to design a logo and deliver a clean brand kit, not to make strategic decisions from scratch. I will share a fully designed HTML landing page as your primary style reference. The Brand Name:** The Three-Hour BD Week Tagline:** Stop Scrambling. Start a System. Doctrine (used inside the program, not on the logo): Consistency compounds. Intensity fades. What it is: A business development system for small business owners and professional services firms who want consistent pipeline without burning hours on BD every week. Three focused hours per week, done in a specific rhythm, compounds over time. Audience: Small business owners, consultants, fractional executives, and sales professionals — primarily in professional services. Educated, busy, skeptical of hustle-culture marketing. Tone: Clean, credible, professional. Think established consultant, not startup casual. Trustworthy and direct. Not corporate-stiff, but not bro-y either. Closest visual references: Basecamp, Notion, a boutique management consulting firm. Colors | Blue | `#4A6FA5` | Primary — CTA buttons, icon accent, links | | Blue Light | `#5A82B8` | Hover states only | | Blue Subtle | `#EDF1F7` | Backgrounds, callout cards | | Black | `#111111` | Logo wordmark, primary headings | | Dark | `#1A1A1A` | Dark backgrounds | | White | `#FFFFFF` | Default page background | | Gray 700 | `#444444` | Body text | | Gray 500 | `#777777` | Supporting text | | Gray 300 | `#BBBBBB` | Borders | | Gray 100 | `#F5F5F5` | Alternate section backgrounds | ## Typography - **Headings:** Playfair Display (Google Fonts, free) - **Body / UI:** Inter (Google Fonts, free) ## What I Need You to Design 1. Logo System A logo with two components: an icon mark and a wordmark. Deliver both together and the icon alone as a standalone mark. 2. Logo Variations Deliver all of the following: - Full logo (icon + wordmark) on white background - Full logo (icon + wordmark) on dark (#1A1A1A) background - Icon mark only (standalone) on white - Icon mark only on dark - Horizontal and stacked versions if both are viable - One-color version in blue (#4A6FA5) on white 3. Brand Style Sheet (one page) A clean one-page PDF showing: - Logo usage and clear space rules - Color palette with hex codes - Typography pairing (Playfair Display + Inter) - Do/don't usage examples What I Am Providing - Complete HTML landing page as the primary visual reference (shared after hiring) - All copy - Brand colors and fonts as specified above - Feedback within 24 hours on all rounds I might need a website if you're open to add that later. Do not use: briefcases, handshakes, bar charts, lightbulbs, generic arrows, LinkedIn-style icons, or anything that reads as generic "business" clipart. No drop shadows. No gradients. No decorative script fonts. Questions to Answer in Your Proposal Share two or three logo or brand identity projects from your portfolio, ideally for service businesses or programs (not product companies or consumer brands). How many initial concepts do you present in the first round? Have you designed for a personal brand or thought leadership product before?

Team Logo Illustration.d: I need to optimize this image. It's supposed to be a collage of the hometown of people on my team with all the places that they mentioned. Please optimize it so no weird artifacts, each country has one flag, its seamless and no weird intersection of countries, etc. Do your best! Example attached. Example Prompt attached: Create a polished **hand-drawn doodle art team emblem** for a team named **“Catalog Quality.”** The design should look like a colorful collaborative doodle mural transformed into a clean badge or sticker. Use **bold black hand-drawn outlines, simple iconic shapes, playful sketch details, small decorative marks, arrows, swirls, dots, stars, motion lines, and sticker-like doodle elements**. The style should feel whimsical, energetic, and handmade, like a high-quality illustrated notebook doodle or creative team whiteboard drawing. The words **“Catalog Quality”** must be centered prominently as the main focal point. Make the lettering bold, clean, readable, and hand-drawn, like custom doodle lettering. Keep the text unobstructed. Arrange all icons around the centered text in a balanced circular or badge-like composition. The landmarks and symbols should wrap naturally around the team name, forming one cohesive global collage. Do not create separate realistic scenes. Each place should be simplified into a recognizable doodle icon. Use vibrant but harmonious colors, clean spacing, and a playful team-logo aesthetic. The final image should be dense and rich, but still readable. Include a small, clearly recognizable **flag doodle for every country represented** somewhere in the composition: **Mexico, Brazil, Canada, United States, India, Nigeria, Chile, China, Malaysia, and Hong Kong**. The flags should be integrated naturally as small waving flags, sticker badges, banners, pins, or decorative pennants near the related landmarks. Also include the **Maryland state flag** near the Washington, DC / Maryland section. Keep all flags simplified but identifiable. Include these doodle icons: **Mexico** Aztec calendar, Angel of Independence, Xochimilco chinampa with flowers and canals, plus a small Mexican flag. **Belo Horizonte, Brazil** Igreja da Pampulha with its curved modernist church shape, plus a small Brazilian flag. **Maranhão, Brazil** Bumba Meu Boi, shown as a spectacular festive mythical ox with decorative patterns, ribbons, embroidery-like details, music, dance, and theatrical folk-performance energy. It should feel rooted in Maranhão’s June festivals, like a vibrant street celebration in São Luís, blending music, dance, theater, and visual art around the traditional mythical ox story of death and resurrection. Add a small Brazilian flag nearby. **Brazil — Cordel / Xilogravura Style Accent** Include a small inset or decorative sticker element inspired by **xilogravura de cordel**, the traditional Brazilian folk-literature woodcut style. Make it rustic, high-contrast, bold, and graphic, like a simplified black-and-white or limited-color woodcut print that captures the beauty of regional storytelling and festival imagery. This should be a style accent within the doodle badge, not the entire image style. **Lençóis Maranhenses, Brazil** Simplify Lençóis Maranhenses National Park into a doodle icon of sweeping white sand dunes with turquoise-blue rainwater lagoons, using curved lines and playful color blocks. **Canada** Maple leaves, CN Tower, Banff mountain peaks, Vancouver Canucks-inspired orca breaching through a stylized “C,” green Lions Gate Bridge with Stanley Park greenery and North Shore mountains, Stanley Park Totem Poles, and the pixelated Digital Orca sculpture, plus a small Canadian flag. **Ottawa, Canada** A winter skating scene on the Rideau Canal and the Canadian Parliament Building, simplified as cheerful doodle icons, with the Canadian flag visible near the Parliament Building. **Atlanta, USA** One Atlantic Center “pencil building,” Mercedes-Benz Stadium, and simplified Atlanta downtown skyline, plus a small United States flag. **Princeton, USA** Ivy-covered Princeton University-style collegiate building and Princeton Junction Fire Department, plus a small United States flag. **Los Angeles, USA** Hollywood Sign on green-brown hills, with the word **“HOLLYWOOD”** clearly readable, plus a small United States flag. **Washington, DC / Maryland, USA** Lincoln Memorial, Reflecting Pool, a blue crab, a small United States flag, and the Maryland state flag with its distinctive red, white, black, and gold pattern. **Delhi, India** India Gate, shown as a prominent national monument and war memorial in the heart of New Delhi, simplified into a recognizable doodle landmark, plus a small Indian flag. **Nigeria** A proud eagle representing Nigeria’s soccer team mascot and the Lekki-Ikoyi Bridge, simplified as bold, energetic doodle icons, plus a small Nigerian flag. **Chile** Cerro San Cristóbal in Santiago with the white Virgin Mary statue, city skyline, and the **Andes Mountains** behind it. Add snowy Santiago apartment-and-office-view mountains. Include Valparaíso funicular elevators on colorful steep hills, Torres del Paine granite towers with turquoise lake, El Tatio geysers with desert steam, Easter Island moai statues, Colchagua vineyard grapevines, Atacama Desert observatory under a Milky Way sky, Chillán ski center with snowy slopes, and a completo italiano hot dog with tomato, avocado, and mayonnaise, plus a small Chilean flag. **Xi’an, China** Terracotta Army soldiers, Bell Tower, Drum Tower, Xi’an city walls, Giant Wild Goose Pagoda, and Tang Dynasty Ever Bright City lights, plus a small Chinese flag. **Shenzhen, China** A spectacular drone light show above the Shenzhen Civic Center in Futian District, with synchronized illuminated skyscrapers, glowing geometric drone formations, and a futuristic skyline, simplified into colorful doodle shapes. Add a small Chinese flag nearby. **Malaysia** Kuala Lumpur Twin Towers as a clean, recognizable doodle landmark, plus a hibiscus flower representing Malaysia’s national flower. Add a small Malaysian flag nearby. **Hong Kong** Victoria Harbour with a simplified skyline, harbor water, and small boats, plus a playful dim sum icon such as bamboo steamers or dumplings. Add a small Hong Kong flag nearby. Composition instructions: Make the overall silhouette feel like a finished doodle badge. Put large landmarks around the outer ring and smaller icons in the gaps. Prioritize immediate recognizability for: CN Tower, Lions Gate Bridge, Hollywood Sign, Cerro San Cristóbal with the Andes Mountains, Valparaíso elevators, Torres del Paine, Easter Island moai, Atacama observatory, completo italiano, Angel of Independence, Terracotta soldiers, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Lincoln Memorial, Digital Orca, India Gate, Canadian Parliament Building, Rideau Canal skating, Lekki-Ikoyi Bridge, Nigerian eagle mascot, Bumba Meu Boi, Lençóis Maranhenses dunes and lagoons, Shenzhen Civic Center light show, KL Twin Towers, hibiscus flower, Victoria Harbour, dim sum, the country flags, and the Maryland flag. Use doodle-style simplification: expressive outlines, flat colors, minimal shading, hand-drawn texture, playful proportions, and small decorative filler elements. Keep every object illustrated, not photographic. Avoid photorealism, 3D rendering, realistic lighting, heavy shadows, cluttered overlapping, excessive tiny detail, messy text, distorted lettering, extra words, watermarks, and blank unused space. The final result should be a vibrant, charming, hand-drawn doodle-art global team logo celebrating city pride, collaboration, and quality.

Product Designer + CAD Designer Needed f.t: Product Designer + CAD Designer Needed for Premium Baseball-Shaped Trading Card Vault
Product Designer + CAD Designer Needed f.d: OVERVIEW We are developing a premium collectible sports product called the Baseball Vault. The Baseball Vault is a baseball-shaped collectible storage product designed to hold and protect trading cards. It combines sports memorabilia, premium collectible design, and practical card storage. We are looking for a designer who can help transform an early prototype concept into a polished, premium, manufacturable product. The ideal partner can support both: - Industrial / Product Design - CAD Development We are especially interested in someone who can improve the concept, not simply execute a predetermined design. ⸻ DESIGN FIRST, CAD SECOND We do not want to immediately jump into final CAD. The first phase of the project is design exploration. We want to review and approve the overall product direction before moving into detailed CAD development. We are looking for a designer who can contribute ideas, identify opportunities, challenge assumptions, and elevate the product experience. ⸻ PRODUCT CONCEPT The Baseball Vault is: - Shaped like a baseball - Designed to store trading cards (not baseballs) - Built as a two-piece shell - Intended to close with magnets - Designed with a clean opening experience - Intended to include a soft felt or felt-like interior - Potentially designed with interchangeable inserts for different card formats Potential card formats include: - Raw cards - Sleeved cards - Top loaders - Graded/slabbed cards The product should feel like: - A premium sports keepsake - A collectible object - A giftable product - A polished consumer product The product should not feel like: - A cheap plastic box - A generic 3D print - A novelty item - A basic storage container ⸻ BRAND CONCEPT We are building a premium sports-inspired consumer product brand. Our design direction is: - Premium but approachable - Collectible but functional - Sports-inspired without feeling like cheap fan merchandise - Modern - Tactile - Giftable - Display-worthy This product may become the first product in a broader product line and should help establish a recognizable design language and quality standard. We are not looking for a one-off novelty product. We want something that feels like the beginning of a premium consumer brand. Additional brand context can be shared with the selected designer if needed. ⸻ KEY DESIGN CHALLENGES 1. Premium Baseball Exterior The product should immediately read as a baseball while feeling refined, collectible, and premium. Areas to explore: - Baseball stitching interpretation - Seam placement - Leather-inspired surface treatments - Matte or tactile finishes - Sports memorabilia inspiration - Modern industrial design influences We are interested in solutions that evoke baseball leather without requiring actual leather materials. 2. Opening & Closure Experience The vault should feel satisfying and intentional to open and close. Areas to explore: - Magnetic closure - Hidden magnet integration - Finger/thumb access - Alignment systems - Closure feel - Tactile experience The opening experience should feel premium rather than purely functional. 3. Interior Experience The interior should feel protective and elevated. Areas to explore: - Felt or felt-like lining - Card presentation - Card accessibility - Interchangeable inserts - Support for multiple card formats - Premium presentation when opened We are interested in making the interior feel thoughtful and intentional rather than a simple storage cavity. 4. Manufacturability Initial production will likely utilize high-quality 3D printing. Future manufacturing methods may include: - FDM - MJF/SLS - Resin - Injection molding The design should consider: - Reducing support requirements - Reducing visible support scars - Minimizing post-processing labor - Improving print efficiency - Improving assembly efficiency - Scalability toward future manufacturing methods We are not looking for a design that only looks good in renders. It should be practical to prototype, print, assemble, and eventually manufacture. 5. Size & Efficiency The product should be as compact as reasonably possible while still supporting the intended card formats and maintaining a premium experience. Areas to consider: - Minimizing overall size - Minimizing material usage - Maximizing usable interior space - Comfortable handling - Efficient shipping footprint The goal is to create a product that feels intentional and efficient without feeling undersized. 6. Assembly The product may include: - Top shell - Bottom shell - Connector or hinge component - Magnets - Felt insert - Internal card insert The design should make assembly as simple and repeatable as possible. Areas to explore: - Self-aligning components - Magnet pockets - Felt installation - Insert installation - Reduced glue dependency - Assembly efficiency 7. Product Presentation & Unboxing We would like the designer to consider the overall customer experience, not just the object itself. Areas to explore: - Product presentation - Packaging concepts - Gift-worthy experience - Collector-oriented presentation - Branded insert concepts - Ways to make the product feel memorable and premium We are not necessarily looking for final packaging artwork, but we do want the product design to consider presentation and unboxing. 8. Design for Cost While the product should feel premium, we are also interested in solutions that support healthy manufacturing economics. Areas to consider: - Material efficiency - Print efficiency - Assembly efficiency - Packaging efficiency - Scalability We are interested in thoughtful tradeoffs that improve product viability without compromising the overall customer experience. ⸻ CORE DESIGN PHILOSOPHY The goal is not to maximize complexity. The goal is to maximize: - Perceived quality - Collectibility - Customer delight - Giftability - Manufacturability while keeping production and assembly as simple as possible. ⸻ PHASE 1 DELIVERABLE– DESIGN EXPLORATION Please propose approximately 2–3 design directions. Deliverables may include: - Concept sketches - Product renderings - Exterior concepts - Interior concepts - Opening/closure concepts - Material recommendations - Manufacturing recommendations - Packaging/presentation concepts - Suggestions for improving the overall product We want to approve the design direction before CAD development begins. ⸻ PHASE 2 DELIVERABLES - CAD DEVELOPMENT After design approval: - Production-ready CAD - STEP files - STL files - Organized source files - Separate printable/manufacturable components - Manufacturing recommendations - Print orientation recommendations where appropriate The CAD should be suitable for prototyping and future refinement. ⸻ IDEAL EXPERIENCE Strong candidates may have experience with: - Industrial design - Consumer products - Collectibles - Toys - Premium gift products - Sports products - Desk accessories - Design for manufacturing - CAD for physical products - 3D printed products Experience with magnets, hinges, packaging, collectibles, MJF/SLS, injection molding, or premium consumer products is a plus.

Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist N.t: Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist Needed for Influencer-Led App
Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist N.i: Hourly: $10.00 - $100.00 Intermediate Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs
Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist N.d: We are launching a new fitness app. The app is already close to launch. We are using a white-label app solution, and the app is expected to go live soon on the App Store and Google Play. We are not looking for an app developer at this stage. We are looking for an experienced Fitness App Launch & Growth Strategist who can help us successfully launch, activate, monetize, and retain users inside the app. Our team already has: -A positioned personal brand in the fitness space -An existing audience waiting for the app -Content and marketing experience -A white-label fitness app almost ready -A team that can execute content, community, and marketing What we need is someone who has real experience launching and growing fitness, wellness, coaching, habit, or subscription-based mobile apps. What we need help with We need a launch and growth expert to help us with: 1. Pre-launch readiness Review the app experience before launch Identify gaps in onboarding, activation, pricing, and user journey Help us define what must be ready before going public Create a launch checklist for App Store and Google Play Review app positioning, screenshots, descriptions, and store messaging 2. Launch strategy Build a clear launch plan for the first 30–60 days Help us convert Andreana Fitness’ audience into app users Design the launch campaign using Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, email, WhatsApp, and community Create a waitlist / early access / founding members strategy if needed Recommend how to structure the first offer 3. Monetization Recommend subscription structure Help define pricing, free trial, introductory offer, or founding member offer Review paywall and conversion flow Suggest upsell or retention opportunities 4. Activation and retention Improve the first-time user experience Define the first 7-day and 30-day user journey Recommend push notification strategy Recommend challenges, progress tracking, habit loops, community, and accountability mechanics Help reduce drop-off after download 5. Metrics and analytics Define the most important metrics to track from day one Help us understand what events we should measure Create a simple dashboard structure for the team Recommend weekly review process after launch 6. Team guidance Work with our Spanish-speaking team Help us translate strategy into clear execution tasks Guide our content, marketing, support, and app teams during launch Help us avoid common mistakes in fitness app launches Ideal candidate You should have experience with at least one of the following: Fitness apps Wellness apps Coaching apps Habit tracking apps Nutrition apps Subscription-based mobile apps Influencer-led app launches Mobile app growth App Store Optimization App onboarding and retention Consumer subscription products Spanish is strongly preferred. Our team communicates best in Spanish, although we can understand English. The ideal candidate should be comfortable working with a Spanish-speaking team. Deliverables For the first phase, we expect: App launch audit Pre-launch checklist 30–60 day launch strategy Monetization and pricing recommendations Onboarding and retention recommendations App Store / Google Play optimization recommendations Metrics and analytics plan Weekly action plan for the team Engagement We are open to: A fixed-price launch strategy sprint An hourly consulting engagement A short-term advisory role during the launch This could become a longer-term role if the launch goes well.

Food Technologist Needed for Confectione.t: Food Technologist Needed for Confectionery Coating Process
Food Technologist Needed for Confectione.i: Hourly Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Food Technologist Needed for Confectione.d: We are seeking an experienced food technologist with expertise in confectionery to help us develop a method for coating individual hazelnuts with a thin layer of pelamushi, a mixture of boiled grape juice and flour. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in food science and technology, particularly in coatings and confections, drying systems, anti sticking solutions. I am developing a modern snack product inspired by traditional Georgian churchkhela, redesigned into individually coated nuts suitable for loose packaging. Unlike traditional churchkhela (nuts threaded on a string), the goal is to produce separate coated nuts that can be packaged loose without sticking to each other or absorbing excess moisture. .Each product unit consists of a single hazelnut, coated with a thin, smooth fruit-based layer (initially grape-based pelamushi), forming a uniform, round coating around each nut. The finished product will be packaged loose, with approximately 30-50 coated hazelnuts per pouch, including mixed fruit flavors maybe artificial flavouring will be also needed and natural colors (derived from different fruit juices) Key Requirements & Challenges: . Coating must be: ◦ thin, smooth, and visually uniform o non-sticky even when multiple pieces are in contact ○ stable inside sealed packaging . Initial variants: ◦ Grape juice (sugar-free) ◦ Future fruit variants (apple, pomegranate, berry blends), some may require added sugar . Clean-label approach preferred • No moisture migration or surface sweating . Shelf-stable at room *-temperature. expected Outcome: • Optimized formulation for grape-based coating . Recommended coating and drying process . Guidance on anti-sticking strategy . Packaging considerations for moisture control . Feasibility assessment for scale-up. Collaboration Format: . R&D consultation . Recipe/formulation development . Process design guidance (Remote collaboration is acceptable)

Logo design.d: Hello, I am looking for a logo design for my new company called Gloss Architect. Here are the branding details: Color Palette: Primary: Obsidian Black (Deep, satin black) – the foundation of everything. Accent 1: Liquid Silver (Chrome/Silver) – symbolizing paintwork in pristine condition. Accent 2: Blueprint Blue (Deep, technical blue) – a reference to architectural blueprints, adding a professional touch. Logo Concept: I envision a logo that merges the world of automotive detailing with architecture: The Symbol (Signet): A stylized letter „A” where the sides resemble a car hood or the roofline of a modern building, intersected by a horizontal line representing a light reflection (the so-called "horizon line"). Layout: I need a horizontal version (text next to the symbol) for elegant signage/facades, and a vertical version (symbol above the text) for apparel and social media. I have a preliminary logo concept generated by Gemini. Please use it as a reference and refine it to be more aesthetically polished. I need the final deliverable in a professional vector format. Best regards."
