Catalog includes sleep aids, sinus/pain relief, gummies, and softgels. We're scaling fast and need listings that look as premium as the products. --- **The Role** This is an **AI-native** role — NOT traditional Photoshop work. You'll use AI image generators (Midjourney, Ideogram, Flux, GPT-4o image, Firefly, etc.) as your primary production engine for speed and consistency, and use Photoshop/Figma only for compositing and finishing. Ongoing work as we expand and refresh our catalog. --- **What You'll Do** - Build A+ Content modules (A+ Premium) and full listing image stacks (main through image 6) on AI-generated visuals - Prompt-engineer for consistent, photorealistic, on-brand product and lifestyle imagery across many SKUs - Optimize for conversion — benefit-first hierarchy, mobile-first layout - Audit and refresh underperforming listings --- **You Must Have** - Portfolio of Amazon A+ / listing images for supplement, OTC, or health products that **includes AI-generated assets** (required to be considered) - Hands-on proficiency with 2+ AI image tools (Midjourney, Ideogram, Flux, GPT-4o image, Firefly, etc.) - Conversion-focused design sense (hierarchy, buyer psychology, mobile-first) - Photoshop or Figma for compositing, text precision, and final QA - Reliable turnaround and clear communication --- **Nice to Have** - Premium A+ Content and Brand Registry experience - AI video generation (Runway, Kling, Sora) --- **Scope** Starting with a full A+ + image stack refresh for ~10–14 ASINs, then ongoing launches and seasonal updates. Long-term partnership for the right person. --- **To Apply, Include:** 1. Portfolio showing **AI-generated** A+ / listing work for supplement or OTC/health products (ASIN links or samples) 2. A quick overview of your AI workflow and how you keep brand/product consistency across SKUs 3. Typical turnaround and price for a full A+ + listing image package **No relevant AI portfolio = not reviewed.**

Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed for a.t: Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed for a LinkedIn-like Professional Platform
Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed for a.d: # Senior Full-Stack Developer Needed for a LinkedIn-like Professional Platform **C#/.NET, Next.js/React, PostgreSQL, AWS** We are looking for an experienced senior full-stack developer or a small senior team to build a LinkedIn-like professional platform. This will be a milestone-based project. We will start with the first milestone only. If the first milestone is delivered with good quality, clean code, clear communication, and a solid technical foundation, we will continue with the remaining scope. ## Preferred Tech Stack * Backend: C# / .NET * Frontend: Next.js, React, or a similar modern frontend framework * Database: PostgreSQL on Amazon RDS * Cloud: AWS * Object/file storage: AWS S3 or another suitable AWS-native solution * Deployment: AWS-based production deployment ## Timeline The first milestone should be completed in **1 week**. The client target for the full project is **June 25**. This may be aggressive, so please be honest in your proposal. If you think the timeline is not realistic, clearly explain what can realistically be delivered by that date. ## Project Summary The product is a professional networking and company experience platform. It includes individual users, company accounts, user/company profiles, content sharing, professional experience/review flows, job-related flows, interview-related flows, CV/portfolio features, academy/career content, messaging/networking features, and an admin panel. The exact business details will be shared later if needed. For now, the scope below should be enough for an experienced developer to estimate the project and propose a technical solution. --- # Milestone 1 — Architecture Foundation, Registration, and Profiles **Target duration: 1 week** This is the trial milestone. ## Scope ### Technical Foundation * Create backend project structure * Create frontend project structure * Define initial architecture and module boundaries * Set up PostgreSQL database foundation * Prepare initial database schema and migrations * Set up environment configuration * Add basic logging/error-handling structure * Prepare local development setup * Prepare initial AWS deployment approach or basic deployment ### Authentication and Authorization * Individual user registration * Company registration * Login/logout flow * Password reset foundation * Role foundation: * Individual user * Company user * Admin * Secure authentication/authorization approach should be proposed and implemented in a basic form ### User Profile * Create and edit individual profile * Basic profile information: * Name * Title/profession * City/location * Sector * Contact preferences * Short bio * Profile photo upload foundation * Profile completion percentage foundation * Public profile view foundation * Open/anonymous identity concept foundation ### Company Profile * Create and edit company profile * Company registration data * Company contact data * Company verification foundation * Company profile page foundation * Company logo/image upload foundation ### Expected Deliverables * Working frontend and backend * User registration/login * Company registration/login * User profile create/edit * Company profile create/edit * PostgreSQL schema foundation * Initial tests * Initial deployment plan * Short documentation: * How to run locally * How to deploy * Architecture overview * What is included/excluded in Milestone 1 After this milestone, the code quality, architecture, maintainability, and communication will be reviewed before continuing. --- # Milestone 2 — Core User Platform ## Scope ### Home / Feed * Main logged-in homepage * Feed layout * Create post * Create professional experience/review post * Anonymous or public sharing option * Image attachment foundation * Poll creation foundation * Feed categories/tabs * Like/useful action * Commenting * Replying to comments * Save/bookmark content * Share/copy link actions * Report content flow * Follow user/company * Add user to network ### Search * Global search page * Search by: * Companies * People * Content * Topics * Search result tabs * Basic search filters * Empty state and loading state ### Professional Experiences / Reviews * Experience listing page * Experience detail foundation * Write experience flow * Multi-step experience submission: * Experience details * Company selection * Supporting document upload foundation * Final publish/submit step * Anonymous/public experience option * Experience status tracking: * Published * Pending review * Rejected * Notified to company * Edit rejected experience * Resubmit experience * Delete experience * Appeal/review objection flow * “Was your issue resolved?” style feedback flow * Support/useful count * Comments on experiences * Company reply foundation * Moderation status foundation ### Company / Experience Discovery * Company/experience listing page * Filters: * Category * Sector * Department * Experience sentiment/rating * Work model * Company-related experience cards * Pagination or “load more” * Advertisement/banner placeholder support ### Saved Items * Saved posts * Saved job posts * Saved companies * Empty states for each section ### Comments and User Activity * User’s commented content page * Filter comments by status/type * Edit comment * Hide/deactivate comment * View related content * Reference comment foundation ### Networking * My network page * Connections list * Incoming connection requests * Sent requests * Followed users/companies * Suggested people * Follow/unfollow * Add/remove connection * Basic pagination/filtering ### Messaging * Messages page * Message list * Unread count * Conversation detail * Send message * Basic message categories: * Platform messages * Company messages * Individual messages * Load older messages * Basic notification count foundation ### Jobs * Job listing page * Filters: * City * Remote/on-site/hybrid * Work type * Sector * Position level * Department * Experience level * Education level * Sorting: * Newest * Most applied * Salary high-to-low, if available * Job cards * Apply button * Applied status * Job detail foundation * Saved job foundation ### Applications * My applications page * Application status tracking: * Pending * Reviewing * Interview * Rejected * Completed/resulted * Application count summary * Application list filters ### Interview Flow * My interviews page * Interview list * Verified interview code concept * Unverified interview record * Interview detail foundation * Interview evaluation form * Rating questions * Open or anonymous interview experience sharing option * Interview code status foundation * Rule foundation for interview-code-related restrictions ### Interview Codes * My codes page * Active codes * Requested codes * Used codes * Under-review codes * Code request flow foundation * Code validation foundation * Basic status tracking ### CV / Resume / Cover Letter / Video CV * My resumes page * Create resume foundation * View/edit/copy/download/delete resume * CV score/analysis placeholder * Video CV list * Video CV upload/playback foundation * Video CV create/edit/share/delete foundation * Cover letter list * Create/edit/copy/delete cover letter * File upload/download foundation * CV permission foundation ### Portfolio * Portfolio and documents page * Add portfolio item * Add project * Add certificate * Add document * Upload file/image * Featured portfolio items * Document list * Portfolio visibility foundation * Delete/edit portfolio items ### Career Center * Career goal selection * Service recommendation flow * Pre-application/payment-information foundation * Campaign/event code foundation * Career service list: * CV & LinkedIn optimization * Interview preparation * Interview simulation * Career consulting * Assessment center preparation * Presentation/communication support * Leadership/management preparation * Career center application foundation ### Academy / Learning Area * Academy landing page * Modules list * Videos list * Video player foundation * Progress tracking foundation * Recommended video/module * Continue watching section * Module detail * Completion status ### Blog / FAQ / Contact / Static Pages * Blog listing page * Blog categories * Blog detail foundation * FAQ page with categories * Contact page * Contact form * Static/legal pages foundation: * Terms * Privacy/KVKK * Cookie policy * User agreements --- # Milestone 3 — Admin Panel The admin panel is required after the first milestone. ## Company Management * List companies * View company detail * Add new company * Edit company * Delete company * Approve/reject company registration * Reset company password * Manage company verification status * View company-related content ## User Management * List members/users * View user detail * Edit user status * Deactivate/reactivate user * Send message to user * View user activity summary * Manage user roles where needed ## Content / Experience Management * List experiences/reviews * View experience detail * Edit experience * Delete experience * Deactivate experience * Approve/reject experience * Manage experience status * View experience comments * View company replies * Edit/delete/deactivate comments * Edit/delete/deactivate company replies ## Experience Category Management * List experience categories * Add category * Edit category * Delete category * Activate/deactivate category ## Job Management * List job posts * View job post detail * Delete job post * Manage job visibility/status ## Blog Management * Add blog content * Edit blog content * Delete blog content * Manage blog categories if needed ## Sector and Definition Management * Add/edit/delete sectors * Add/edit/delete departments if needed * Add/edit/delete position levels if needed * Manage other lookup/reference data ## Interview Management * Add/edit/delete interview evaluation questions * Manage interview question categories * View interview evaluations if required * Manage interview code-related records if required ## Academy Management * Add/edit/delete academy categories * Add/edit/delete academy modules * Add/edit/delete academy videos * Add/edit/delete video resources * Manage video order and visibility * Manage module status ## Badge Management * Add/edit/delete badges * Assign badges to users * Remove badges from users ## Career Package Management * Add/edit/delete career packages * Assign career packages to users * Manage package status ## Admin User Management * Create admin users * Edit admin users * Delete admin users * Role-based admin authorization * Admin activity/audit log foundation ## Dashboard * Dashboard with basic metrics: * Total users * Total companies * Total experiences/reviews * Pending approvals * Job post count * Blog/content count * Messages or contact requests count * Academy/video counts if needed * Basic charts or summary cards * Recent activity list ## Messaging / Support Management * View incoming messages/contact requests * Send messages to users * View message status * Basic support inbox foundation --- # Milestone 4 — Migration, Production Readiness, Testing, and AWS Deployment ## Data Migration The old system uses MS SQL. Existing users and companies must be migrated to the new PostgreSQL/RDS system. Scope: * Analyze old MS SQL schema * Map old data to new PostgreSQL schema * Create migration scripts * Migrate users * Migrate companies * Validate migrated data * Handle duplicate/invalid records * Prepare rollback or re-run strategy * Document the migration process ## AWS Deployment Please propose a cost-effective AWS architecture. Expected areas: * Application hosting * PostgreSQL on Amazon RDS * Object/file storage * CDN if needed * Logging and monitoring * Secrets management * Backup strategy * SSL/domain setup * CI/CD approach The AWS solution should meet the target requirements with minimum reasonable cost. ## Quality Requirements The system should be: * Observable * Monitorable * Fault-tolerant enough for the expected scale * Secure enough for production use * Maintainable * Modular * Scalable enough for future growth * Cost-effective on AWS ## Performance Target Initial expected load: * Around 0–5000 simultaneous users * Normal API requests should ideally respond under 200ms where realistic Please explain your assumptions. If you think this target requires additional infrastructure, caching, load testing, or scope changes, mention it clearly. ## Security Requirements Please explain how you would handle: * Authentication * Authorization * Role-based access control * Admin security * Password reset * Secure file upload * Basic rate limiting * Input validation * Protection against common web vulnerabilities * Secrets management * Audit logging for critical admin actions ## Observability Requirements Please explain how you would handle: * Structured logging * Error tracking * Metrics * Health checks * Performance monitoring * Database monitoring * Alerts * Request tracing if applicable ## Maintainability Requirements Please explain how you would keep the code maintainable. Expected topics: * Architecture style * Module boundaries * Clean code conventions * Separation of concerns * Domain/business logic organization * Reusable frontend components * API contract structure * Error handling strategy * Validation strategy * Documentation * Code review expectations ## Testing Requirements Expected: * More than 80% test coverage where practical * Unit tests * Integration tests * Database-related integration tests * Preferably E2E tests for critical business flows Important business flows to test: * User registration/login * Company registration/login * User profile update * Company profile update * Experience submission * Experience approval/rejection flow * Commenting * Job application * Admin approval flows * Data migration validation --- # What to Include in Your Proposal Please do not send a generic proposal. Include: 1. Your recommended architecture 2. Why you chose that architecture 3. Which AWS components you would use and why 4. How you would keep AWS cost low 5. How you would implement authentication and authorization 6. How you would design PostgreSQL/RDS usage 7. How you would handle MS SQL to PostgreSQL migration 8. How you would handle file/object storage 9. How you would make the system observable and monitorable 10. How you would make the system fault-tolerant 11. How you would make the code maintainable 12. Your testing strategy 13. Your plan for Milestone 1 14. What you can realistically deliver by June 25 15. Whether the full timeline is realistic 16. Similar projects you have built before Please apply only if you can work seriously, communicate clearly, and deliver production-quality code.

Art Editing Assistant — Photoshop, Topaz.t: Art Editing Assistant — Photoshop, Topaz Photo AI & ON1 Resize | Ongoing Work
Art Editing Assistant — Photoshop, Topaz.d: We are seeking a reliable, detail-oriented editor to prepare original digital artwork for large-format print production and e-commerce. All work follows a comprehensive, well-documented SOP. The right candidate is disciplined enough to follow a defined process exactly, and experienced enough to know when to pause, ask a question, or flag something before moving forward. Editors who communicate proactively and take ownership of their work are a great fit here. A full SOP is provided before work begins. For candidates who prefer it, we are also happy to do a brief orientation call to get acquainted, answer any questions, and make sure everything is clear before the first task. The Work: This role has two stages, each with its own rate. Stage 1 — Editing Pipeline: $2 per artwork Each artwork goes through a full editing pipeline: AI upscaling in Topaz Photo AI, set image reconstruction in Photoshop, and final export in multiple formats. The $2 rate covers the complete pipeline for one artwork — from raw original through all final exported files. Exporting multiple file formats from one artwork does not count as multiple artworks. The reconstruction stage is the most involved part of the work. It requires identifying how the original artwork was composed, extracting the correct section, and rebuilding a clean symmetrical composition using the upscaled original as source material. Precise alignment is essential. Each image also requires embedded text that blends seamlessly with the artwork. Stage 2 — Mockup Creation: $12 per artwork Once an artwork is fully exported, mockups are created across 8 merchandise products and 13 wall art products using Printify, Prodigi, Canvy, and Photoshop. The $12 rate covers all mockups for one artwork. Some products are straightforward — upload the file and save the mockup. Others require creative judgment, such as selecting room colors and compositions in Canvy, or editing PSD templates for cushion and mug set products. All files are stored and shared via Box. Editors will need a Box account to access source files and submit completed work. Trial Structure: Editing trial: 4 artworks ($8) — editing pipeline only Mockup trial: 1 artwork ($12) — follows once editing trial is approved First milestone after trial approval: to be determined Ongoing: Many artworks are available for verified editors Payment: Released upon quality-approved delivery Active Editing Time: Editing pipeline: approximately 12 minutes per artwork, not including render and loading time. Mockup creation: approximately 1.5 hours per artwork across all 21 products. System Requirements: All requirements are mandatory. Please do not apply if your system does not meet the minimum specifications. RAM: 32 GB minimum (64 GB preferred) GPU: Dedicated graphics card with 4 GB+ VRAM Storage: SSD required (NVMe preferred) Software: Photoshop, Topaz Photo AI, and ON1 Resize OS: Windows 10/11 or macOS 11+ What We're Looking For: Strong Photoshop skills — layer management, guides, precise alignment, and symmetrical composition Comfortable working with very large files Meticulous with file naming, versioning, and folder organization Professional and communicative — asks questions when something is unclear Able to make creative decisions for mockup room styling and product presentation Trustworthy and discreet with proprietary artwork Experience with print production is a plus but not required Ownership & Confidentiality: All edited files remain the exclusive property of our studio. By accepting this project, the contractor agrees not to share, reuse, reproduce, or distribute any artwork or project files in any form.

communicate clearly with project teams • Experience reviewing construction documents and coordinating with other disciplines • Familiarity with Revit and AutoCAD workflows • Strong understanding of applicable mechanical, plumbing, energy, and building codes • Consulting engineering experience preferred • Ability to support projects on an as-needed, deadline-driven basis Preferred Qualifications • PE license in Pennsylvania or surrounding states preferred • Multi-state PE licensure is a plus • Experience with healthcare, higher education, government, transportation, or infrastructure-related projects is a plus • Experience with energy-efficient design, sustainable design, LEED, or building performance analysis is a plus • Ability to provide QA/QC review and redline drawings is strongly preferred Important Notes This opportunity is intended for freelance, 1099, or project-based support. LLC Registration Website Developer.d: LLC CoPilot is a side hustle website that helps people form LLCs in multiple U.S. states. Think a leaner, faster version of LegalZoom — customers select a state, review filing requirements, fill out a form, pay, and we handle the filing manually on the backend. The good news: the hard part is already done. - Full landing page design is complete (HTML/CSS) - Multi-step LLC filing app is built (state selector, intake form, order review flow) - All 6 state data sets are populated (VA, MD, DC, TX, FL, NY) - Complete task list and spec is ready for you Your job is purely integration and backend — wiring everything together so it works in production. --- WHAT NEEDS TO BE BUILT Phase 1 — Supabase database setup (~3–4 hrs) - Create orders, customers, states, and renewals tables - Set up Row Level Security (RLS) policies - Seed state data from existing dataset Phase 2 — Stripe integration (~4–5 hrs) - Wire Stripe Checkout for 3 plans: Basic ($149), Standard ($249), Premium ($399) - Set up recurring price for annual renewal add-on ($49/yr) - Build and handle checkout.session.completed webhook - Enable Stripe Customer Portal for renewal management Phase 3 — Backend API endpoints (~4–5 hrs) - POST /api/create-checkout — creates Stripe session from form submission - POST /api/webhook — confirms payment, saves order to Supabase, triggers emails - GET /api/orders — fetches all orders for admin view - PATCH /api/orders/:id — updates order status (pending → filed → complete) - GET /api/renewals/due — fetches upcoming renewal deadlines Phase 4 — Resend email setup (~3–4 hrs) - Customer confirmation email (order details + next steps) - Internal new order alert email (notifies us) - Status update email (when order marked as filed) - Completion email with LLC documents attached - Renewal reminder email (triggered 60 days before annual report due date) Phase 5 — Frontend wiring (~3–4 hrs) - Connect landing page CTAs to the filing app - Replace mock submit button with real Stripe checkout redirect - Add post-payment success page - Add loading states, form validation, and error handling Phase 6 — Admin dashboard (~5–6 hrs) - Password-protected /admin page - Orders table: status, state, plan, customer name, date filed - Status update controls (pending → filed → complete) - Document upload to attach filed LLC docs (triggers completion email) - Renewals view sorted by upcoming due date - Basic revenue summary (total orders, MRR, orders by state) Phase 7 — Vercel deployment + domain (~1–2 hrs) - Deploy to Vercel connected to GitHub repo - Configure all environment variables in Vercel dashboard - Connect custom domain and verify HTTPS - End-to-end test in Stripe test mode Phase 8 — QA + bug fixes (~3–4 hrs) - Full flow test: form → payment → email → admin dashboard - Mobile responsiveness check - Cross-browser check --- TECH STACK - Frontend: HTML/CSS/JS (existing) or Next.js if preferred - Database: Supabase (Postgres) - Payments: Stripe Checkout + Webhooks - Email: Resend - Hosting: Vercel - Auth (admin): Supabase Auth or simple env-variable protected route --- WHAT I'M LOOKING FOR - 2+ years of full-stack experience - Demonstrated Stripe integration in your portfolio (this is non-negotiable — please share a live example) - Comfortable with Supabase or similar Postgres/BaaS platforms - Can work independently with minimal hand-holding — the spec is detailed, you should be able to run with it - Clear English communication and regular progress updates (every 2 days minimum) - Available to start within 1 week --- HOW TO APPLY Please include the following in your proposal or I will not review it: 1. A link to a live project where you integrated Stripe payments 2. Your experience with Supabase or Postgres 3. Confirm you've read the spec — tell me which phase you'd start with and why 4. Your realistic timeline to complete all 8 phases 5. Your fixed-price bid Generic proposals will be declined. I'm looking for someone who has read this post, understands the scope, and can hit the ground running. --- NICE TO HAVE (not required) - Experience with Next.js App Router - Familiarity with U.S. LLC/legal filing services (LegalZoom, ZenBusiness, etc.) - Interest in an ongoing retainer ($100–$150/mo) for maintenance and adding new states post-launch --- This is a well-scoped project with a clear spec, existing UI, and a straightforward tech stack. If you're organized, communicate well, and know your way around Stripe and Supabase — this should be smooth. Looking forward to your proposal.

Our goal is to partner with local businesses throughout **Prince George's County, Charles County, and St. Mary's County, Maryland** by securing: * Souvenir Journal Advertisements * Event Sponsorships * Financial Donations * Scholarship Program Support We are looking for a proactive professional who can research businesses, build a quality database, conduct outreach, manage follow-ups, and track responses. --- ## Scope of Work ### Phase 1: Business Database Creation Develop a database containing a minimum of **500 qualified businesses** throughout: * Prince George's County, MD * Charles County, MD * St. Mary's County, MD The database should include: * Business Name * Industry/Business Category * Contact Person (if available) * Email Address * Phone Number * Website * Physical Address * Social Media Links * Outreach Status * Follow-Up Dates * Notes Section Businesses should be organized by industry type such as: * Restaurants * Automotive * Medical & Dental * Real Estate * Insurance * Financial Services * Construction * Home Improvement * Retail * Legal Services * Professional Services * Churches * Event Venues * Fitness Centers * Nonprofits * Government Contractors * Other Local Businesses --- ### Phase 2: Email Outreach Campaign The selected freelancer will: * Send professional sponsorship solicitation emails * Introduce Bee Hive Lodge No. 66 and our Scholarship Program * Request souvenir journal advertisements * Request sponsorship opportunities * Request financial donations * Attach the event flyer and souvenir journal advertisement form * Track all outreach efforts and responses --- ### Phase 3: Follow-Up Management The selected freelancer will: * Send follow-up emails every 3–5 days * Maintain accurate outreach records * Update response statuses * Track interested businesses * Maintain communication logs --- ### Phase 4: Response & Thank You Communications For businesses that respond positively: * Send thank-you emails * Provide sponsorship and advertising information * Track commitments and payments * Maintain detailed records --- ## Deliverables * Database of 500+ businesses * Categorized by industry * Outreach email templates * Follow-up email templates * Thank-you email templates * Weekly progress reports * CRM-style tracking spreadsheet * Complete outreach history --- ## Ideal Candidate We are seeking someone with experience in: * Lead Generation * Business Research * Contact List Building * Email Marketing * Sponsorship Acquisition * Fundraising Campaigns * CRM Management * Community Outreach * B2B Sales Development Experience with the following tools is preferred: * Microsoft Excel * Google Sheets * HubSpot * Mailchimp * Apollo.io * Instantly.ai * Hunter.io * LinkedIn Sales Navigator --- ## Proposal Requirements Please include: 1. Examples of similar projects you have completed. 2. Experience with fundraising, sponsorship, or nonprofit outreach. 3. Tools you use to build and verify business contact lists. 4. Estimated timeline for completing the database and launching outreach. 5. Your proposed pricing structure (hourly or fixed-price). Our goal is to build long-term relationships with local businesses while generating support for Bee Hive Lodge No. 66's Scholarship Program and Master Mason Ball. We are looking for a reliable professional who can begin immediately and help maximize outreach before our July 18, 2026 event.

We have a strong concept, loyal customer base, and growing team, but many of our operational systems, training processes, and leadership structures need to be formalized and implemented. Current Challenges * Operational knowledge and standards live primarily with the owner * Need stronger leadership development for managers and shift leads * Inconsistent training and onboarding processes * SOPs and operational systems need to be documented and implemented * Need stronger accountability systems and role clarity * Owner remains a bottleneck for too many decisions * Need to reduce owner dependence while maintaining culture and guest experience What I’m Looking For A consultant who can help: * Assess current operations * Identify operational bottlenecks and blind spots * Develop leadership and accountability systems * Create manager and shift lead scorecards * Build onboarding and training frameworks * Help document and implement key operational SOPs * Establish management meeting cadence and communication systems * Guide implementation, not just provide recommendations Ideal Background Preferred candidates will have experience as: * Director of Operations * Regional Manager * Area Director * Multi-Unit Restaurant Leader * Restaurant COO * Hospitality Operations Consultant * Franchise Operations Leader Experience in fast-casual, pizza, hospitality, restaurant groups, or franchise operations is highly preferred. I am specifically looking for someone who can bring proven operational frameworks, leadership systems, and restaurant experience, then help customize and implement those systems within my business.

Backend + integration to launch a finish.t: Backend + integration to launch a finished frontend restaurant deals app
Backend + integration to launch a finish.d: Restaurant & Café Deals App — Backend + UI Polish | تطبيق عروض مطاعم — ربط باك إند + تلميع واجهة ═══════════════════════════════ 🇬🇧 ENGLISH ═══════════════════════════════ THE IDEA: A Tinder-style deals discovery app for restaurants and cafés in Saudi Arabia. Users swipe right on deals they like (saved to wallet), swipe left to skip. At the restaurant, the cashier scans the customer's QR code or enters a short code (FL-XXXX) — discount applied instantly. No vouchers, no entering phone numbers. Restaurants control everything: discount % (5–50%), quantity, and off-peak time window. Revenue model: merchants pay to boost their card to the top of the deck + in-app ads. ALREADY BUILT (live demo available): • Consumer app: phone+OTP login, location-based swipe deck (4 card types: offer, sponsored, quiz-unlock, brand deal), wallet with QR + short code • Cashier portal: scan/enter code → confirm redemption (no customer phone exposed) • Merchant dashboard: manage offers, priority boost (top listing), redemption log, stats • Full Arabic RTL design, Tajawal font, lime-green brand identity (~70% complete) • Developer handover doc: data model, API endpoints, business rules, security notes WHAT WE NEED: 1. UI POLISH — Refine and complete existing design. Smooth animations, micro-interactions, mobile-native feel. NOT a redesign — elevate what's there. 2. BACKEND — Supabase or Firebase, real phone OTP (Taqnyat), replace localStorage via one-file API swap, QR generation + scanning, push notifications, merchant prepaid wallet with auto-deducted commission per redemption. 3. PWA + optional Capacitor wrap for iOS/Android. 4. PDPL compliance: consent, deletion, audit log, no raw phone numbers to merchants. 5. Deploy (Cloudflare/Vercel) + handover docs + walkthrough. TO APPLY: • 2–3 apps you shipped with: auth + real-time data + admin dashboard (links required) • Fixed price + timeline • Recommended stack + one thing you'd improve in the current design • Gulf/Saudi market experience is a plus Budget: Fixed. Timeline: 3–5 weeks. Codebase + live demo shared with shortlisted candidates only. ═══════════════════════════════ 🇸🇦 العربي ═══════════════════════════════ الفكرة: تطبيق اكتشاف عروض للمطاعم والكافيهات في السعودية بأسلوب السحب (مثل تيندر). المستخدم يسحب يمين على العرض اللي يعجبه (يُحفظ في المحفظة)، يسار لو ما يناسبه. عند المطعم، الكاشير يمسح QR العميل أو يدخل كود قصير (FL-XXXX) — الخصم يُطبَّق فوراً. بدون قسائم ورقية، وبدون إدخال رقم جوال العميل. المطعم يتحكّم بكل شي: نسبة الخصم (٥–٥٠٪)، الكمية، والنافذة الزمنية (أوقات الركود). نموذج الدخل: التجار يدفعون للصدارة (توب لستينق) + إعلانات داخل التطبيق. ما تم بناؤه (نموذج حي جاهز): • تطبيق العميل: دخول بالجوال+OTP، مكدّس سحب مبني على الموقع (٤ أنواع كروت: عرض، شريك، كويز يفتح خصماً، براند خارجي)، محفظة بـQR + كود قصير • بوابة الكاشير: مسح/إدخال الكود ← تأكيد الاستخدام (بدون كشف رقم جوال العميل) • لوحة الإدارة: إدارة العروض، رفع الأولوية (توب لستينق)، سجل الاستخدام، إحصائيات • تصميم عربي RTL كامل، خط تجوال، هوية ليمونية (~٧٠٪ مكتمل) • وثيقة تسليم للمطوّر: نموذج البيانات، API endpoints، قواعد العمل، الأمان المطلوب: ١. تلميع الواجهة — إتمام التصميم الحالي وتحسينه: أنيميشن سلس، micro-interactions، تجربة موبايل احترافية. مو إعادة تصميم — تحسين وإتمام ما هو موجود. ٢. الباك إند — Supabase أو Firebase، OTP حقيقي (تقنيات)، ربط API بنقطة تبديل واحدة، توليد QR + مسح، إشعارات، محفظة تاجر مشحونة مع خصم عمولة تلقائي. ٣. PWA + تغليف اختياري لـiOS/Android بـCapacitor. ٤. امتثال PDPL: موافقة، حذف، سجل تدقيق، حماية رقم الجوال. ٥. نشر (Cloudflare/Vercel) + توثيق + فيديو تسليم. للتقديم: • ٢–٣ تطبيقات بنيتها تشمل: مصادقة + بيانات مباشرة + لوحة إدارة (روابط إلزامية) • سعر ثابت + جدول زمني • Stack المقترح + شي واحد تحسّنه بالتصميم الحالي • خبرة بالسوق السعودي أو الخليجي ميزة الميزانية: سعر ثابت. المدة: ٣–٥ أسابيع. الكود والنموذج الحي يُشاركان مع المرشّحين المختارين فقط.

Sports Branding Designer for Logo Refine.t: Sports Branding Designer for Logo Refinement
Sports Branding Designer for Logo Refine.d: We're looking for a talented Sports Branding Designer to refine and modernize an existing logo concept. This project focuses on enhancing the current design rather than a complete overhaul. Your expertise will help create a production-ready logo that embodies our brand's spirit. If you have a passion for sports branding and a keen eye for detail, we want to hear from you! Please include your portfolio showcasing relevant work in sports logos. What This Is I have an existing logo concept for Georgia Referee — a soccer referee organization operating under US Soccer — that I need taken to a professional, production-ready standard. This is not a vectorization-only job. I am hiring you for your design expertise to refine, polish, and where needed improve the current concept while preserving its core identity. The attached PNG is the reference design. Use it as the creative foundation, not a pixel-perfect spec. The Logo — What Works, What Needs Attention The overall concept is strong and should be preserved: a shield shape, the whistle and crossed referee flags as the central image, "GEORGIA REFEREE" typography, the Georgia peach at the base, and a red/white/blue stripe composition with a silver border. These are locked elements compositionally — do not remove or replace them. Beyond that, I am giving you genuine creative latitude to make this look great. Specific areas that need attention: Flags The crossed flags behind the whistle are the weakest element. The checkered pattern is inconsistent and the rendering feels rough. Please redraw these cleanly. The flag style should read clearly as referee/linesman flags — keep the checkered pattern, but execute it with precision and make the flags feel dynamic and intentional rather than incidental. Lower Composition The area between the whistle/flags and the peach at the bottom is cluttered. The diagonal stripe lines in that zone are competing with the central imagery rather than supporting it. Please find a cleaner solution that gives the peach room to breathe and creates a clear visual hierarchy from the whistle down to the peach. You have latitude here — if that means simplifying the stripe treatment, adjusting angles, or restructuring that section of the shield interior, propose it. Overall Rendering Quality The whistle and shield border have gradient and bevel work that is functional but not polished. Bring these up to a professional standard. The style should feel modern and sporty — think athletic badge, not clip art. Clean gradients, consistent light source, sharp edges where appropriate. Typography The "GEORGIA REFEREE" lettering works well overall. Minor refinements to tracking, weight, or the red stripe framing of "REFEREE" are fine if they improve the result — but do not change the font character or layout significantly without showing me first. Color The current palette is red, white, blue, and silver — a patriotic register that fits the US Soccer context. I am open to moderate color refinement: adjusted shades, better contrast ratios, a more considered palette that still reads as red/white/blue without being a literal flag reproduction. What I am not open to: a full recolor, removing the silver border treatment, or introducing colors that take it outside the patriotic/sporty register. As part of delivery, please document the final approved palette in Pantone, CMYK, RGB, and HEX. We do not have existing specs — you are establishing the standard. Compositional Changes You may propose compositional adjustments — whistle angle, stripe geometry, element spacing — if they improve the overall result. However, please do not implement significant compositional changes without showing me a concept first. A quick sketch or rough mockup for anything structural is fine and preferred over a surprise in the final file. While we have a strong existing concept, we are specifically looking for a designer's expertise. We welcome thoughtful improvements to geometry, spacing, line work, color palette, visual hierarchy, and overall execution. The goal is not strict recreation, but rather a refined, modern, production-ready version that preserves the identity and spirit of the current logo.

Cinematic Video Editor for Premium Suppl.t: Cinematic Video Editor for Premium Supplement Brand Spot (Transformation Story)
Cinematic Video Editor for Premium Suppl.d: We're RECALM (recalm.ch), a Swiss premium recovery supplement brand. We're producing a cinematic ~35-second brand spot that will run on gym screens (16:9) — and we want it to be the best supplement ad ever made. Not the loudest. The most felt. The bar is ESN / Gymshark / Nike level and beyond, executed with restraint instead of hype. The concept: a wordless transformation story. A man in his early 40s, unhappy with himself, takes the first step and rebuilds himself through consistency. The film travels from cold and desaturated to warm and golden as he transforms. It's pure cinematic B-roll — no voice-over, no on-screen text — until the end card, where the product appears with a discount code. Two things must come through crystal clear: the transformation, and the long-term success that RECALM enables. Before you apply: Do your own research on recalm.ch so you understand the brand and product. You should already understand supplement / fitness ad spots and the category. Bring your own ideas. We have a full story and shot list (see attached brief), but we want a pro's creative input to make it bigger. The brief is the foundation, not a cage — the only fixed pillars are the transformation and the long-term success with RECALM. Scope & craft: 16:9 master (~35s) + cutdowns (9:16, 1:1, 15s). Cinematic color grade (the cold→warm arc), sound design, tight narrative pacing, end-card motion graphics (logo, discount code, BRACK trust badge). We provide a full creative brief, brand assets, product images, brand colors, and reference clips as the editing spine. Footage approach to be discussed. A full creative brief is attached — please read it before applying. In your proposal, include: A portfolio reel of narrative / emotional edits (not just fast-cut hype reels). One example of your color grading and one of your sound design. Your timeline and price. Your honest take: is our story strong enough, and what would you add to make it elite? We're looking for a senior editor who treats this like a brand film, not a quick gig.

Logo for Yoga/Wellness brand.d: rand Creative Brief: Zennest of the Zen What: Zennest of the Zen is your antidote to burnout. See Instagram @zennestofthezen I’m creating a sanctuary for overstimulated minds - from calming studio classes to retreats with an elevated vibe, and personalized events like bachelorette parties and dog rescue fundraisers. Zennest of the Zen will extend beyond the mat, with merch that people will want to wear because it’s super cool, high-quality and grounded in purpose. I’m offering mentorship + yoga as an offering for stressed out startup people in silicon valley. Industry: Wellness, yoga, mentorship, merchandise Mission: I help stressed-out souls, especially professionals under pressure, reclaim peace through yoga, mindfulness, and movement. -Inner peace shouldn’t be your side hustle. -Wellness isn’t luxury - it’s your nervous system. Why: My rollercoaster journey and recovery from burnout inspired me to become a yoga instructor to help others reduce workplace stress through yoga and healthier habits. Yoga is for every body. I help people look good and feel great. How: Multiple offerings to generate revenue through multiple business lines. -Yoga classes in studio -Personal yoga instruction -Retreats -Mentorship -Online store Brand: Modern and deeply calming Carries soul but looks hot AF on a hoodie. Evokes a sense of sanctuary and maybe even some cheekiness This brand makes people say ‘I don’t even do yoga but I want this. I want to rep it, wear it, tell other people about it. Internal AI Knowledge & Proposal Copilot.i: Hourly: $65.00 - $118.00 Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
Internal AI Knowledge & Proposal Copilot.d: # Upwork RFP: Internal AI Knowledge & Proposal Copilot for General Contracting Firm ## Company Overview Pizzano Contractors is a commercial general contractor based in Alexandria, Virginia, serving clients across the Mid-Atlantic region. Our work includes commercial interiors, law firms, associations and nonprofits, government-leased projects, secure spaces, building repositioning, Corporate Services/small projects, and related commercial construction services. We are seeking an experienced AI developer, consultant, or small team to help us create an internal AI-powered knowledge and proposal tool for firm-wide use. The goal is to help our employees quickly access approved company information, locate forms and templates, draft client responses, support onboarding, and help our marketing team generate first-draft RFP/proposal responses using approved Pizzano Contractors content. ## Project Name Pizzano AI Knowledge & Proposal Copilot ## Project Objective We want to build a secure internal AI tool that allows employees to ask natural-language questions and receive accurate, source-backed answers based on approved company documents. The tool should help our team: * Find approved forms, templates, checklists, policies, and internal documents. * Search approved SharePoint or network-drive content intelligently. * Draft client-facing responses using approved Pizzano language. * Help employees understand Pizzano Contractors, our services, divisions, history, market sectors, project experience, and internal processes. * Upload an RFP and generate a first-draft proposal package using approved content. * Help marketing prepare cover letters, bios, relevant case studies, boilerplate responses, safety language, quality-control language, risk-management language, schedule-management language, cost-control language, and other standard proposal sections. This tool should be designed as an internal assistant and drafting tool. It should not send client communications or final proposals without human review. ## Primary Use Cases ### 1. Internal Knowledge Search Employees should be able to ask questions such as: * Where is the latest approved client satisfaction survey process? * Where can I find the approved subcontractor form? * What is our current project closeout process? * What is the approved language for Pizzano’s safety program? * What is our Corporate Services Division? * What are our main market sectors? * What projects have we completed for law firms? * What experience do we have with secure spaces or government-leased work? The system should return a clear answer and cite or link back to the source document whenever possible. ### 2. Approved Forms and Templates Finder The system should help employees find the correct, most current version of internal documents, including but not limited to: * Forms * Templates * Checklists * Proposal templates * Cover letter templates * Safety documents * Quality control documents * Client survey materials * Project closeout documents * Estimating or handoff documents * Marketing and business development materials The tool should prioritize approved/current documents and avoid surfacing outdated or duplicate versions unless specifically requested. ### 3. RFP Upload and Proposal Drafting The tool should allow a user to upload an RFP, RFQ, or proposal request and then generate a first-draft response using approved Pizzano content. The draft proposal should be able to include: * Executive summary * Cover letter * Understanding of the project * Relevant project experience * Relevant case studies * Team bios * Company overview * Safety approach * Quality control approach * Risk management approach * Cost management approach * Schedule management approach * Similar client experience * Sector-specific experience * Boilerplate proposal responses * Clarification questions or missing information list The output should be editable and should be clearly labeled as a first draft for marketing/team review. ### 4. Client Response Drafting The tool should help draft professional responses to client questions using approved company language. Examples include responses related to: * Schedule concerns * Cost control * Change management * Quality control * Safety * Occupied renovations * Preconstruction * Budgeting * Closeout * Corporate Services/small projects * Secure or sensitive project environments The tool should be able to provide draft responses that are polished, professional, and consistent with Pizzano’s tone and brand. ### 5. Employee Onboarding and Company Knowledge The tool should help new and existing employees better understand the company, including: * Company history * Leadership * Divisions * Market sectors * Internal processes * Tools and systems * Standard operating procedures * Project types * Company values and brand language ## Desired System Capabilities The ideal solution should include: * Secure login/user access. * Permission-based document access. * Integration with SharePoint and/or Microsoft 365. * Ability to connect to approved folders or libraries. * Ability to exclude outdated, draft, duplicate, or unapproved documents. * Source citations or document links in AI answers. * Ability to upload an RFP and generate a structured proposal draft. * Ability to retrieve relevant case studies, bios, and boilerplate from approved content. * Admin controls for managing approved content sources. * Clear separation between approved content and general AI knowledge. * Human review workflow for proposal drafts. * Ability to export drafts to Word, Google Docs, or another editable format. * Strong data privacy and security practices. * Documentation and training for internal users. * Scalable architecture that can grow with the company. ## Preferred Technical Approach We are open to recommendations, but we expect the solution may involve: * Microsoft SharePoint / Microsoft 365 integration. * Retrieval-Augmented Generation, also known as RAG. * Vector search or semantic search across approved documents. * AI model integration through a secure API. * User authentication and permission control. * A web-based internal interface or Microsoft Teams-based interface. * Admin panel for managing knowledge sources. * Logging, feedback, and answer-quality review. We are not looking for a generic chatbot connected to all company files without controls. We need a secure, business-focused tool grounded in approved company content. ## Important Security and Governance Requirements The selected consultant should address: * How company data will be protected. * Whether our data will be used to train public AI models. * How user permissions will be handled. * How confidential documents will be protected. * How source documents will be cited. * How outdated documents will be excluded or flagged. * How hallucinations or unsupported answers will be minimized. * How administrators can approve, remove, or update content. * How proposal drafts will be reviewed before external use. ## Desired Deliverables Please include the following in your proposal: ### Phase 1: Discovery and Planning * Review our goals, document structure, SharePoint/network-drive structure, and current proposal process. * Recommend the best technical architecture. * Identify content governance needs. * Recommend how approved content should be organized. * Provide a project roadmap. ### Phase 2: Prototype / Pilot Build a working prototype that can: * Search a limited approved content library. * Answer employee questions with source references. * Locate forms and templates. * Upload a sample RFP. * Generate a sample proposal draft using approved source materials. ### Phase 3: Full Build Develop the broader internal tool with: * Secure user access. * SharePoint or Microsoft 365 integration. * Approved document indexing. * RFP upload and proposal generation. * Source citations. * Admin controls. * Exportable proposal drafts. * Feedback and improvement loop. ### Phase 4: Testing and Training * Test with a small internal team. * Improve answer quality. * Train marketing, business development, operations, and leadership users. * Provide documentation. * Provide admin training. ### Phase 5: Ongoing Support * Provide support options. * Provide maintenance recommendations. * Recommend future enhancements. ## Questions for Respondents Please answer the following in your response: 1. Have you built similar internal AI knowledge tools for companies before? 2. Have you built tools that integrate with SharePoint, Microsoft 365, Teams, OneDrive, or network drives? 3. Have you built RFP or proposal-generation tools before? 4. What AI models or platforms do you recommend for this use case and why? 5. How would you prevent the tool from using outdated or unapproved content? 6. How would you manage permissions and confidential documents? 7. How would you ensure answers include source citations? 8. How would you reduce hallucinations or unsupported answers? 9. Would our data be used to train any public AI model? 10. What would your recommended phased approach be? 11. What would you need from us to begin? 12. What is your estimated timeline for a prototype? 13. What is your estimated timeline for a full working version? 14. What is your estimated cost range for the prototype and full build? 15. What ongoing support would you recommend after launch? 16. Can you provide examples of similar work, screenshots, demos, or references? ## Proposal Response Format Please structure your response as follows: 1. Executive summary 2. Relevant experience 3. Recommended technical approach 4. Security and privacy approach 5. SharePoint/Microsoft 365 integration approach 6. RFP/proposal generation approach 7. Content governance recommendations 8. Project phases and timeline 9. Deliverables 10. Cost estimate 11. Ongoing support options 12. Relevant examples or portfolio 13. Key risks and how you would address them ## Selection Criteria We will evaluate responses based on: * Experience with internal AI knowledge tools. * Experience with SharePoint/Microsoft 365. * Experience with RFP/proposal automation. * Security and privacy approach. * Ability to create a practical business tool, not just a chatbot. * Understanding of document governance and approved content. * Quality of recommended approach. * Cost and timeline. * Communication style and ability to explain technical topics clearly. * Ability to provide ongoing support. ## Project Budget Please provide pricing options for: * Discovery and planning only. * Prototype/pilot build. * Full production build. * Monthly support and maintenance. We are open to phased pricing and would prefer a clear approach that allows us to test the concept before committing to a full build. ## Desired Outcome At the end of this project, Pizzano Contractors should have a secure, easy-to-use internal AI assistant that helps employees find approved information, understand company processes, draft better client responses, and help our marketing team produce stronger first-draft proposals more efficiently. The tool should make our company smarter, faster, better organized, and more consistent across the firm.

Product Designer for Single Page Clinica.t: Product Designer for Single Page Clinical AI Interface
Product Designer for Single Page Clinica.d: SHORT SUMMARY: We have a working demo of a clinical AI / diagnostic support interface and need a product designer to create a clearer, more polished Figma (or your preferred UI design tool) redesign direction for the one of our demo screens. We can provide you with unredacted screenshots and short clips of the UI in action upon acceptance and request. This is a single-page demo project, not a full site or application. We are looking for creative talent. We need broad ideas and a good starting point. At this stage, we do not need a production-ready Figma file, a full design system, fully built component library, detailed developer handoff specs, or a fully clickable prototype. Static mockups are fine. The goal is to explore a strong design direction that we can refine further afterward. FURTHER INFORMATION Deliverables: One Figma (or similar UI tool) file for the demo page Short notes explaining the main layout and decisions Ideal Background: Product design / UX/UI design for web apps SaaS, dashboard, enterprise, workflow, admin, healthcare, fintech, legal, or data-heavy product experience Strong Figma skills Experience designing within Material UI / MUI or similar component libraries Ability to simplify dense interfaces without making them feel generic Experience with progressive disclosure, information density, or customizable views is a plus Good visual hierarchy and information architecture skills Full summary: The goal is to make the demo easier to understand, more professional, and more visually polished while keeping the same basic workflow. The viewer should quickly understand: what findings were entered, what follow-up questions are being asked, how the differential diagnosis is changing, and why the system is suggesting certain diagnoses. The engine that creates the data is fixed and we can answer any questions about it via instant message if you have them during the course of your work. A key design challenge is that the amount of visible clinical reasoning and supporting data should feel dynamic/customizable. We want to be able to demo the full “under the hood” power of the engine when appropriate, showing rich diagnostic reasoning, supporting evidence, modifiers, and differential updates. But we also want the interface to demonstrate how the system can be simplified or customized for each doctor’s workflow. We are interested in design ideas for progressive disclosure, collapsible panels, density controls, view modes, or other ways to let the interface reveal more or less detail depending on the user and context. Important implementation context: The rest of the application is built with Material UI / MUI. The redesign should be realistic to implement in an MUI-based React app and should not require a completely new component system. That said, we are not looking for a generic default-Material look. We want thoughtful, creative improvements to layout, visual hierarchy, density, grouping, and interaction patterns while staying compatible with MUI components and our existing app.

Logo Designer — BDSA Conference Newcastl.d: We are a team of dental students organising the BDSA (British Dental Students' Association) Conference 2027 — the UK's largest annual dental student conference. It will be held in Newcastle in January 2027, making this the first time the event has ever come to the North East. The conference brings together around 1,000 delegates from dental schools across the UK for two days of speakers, workshops, a gala dinner, and socials. We need a logo that will live on everything — lanyards, name badges, programmes, merchandise, social media, and our official bid presentation to the BDSA AGM in June 2026. This is a visible, meaningful project and we want something we are genuinely proud to put our name on. What We Are Looking For We want a skilled logo designer with creative freedom to bring their own vision to this project. We are not looking for someone to execute a rigid specification — we are looking for a designer who can take a brief, understand the spirit of what we need, and surprise us with something great. We have three non-negotiable requirements. Everything else is open to your interpretation and creative judgement: The Three Requirements 1 Circular format The logo must be a circle — badge, emblem, crest, seal, or any circular format you feel suits the project. Beyond that, the layout, internal structure, and composition are entirely your call. 2 Dark blue as the primary colour Dark blue must be the dominant colour — the exact shade, secondary colours, and accent palette are your choice. Go wherever feels right creatively. Navy, royal blue, midnight — all open. We have been working with #1A3A8F as a reference point but are not attached to it. 3 A combination of Newcastle and dental elements The logo must somehow connect Newcastle and dentistry. How you interpret and combine these two things is entirely up to you. Some directions previous designers have explored — you are not limited to these: • The Tyne Bridge arch echoing the shape of a dental arch or smile • A tooth silhouette incorporating Newcastle's skyline or landmarks within it • Newcastle's castle keep rendered with dental motifs • An abstract mark that suggests both without being literal • Something else entirely — surprise us Text to Include The logo must include the following text somewhere within it — how and where you incorporate it is your decision: Required text Notes BDSA Conference Casing, font, and placement are your choice Newcastle 2027 Can be combined or separated — your call Style Guidance We have no fixed style in mind. What we do care about is quality and craft — the logo should feel like it was made by a human designer with genuine skill, not generated by AI or assembled from stock elements. Beyond that, we are genuinely open. Some styles we think could work well, though we are not prescribing any of them: • Illustrated badge or emblem — detailed central icon, heraldic feel • Clean modern geometric — bold shapes, confident typography • Minimalist wordmark with a strong iconographic element • Classic crest — structured, prestigious, layered • Contemporary sports or festival badge — energetic, bold The logo needs to work at small sizes (lanyard badge, social media profile picture) and large sizes (conference banner, programme cover). Please keep this in mind when considering detail level. Reference Material We have attached the following for context — these are reference points, not constraints: • BDSA Conference Leeds 2025 logo — the previous year's conference logo, included as a quality benchmark and to show the format context. We are not trying to copy it — we want something distinctly Newcastle. • A photograph of the Tyne Bridge — in case it is useful for any Newcastle landmark reference. • A provisional logo we have made for inspiration and idea of what we are looking for You are under no obligation to use either. If your concept takes a completely different direction and it is strong, we want to see it. Deliverables • AI or EPS — fully editable vector source file • SVG — for web and digital use • PNG — transparent background, minimum 2000 × 2000px • PNG — on dark blue background, minimum 2000 × 2000px • PNG — on white background, minimum 2000 × 2000px All files must be 100% vector — no embedded raster elements. The logo must be sharp and clean at any size. We genuinely want to see your ideas and creative interpretation. If you have a concept that breaks from what we have described but feels right, tell us about it — we are open. To confirm you have read this brief, please begin your proposal with the word Newcastle.

Blockchain Architect for Decentralized E.t: Blockchain Architect for Decentralized E-commerce & Native Token
Blockchain Architect for Decentralized E.i: Hourly: $100.00 - $150.00 Expert Est. time: More than 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
Blockchain Architect for Decentralized E.d: We're building a decentralized e-commerce platform from the ground up. The problem we're trying to solve is pretty simple. Today's marketplaces sit in the middle of every transaction, charge significant fees, control the ecosystem, and own the relationship between buyers and sellers. We believe there is a better way to do it. Our goal is to build a platform where blockchain is used where it makes sense — payments, settlement, incentives, staking, and governance — while keeping the overall user experience as straightforward as any modern e-commerce application. Alongside the platform, we're developing a native token that will be used across the ecosystem. The token isn't the product. The platform is. The token exists to support activity on the platform through payments, rewards, staking mechanisms, and community participation. We're still early. The project is currently in the PoC phase, which means many of the important architectural decisions haven't been made yet. That's exactly why we're hiring. What We're Looking For We're looking for someone who can help answer questions such as: - What should live on-chain and what shouldn't? - How should payments, rewards, staking, and governance be structured? - Which parts of the system need smart contracts and which don't? - How do we keep transaction costs reasonable as usage grows? - How do we design something that's secure without making it unnecessarily complicated? We're looking for someone who can challenge assumptions, identify risks early, and help design the right foundation before significant development begins. Your Role You would work closely with the founders and engineering team to: - Define the blockchain architecture - Design the token and supporting smart contract systems - Help make infrastructure and scalability decisions - Review technical designs and implementation plans - Establish security and development standards - Guide blockchain-related development as the project evolves Depending on your background, the role can be hands-on, advisory, or somewhere in between. What Matters Most - Experience designing blockchain systems, not just deploying contracts - Strong understanding of EVM ecosystems and smart contract architecture - Experience with token-based products and incentive models - Ability to think through security, scalability, and long-term maintenance - Comfortable working in an early-stage environment where not everything is already defined Why This Role Is Interesting We're still early enough that the right person can have a meaningful impact on the direction of both the platform and the token ecosystem. If you enjoy solving difficult design problems and helping shape products from the beginning, we'd love to talk.

We're looking for a clean, polished style — think animated trading card reveal energy meets cinematic game trailer. We also understand that the this is a fast turn around time, so please confirm that you can get this done by June 16th, June 17th latest. Here are some comparison videos for your reference. If you were starting fresh with a Shopify jewelry store and a $1,200/month ad budget — no purchase history in the pixel, maybe 200 visitors/month organic — what would your first 60 days look like? Be specific, I'm not looking for a general answer. 4. Do you handle creative or is that on us? If you need video or lifestyle content, how do you usually solve that for clients at this budget level? 5. What does a normal working month with you look like — how do we communicate, what do I get from you and how often? 6. What's your monthly fee for this kind of engagement and what does it include? I need an operator who can turn the system into consistent weekly content: AI-generated visuals, short-form video edits, carousels, Stories, captions, scheduling, and basic performance reporting. The role is a mix of: AI image/video production, social-first design, short-form video editing, content packaging, brand consistency, platform-safe marketing, and analytics-driven iteration. You must be comfortable working on an adult-adjacent but public-safe brand. Public content must stay non-explicit, non-nude, non-medical, and compliant with Instagram/TikTok/YouTube rules. The private monetization path is Fanvue later, but this role starts with mainstream-safe social growth: Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts. The ideal person has taste. We are aiming for cinematic luxury, not cheap "AI girl" spam. Think dark editorial photography, candlelight, black silk, warm gold, calm authority, premium captions, and consistent character identity. Initial goal: produce and schedule weekly content batches that can grow toward 50k combined followers across IG/TikTok/Shorts. WHAT YOU WILL DO Typical weekly workload: 1. Produce short-form videos - Generate or source brand-consistent AI visuals - Animate stills or create subtle video loops - Edit 9:16 Reels/TikToks/Shorts - Add captions, sound design, cover frames, and platform-specific exports - Keep the identity visually consistent 2. Design supporting social assets - Instagram carousels - Story packs - Quote/atmosphere posts - Cover frames - Simple brand templates 3. Package content for posting - Captions - Hashtags, 3-5 max - TikTok-safe variants - YouTube Shorts variants - IG Story text/polls/questions - File naming and handoff 4. Maintain the brand system - Keep face, wardrobe, environments, color palette, and tone consistent - Reject images/videos that drift into generic AI beauty, daylight, cheap glam, or explicit territory - Follow the compliance rules before anything public goes live 5. Track performance and iterate - Log basic metrics: views, retention if available, saves, shares, profile visits, follows, link clicks - Identify winners and propose 5-10 remixes of winning hooks - Recommend what to repeat, remix, kill, or test next BRAND DIRECTION The character is a cinematic AI/synthetic creator: an American, noir-luxury, dominant hypnotic muse for high-performing men who can't switch off at night. Public content is platform-safe evening decompression: "put the day down," "give me the evening," "drop your shoulders," "you don't have to decide anything else tonight." The content should feel intimate and commanding, but never crude, explicit, medical, or desperate. Visual style: noir luxury, candlelight gold, deep blacks, muted rose, dark walnut interiors, black silk / robe / blouse / slip (SFW), elegant jewelry, cinematic editorial framing, 9:16 vertical compositions, direct gaze, calm authority, warm low light, city-at-night feel. Avoid: daylight, bright white rooms, cheap AI influencer gloss, nudity, explicit sexual posing, random face drift, "AI girl" novelty aesthetics. Tone: composed, intimate, commanding, restrained, premium, slow, deliberate, confident. REQUIRED SKILLS Must-have: - AI image/video production: Higgsfield, Midjourney, Runway, Kling, Seedance, ComfyUI, Leonardo, or similar. Ability to preserve consistent character identity across outputs. - Short-form video editing: CapCut, Premiere, DaVinci, Final Cut, or similar. Strong 9:16 instincts, captions, cover frames, pacing, atmospheric edits. - Social design: Instagram carousel design, story design, premium minimal layout — not Canva-template clutter. - Social growth basics: packaging hooks, captions, hashtags, posting variants. Can read simple analytics and recommend iterations. - Compliance discipline: Can follow strict public-safe rules. Understands adult-adjacent marketing on mainstream platforms. Strong bonus: - Experience with virtual influencers / AI creators - Experience with adult-adjacent but SFW social funnels - Experience with Fanvue, Beacons, Linktree, Later, Metricool - ElevenLabs or voice/audio production - Copywriting in a cinematic/luxury voice FIRST 30 DAYS Week 1 — onboarding + test batch: - Read the brand brief and compliance rules - Review existing face/visual references - Create 3 visual style tests - Produce 1 test Reel, 1 carousel cover direction, 3 Story frames - Deliver a short QC note: what works, what is drifting, what should be standardised Week 2 — first production batch: - 2 Reels / TikToks / Shorts (9:16) - 1 Instagram carousel - 5-7 Stories, captions, hashtags, cover frames - TikTok-clean versions where needed - Organized exports Week 3+: iteration, analytics, hook remixes, template improvements. ONGOING MONTHLY OPTIONS Lean: 8 short-form videos, 4 carousels, 20-30 Story frames, captions, monthly analytics summary. Growth: 12-16 short-form videos, 4-8 carousels, 30-50 Story frames, weekly analytics notes, hook remix bank. Aggressive: 20+ short-form videos, 8+ carousels, 50+ Story frames, weekly test plans and scheduling support. Recommended starting point: Lean or Growth tier for month one, then scale only if quality is strong. COMPLIANCE All content approved before posting until trust is established. Nothing explicit, no direct adult-platform links in TikTok/YouTube captions, no medical claims, no claiming Delphine is a real human. When applying, please include: 1. The project centers around a custom pet personality quiz based on the Five Elements of TCVM (Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water). Pet owners will take the quiz, discover their pet’s constitution, and have the opportunity to purchase downloadable educational guides tailored to their pet’s personality, health tendencies, lifestyle, and wellness needs. I have already developed the quiz concept, questions, scoring system, constitution framework, branding, and much of the educational content. I am looking for someone who can help organize, edit, design, package, and implement everything into a polished customer experience. Project Deliverables: 1. Quiz Development * Build and implement an interactive online pet personality quiz * Integrate the quiz with my Wix website * Create automatic scoring and constitution results * Collect user information and email addresses * Create a professional results page * Recommend whether Wix or a third-party quiz platform would be the best fit 2. Digital Product Creation Create 5 branded digital guides: * Wood Constitution Guide * Fire Constitution Guide * Earth Constitution Guide * Metal Constitution Guide * Water Constitution Guide I will provide the majority of the educational content, concepts, business logos, recommendations, and source material. I am looking for someone who can help organize, edit, format, design, and package this information into beautiful, professional guides for pet owners. The guides may include: * Personality traits * Health tendencies * Food therapy recommendations * Activities and enrichment ideas * Wellness tips * Checklists * Worksheets * Visual elements and graphics 3. Website & Sales Funnel Setup * Create product purchase pages * Recommend and implement a simple pricing structure * Set up payment processing * Set up automated delivery of purchased guides * Connect everything to my Wix website * Create email capture and basic automation What I Will Provide * Quiz questions and scoring system * Peace Love Vet branding, logo, colors, and style preferences * Educational content, notes, recommendations, and source material * Veterinary expertise and guidance throughout the project * Existing Wix website Ideal Candidate * Experience with Wix websites * Experience building quizzes, lead magnets, and sales funnels * Canva and/or PDF design experience * Email marketing and automation experience * Strong organizational and editing skills * Ability to take ideas and content and turn them into polished, user-friendly products * Experience in pet, wellness, healthcare, education, or coaching industries is a plus When Applying You May Include * Examples of quizzes, lead magnets, or funnels you have built * Examples of PDF guides, workbooks, or digital products you have designed * Whether you would build this entirely within Wix or recommend a third-party platform * Estimated timeline * Fixed-price quote (negotiable!) * Any ideas you have for improving the customer experience ***Future Opportunities*** This project is intended as a short-term contract with clearly defined deliverables. However, if we are a good fit, I would love to continue working together on additional Peace Love Vet projects in the future. I am actively growing and restructuring my veterinary practice and anticipate many additional projects throughout the year, including: * Membership and concierge care program materials * Membership brochures and welcome packets * Website redesign and service pages * Client education guides and handouts * Digital products and courses * Email marketing campaigns * Lead magnets and wellness resources * Branding and marketing materials This quiz and digital product launch is just the beginning. I have many ideas and am looking for someone who enjoys helping small businesses bring creative ideas to life and can potentially grow alongside the brand. I look forward to hearing your ideas and seeing examples of your work!

Logo design for car diagnostics automoti.d: # Automotive Diagnostic Brand – Logo & Icon Redesign (European Technical Ecommerce Brand) We are looking for a professional logo / brand identity designer to redesign and modernize the visual identity of our automotive diagnostic ecommerce brand: Cartline. IMPORTANT: This is NOT a generic car garage logo project. We are an EU-based ecommerce/distribution business specialized in: * automotive diagnostic tools * coding devices * workshop scan tools * professional vehicle diagnostics * mobile diagnostic ecosystems Brands/products sold include: OBDeleven, TOPDON, Carista, MBito and similar automotive diagnostic equipment. Our websites are evolving toward a premium European automotive-tech direction and the current logo/icon quality became the weakest point of the entire brand experience. We need: * a modernized icon system * a stronger favicon * improved typography direction * industrial / technical visual identity * scalable logo system for ecommerce, mobile, packaging and documents --- ## CURRENT SITUATION We already have an existing logo concept. Core ideas currently used: * OBD connector * circular framing * industrial technical feel * orange accent color * dark technical branding The PROBLEM is NOT the concept. The problem is: execution quality, geometry, scalability and overall modern appearance. The current logo feels: * outdated * aftermarket accessory style * generic electronics shop * early-2010s execution We want to preserve the core diagnostic DNA while redesigning it professionally. --- ## IMPORTANT VISUAL DIRECTION We DO NOT want: * racing logo aesthetics * tuning culture visuals * aggressive sports car styling * shield logos * cyberpunk effects * glowing gaming-style logos * generic AI-generated logos * fake futuristic visuals * repair garage branding * local mechanic workshop feeling We DO want: * premium automotive-tech aesthetic * European industrial design language * modern technical precision * clean geometry * scalable iconography * strong favicon readability * Bosch / Continental / modern automotive supplier feeling * technical reliability * professional engineered look Brand personality should feel: industrial + technical + trustworthy + modern. --- ## TARGET STYLE REFERENCES Think closer to: * Bosch * Continental * modern automotive supplier branding * industrial SaaS hardware brands * premium diagnostic equipment companies NOT: * racing team * drift culture * gaming peripherals * neon tech startup --- ## REQUIRED DELIVERABLES 1. Main Logo * horizontal version * dark/light variants 2. Icon / Symbol * standalone favicon-ready version * highly recognizable at small sizes 3. Typography Direction * font recommendations * spacing recommendations 4. Favicon Set * 16x16 * 32x32 * 64x64 * 128x128 * 512x512 5. Export Files * SVG * PNG * AI or Figma source * transparent versions --- ## VERY IMPORTANT The logo MUST work properly: * in browser tabs * mobile headers * ecommerce websites * dark backgrounds * invoices * packaging * social media * app-like interfaces Current issue: the existing icon loses readability at small sizes. We want: simple, strong, engineered geometry. --- ## COLOR DIRECTION Current direction: * dark industrial background * muted technical orange accent * off-white typography Orange can remain but should be modernized/refined if needed. --- ## TO APPLY Please include: * portfolio examples relevant to automotive / industrial / tech branding * favicon examples if available * examples of minimalist technical logo systems * explanation of your redesign approach Applications with generic AI-generated portfolios or unrelated gaming/racing logo styles will be ignored. * Do not simply polish attached logo. Use it only as conceptual reference.
