New Logo for Swedish Window Restoration.t: New Logo for Swedish Window Restoration Company
New Logo for Swedish Window Restoration.d: Valego is a Swedish company specializing in window restoration and window cleaning. Today, the business offers both services, but the long-term direction is toward the restoration and preservation of traditional Swedish wooden windows. The logo should therefore communicate craftsmanship, heritage, longevity, and respect for traditional construction, rather than simply cleanliness or glass cleaning. What Makes Traditional Swedish Windows Special: Traditional Swedish windows are very different from modern replacement windows. They are characterized by: * Handcrafted wooden construction * Fine proportions and elegant detailing * Spröjs (muntins/glazing bars) * Traditional hardware and fittings * Corner irons (vinkeljärn) used to reinforce the sash * A strong connection to Swedish architectural heritage Many of these windows are over 100 years old and are still functioning today when properly maintained. The goal is not only to preserve the window itself, but also the character and beauty of the building. I have attached pictures. Design Direction: I am interested in exploring a logo based on an abstracted Swedish window profile. The concept is: * A recognizable portion of a traditional Swedish wooden window * A visible spröjs element * A traditional corner iron (vinkeljärn) in the corner * Simplified and stylized into a clean, modern symbol Important: The logo should not look like a generic modern window company. Please avoid: * Generic four-pane window icons * Houses or roofs * Paint brushes * Squeegees * Sparkles or cleaning symbols * Stock-style construction graphics Instead, the logo should feel: * Swedish * Timeless * Crafted * Architectural * Premium * Trustworthy Target Customers: * Owners of older villas * Owners of traditional wooden windows * Heritage-conscious homeowners * Property owners * Housing associations * Customers who value quality craftsmanship Visual Style: * Minimalist * Strong in one color * Works in small sizes * Suitable for vehicles, workwear, business cards, signage, and website use * Scandinavian design language * Clean but not cold Deliverables: Please explore several concepts based on the window-profile idea, including: A) Window profile + corner iron B) Window profile + corner iron + spröjs C) Window profile that subtly forms a "V" for Valego The final logo should be distinctive enough that it could become a recognizable symbol on its own, even without the company name. ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS Required Deliverables: This project is not only for a logo. The final design system should work across several real-world applications used by the business. Please provide the logo in a way that allows it to be used in the following formats: 1. Primary Logo * Full logo with symbol and wordmark ("Valego") * Horizontal version * Suitable for website headers, vehicles, invoices, and signage 2. Business Card Versions * A version optimized for the front of a business card * A simplified isolated symbol that can be used on the reverse side of a business card * The symbol should be recognizable on its own without the company name 3. Signage Version * A version suitable for outdoor signs and advertising flags * Must remain readable from a distance * Should work with accompanying text such as: * Fönsterrenovering * Fönsterputs * Arbetar här idag 4. Workwear Applications * A version suitable for embroidery on baseball caps * A version suitable for printing on T-shirts, jackets, and workwear * The symbol should remain recognizable when reproduced at small sizes 5. Digital Use * Favicon version * Social media profile version * Website use Technical Requirements: The logo should: * Work in full color * Work in one color * Work in black and white * Remain recognizable at very small sizes * Be scalable without losing clarity * Avoid excessive fine detail that may disappear when embroidered or printed small Design Goal: The symbol should ideally become recognizable on its own, independent of the company name. A homeowner should eventually be able to see the symbol on a vehicle, flag, cap, or sign and immediately associate it with Valego. APPLICATION REQUIREMENT To help me identify designers who have read the brief carefully, please include the following in your proposal: 1. A link to one branding, identity, or logo project you are most proud of. 2. One or two sentences explaining how you would approach the challenge of creating a logo inspired by traditional Swedish wooden windows without using a generic four-pane window icon. 3. Include the word "spröjs" somewhere in your proposal so I know you have read the brief. Optional: If you have experience designing for heritage, architectural, woodworking, restoration, or traditional craft businesses, please include examples.

Business Consultant – Commercial Plumbin.t: Business Consultant – Commercial Plumbing & Building Products Industry
Business Consultant – Commercial Plumbin.d: # Business Consultant – Commercial Plumbing & Building Products Industry We're looking for an experienced Business Consultant with a strong understanding of the commercial plumbing, restroom fixture, and building products industry to help guide our company's growth strategy and identify new opportunities within the market. ## About the Role You will work directly with our leadership team to evaluate our current business model, identify areas for growth, and help develop strategies to increase market share within the commercial plumbing and construction industries. Our company manufactures and supplies commercial restroom products, including touchless faucets, flush valves, soap dispensers, and related accessories. We're looking for someone who understands how products move from manufacturer to specification, distribution, and installation. ## What You'll Do * Evaluate our current business strategy and market position * Identify opportunities for growth within the commercial plumbing and building products markets * Review sales channels, distribution strategies, and manufacturer representative networks * Help develop plans to increase specifications with architects, engineers, designers, and contractors * Evaluate opportunities with OEMs, distributors, national accounts, and strategic partnerships * Analyze competitors and identify areas where we can better position our products * Provide guidance on pricing, product development, and market expansion * Help improve operational efficiency and long-term scalability * Assist with planning for new product launches and expansion into new markets * Identify potential risks and opportunities within the industry ## What We're Looking For * Experience within the commercial plumbing, construction, architectural, MEP, HVAC, or building products industries * Strong background in business strategy, growth planning, and market development * Experience working with manufacturers, distributors, manufacturer representatives, or specification-driven sales channels * Understanding of how products are specified and sold in commercial construction projects * Ability to provide actionable recommendations and implementation strategies * Proven track record of helping companies grow revenue, expand market share, or improve profitability ## Preferred Experience * Commercial plumbing fixtures * Touchless and sensor-operated products * Building products manufacturing * Construction product distribution * Government, education, healthcare, hospitality, and commercial facility markets * National account development * Manufacturer representative management * Product commercialization and growth strategy ## Project Type * Contract / Consultant * Flexible engagement based on experience and recommendations * Potential for ongoing advisory relationship ## Goal We're looking for a consultant who understands the commercial plumbing and building products industry and can help us create a clear roadmap for growth, strengthen our market position, identify new revenue opportunities, and build a scalable strategy for long-term success. Please provide examples of previous work, case studies, or companies you've helped within the construction, plumbing, manufacturing, or building products sectors. References and portfolio examples are strongly preferred. ## Important DO NOT CALL OUR OFFICE. Please keep all communication through this platform. Thank you.

Luxury WordPress Website Development.i: Hourly Expert Est. time: More than 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
Luxury WordPress Website Development.d: Home Watch & Fractional Estate Management Website Project Description & Build Instructions Request for Proposal • Scope of Work 1. PROJECT DESCRIPTION PalmHouse Design & Build is commissioning a new marketing website for its estate-oversight division, PalmHouse Home Watch & Fractional Estate Management. We are seeking a developer or agency to design, build, and launch a luxury-grade website that generates qualified consultation requests from affluent homeowners across South Florida. This is not a traditional home-watch site. The company is positioned as a private estate-oversight firm comparable in tone to Four Seasons Private Residences, Ritz-Carlton Residences, and Sotheby’s International Realty. The finished site must read as professional, exclusive, discreet, and white-glove. Brand promise: “When You Can’t Be Here, We Are.” Business context. PalmHouse is a licensed Florida general contracting and design-build firm. Its competitive advantage in estate oversight is that the principal and team are licensed builders and restoration professionals, not checklist inspectors. The site must make that distinction central to the messaging and visual tone. Target audience. Affluent homeowners, seasonal residents, executives, family offices, trustees, and luxury property owners throughout South Florida. Reference sites. These live sites define the target. The first is the primary structural reference; the other two are look-and-feel and positioning references. We are not copying any of them. They show the standard, the page flow, and the tone we expect. ∙ Three Palms Home Watch — PRIMARY structural reference. Use its page flow (trust, service, reporting, CTA) as the structural model. ∙ Luxury Presence demo sites — VISUAL reference only. The finish level we want: editorial typography, generous white space, refined motion. Match the look, do not build on the platform. ∙ Spectrum Concierge — POSITIONING reference. Study how it presents the service ladder, which mirrors our Home Watch, Estate Care, Fractional Estate Management path. Our differentiator (must come through on every reference comparison). Competitors lead with reporting and tenure. PalmHouse leads with construction-trained eyes: a licensed general contractor and restoration professional walking the property. The finished site must make that distinction unmistakable, in a way none of the reference sites currently do. 2. PROJECT OBJECTIVES 3. Generate qualified leads: convert affluent homeowners into private consultations. 4. Establish authority as South Florida’s trusted estate-oversight firm. 5. Build a site structured so that a future SEO provider can rank it efficiently in local search. 6. Build a clear upsell path from Home Watch to Estate Care to Fractional Estate Management. 7. Establish long-term brand equity as the region’s premier estate-management firm. 8. SCOPE OF WORK This engagement is for website development only. We are hiring you to design, build, and launch the site described below, built to a clean, SEO-ready technical standard. Ongoing SEO, content marketing, and link building are NOT part of this engagement and will be handled separately by a dedicated SEO provider. Your SEO responsibility is limited to on-page technical structure (see Section 4), not campaign management or ranking outcomes. Copy for the Home and About pages will be provided by PalmHouse. The vendor will lay out, style, and structure all pages. 3.1 Pages to build ∙ Home: hero, three service tiers, founder-credibility bar, why-PalmHouse, trust indicators, service-area map, proof-of-work block, testimonials, final CTA. Copy provided. ∙ About: founder story, mission, values, credentials, promise. Copy provided. ∙ Home Watch: service explanation, interior/exterior inspection checklist, digital reporting, arrival/departure service. ∙ Estate Care: vendor management, maintenance scheduling, project coordination, storm prep, utility monitoring. Monthly packages. ∙ Fractional Estate Management: executive-level oversight without a full-time hire. Administration, budgeting, capital-project planning, family-office support. ∙ Construction & Owner Rep: renovation oversight, contractor management, QC, budget tracking, owner representation. ∙ Hurricane Preparedness: storm prep, generator checks, securing, post-storm inspection. ∙ Concierge Services: arrival prep, vehicle coordination, stocking, vendor scheduling, seasonal support. ∙ Service-Area Pages: dedicated location pages for local search, structured and ready for a future SEO provider to optimize. Each includes overview, local content, testimonials, FAQs, and local schema. ∙ Blog: functional blog system, launch slate of 6 articles (PalmHouse to supply or approve topics). ∙ Contact: consultation request form, click-to-call, map, service area. 3.2 Brand & design standards (mandatory). Follow the PalmHouse brand system. Do not introduce a separate color identity. ∙ Primary colors: Teal #2A7A6F, Charcoal #3C3C3C, White #FFFFFF ∙ Secondary: Teal-mid #3AA897, Cream #F8FAF9 ∙ Accent: Gold/Bronze #9C7A3C, used sparingly (rules, icons, hover states) ∙ Heading font: Cormorant Garamond ∙ Body font: Jost ∙ Aesthetic: luxury modern, minimalist, large photography, generous white space, elegant motion, editorial feel ∙ Tone reference set: Aman, Four Seasons, Sotheby’s, Apple PalmHouse will provide the logo files, including the inverted white-on-dark version. Photography direction: waterfront estates, refined interiors, inspection-in-progress, storm prep, discreet concierge moments. Stock is acceptable at launch; an original shoot may follow. 3.3 Lead capture (build to spec). A Free Estate Assessment form must appear on every page. Keep the field set short to protect conversion among high-net-worth visitors. Required fields: Name, Phone, Email, Property address, Service needed (dropdown: Home Watch / Estate Care / Fractional Management / Construction Oversight). Do not include a property-value or net-worth field. Form submissions must integrate with the CRM PalmHouse designates at kickoff, with email notification to the office. 4. TECHNICAL & SEO-READINESS REQUIREMENTS The site must be built to a clean technical standard that a separate SEO provider can pick up and optimize without rework. You build the foundation; ongoing optimization is not part of this engagement. 4.1 Platform & technical. WordPress; Elementor Pro or Bricks Builder; Rank Math Pro installed and configured; Cloudflare, SSL, CDN; mobile-first responsive design; Core Web Vitals optimization, fast hosting, image compression; schema markup site-wide. 4.2 On-page SEO structure (every page). SEO title and meta description fields populated, single H1, clean and logical H2/H3 hierarchy, sensible internal linking, optimized images with descriptive alt text, FAQ schema, and LocalBusiness schema. This is the technical groundwork. Keyword research, content campaigns, and ranking work belong to the SEO provider engaged separately. 4.3 Local SEO readiness. Location landing pages built and structured for local search, Google Maps embeds, and the technical hooks for Google Business Profile and review integration in place. Active local-SEO campaign management is out of scope. 4.4 Functionality. Mobile responsive, fast loading, contact and lead-capture forms, click-to-call, CRM integration, live chat, testimonial management, blog system, video background support, review integration. 5. DELIVERABLES & MILESTONES Map pricing to these milestones: 1. Discovery & sitemap: kickoff call, confirmed sitemap, wireframes, hosting/CRM confirmation. 2. Design: Home and About page designs for approval, then template designs for remaining page types. 3. Build: all pages developed, copy placed, forms wired to CRM. 4. SEO-readiness setup: schema, on-page structure, location pages, technical pass, Core Web Vitals check. 5. QA & launch: cross-device QA, form testing, speed test, client UAT, go-live. 6. Handoff: admin training, documentation, and a 30-day post-launch support window. Handoff must leave the site clean and fully editable for a future SEO provider. 7. INSTRUCTIONS TO BIDDERS 6.1 What to include in your proposal 1. Fixed-price quote broken out by the six milestones in Section 5. 2. Timeline from kickoff to launch, with milestone dates. 3. Two or three examples of comparable luxury or service-business sites you have built. 4. Platform stack confirmation (must meet Section 4.1). 5. Confirmation that the client owns the site, domain, content, and all accounts at project end, with a clean exit/handoff process. 6. Named point of contact and your standard communication cadence. 6.2 Ownership & exit. The client must retain full ownership of the domain, hosting account, WordPress installation, all content, and all third-party accounts. Any proposal that locks the site to a proprietary platform or restricts export will not be considered. The site must be delivered in a state that a separate SEO provider can take over without limitation. 6.3 Copy responsibilities. PalmHouse provides final copy for the Home and About pages and will supply or approve copy for service and location pages. The vendor is responsible for layout, populating SEO title/meta fields, heading structure, alt text, and schema. No placeholder Latin text may remain at launch. 6.4 Submission. Please submit your proposal through this platform and include availability for a 30-minute discovery call. 6.5 Selection criteria. Portfolio quality (demonstrated luxury/editorial design at the level referenced in 3.2), technical build quality (clean, SEO-ready, well-structured WordPress), value & clarity (transparent milestone-based pricing), ownership terms (clean handoff and full client ownership), communication (responsiveness and a single point of contact).​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Industrial / Soft-Goods Designer for Pre.t: Industrial / Soft-Goods Designer for Premium Carrying Case (Monastery Partnership)
Industrial / Soft-Goods Designer for Pre.d: Use the “tighter” job description we already wrote—it’s exactly what Upwork calls a good description: clear overview, scope, skills, budget, and timeline. You can paste it straight into the Description field. Here it is again, slightly polished for clarity: Industrial / Soft-Goods Designer for Premium Carrying Case (Monastery Partnership) We are SportGrips, a U.S.-based company that designs and manufactures premium grip and forearm training tools, including the REVOLUTION 2.0 – a compact, all-metal, USA-made grip trainer used by athletes, rehab patients, and serious strength enthusiasts. Our products are engineered for a lifetime of use and recommended by physical therapists and coaches. We’re developing a premium carrying case for the REVOLUTION 2.0 in partnership with a U.S. monastery or convent (monks/nuns), and we need a professional soft-goods/industrial designer to take this from concept to production-ready design. What We Need Designed A compact, rugged carrying case that: Securely holds one REVOLUTION 2.0 grip trainer (approx. 10.5 in length, 38 mm diameter). Protects it in backpacks, gym bags, and carry-ons. Visually matches our premium, industrial aesthetic (think small camera/tool case, not a floppy pouch). Can be realistically sewn/assembled by a small U.S. monastic workshop using standard sewing machines and basic tools. Key Requirements Semi-rigid or structured soft case (e.g., clamshell with zipper). Internal cradle/foam so the device doesn’t move. Approx. internal dimensions: Length ~11.5 in (for a 10.5 in device plus tolerance). Width/height ~2.5–3 in to accommodate diameter and padding. Materials: Exterior: durable, water-resistant fabric (e.g., 600D–1000D nylon). Interior: foam/EVA with soft lining to protect anodized aluminum. Features: Secure fit for the device. Small mesh pocket or strap for a card/QR to our tutorials. Exterior branding area for SportGrips logo and “Made in USA” label. Deliverables 2–3 initial concept directions (sketches or rendered views) with brief rationale. Final design package, including: Dimensioned drawings. Material specifications (fabrics, foam, hardware). Construction details and panel breakdown. Simple tech pack suitable for a small U.S. sewing shop/monastic community. We Will Provide Exact REVOLUTION 2.0 dimensions and reference photos. Brand guidelines (logo, colors, visual references). Example cases we like (for form factor and finish reference). Any constraints/feedback from monastery partners as we get them. Ideal Freelancer Experience designing soft-goods: cases, bags, or protective gear. Comfortable creating production-ready tech packs for small manufacturers. Understands how to balance premium look/feel with manufacturability and cost. Budget & Timeline Budget: 800–1,500 USD, fixed-price, depending on experience and depth of deliverables. Timeline: 1–2 weeks for concepts. 1–2 weeks for iterations and final tech pack. How to Apply Please include: A brief note on why you’re a good fit for this project. 2–3 examples of relevant soft-goods work (cases, bags, etc.). Your proposed process, estimated timeline, and fixed-price quote within the stated budget range.

Canva Designer Needed to Create Custom T.t: Canva Designer Needed to Create Custom Travel Journal / Keepsake Book
Canva Designer Needed to Create Custom T.d: I need a Canva designer to create a custom journal called: THE ARKANSAS JOURNALS This is a personal keepsake gift for an older couple. The journal is intended to record future trips, experiences, photographs, memories, meals, discoveries, friendships, and adventures in Arkansas over the coming years. This is NOT: a marketing piece a travel guide a scrapbook a photo album This IS: a future-focused travel journal elegant and timeless designed to be written in designed to have photos added later DESIGN STYLE Please create something that feels: Warm Elegant Timeless Hopeful Personal High quality Avoid: Cartoon graphics Corporate design Marketing materials Busy scrapbook layouts Think: Premium travel journal Family keepsake Journal quality suitable for gifting BOOK SPECIFICATIONS Approximately 50 pages 8.5 x 11 inches Full color Designed in Canva Use repeating page templates whenever possible I do NOT need unique custom artwork on every page. I want a clean, attractive design that can be printed locally. DELIVERABLES I require: Canva project ownership transferred to me High-resolution print-ready PDF Final editable Canva file The final design should be suitable for printing at a local print shop as a premium wire-bound journal or similar journal format that opens flat for writing and adding photographs. COVER Title: THE ARKANSAS JOURNALS Subtitle: Tommie & Patricia Use an attractive Arkansas landscape image. OPENING PAGE "Every journey deserves a record. This one is still being written." INTRODUCTION PAGE "These pages belong to Tommie and Patricia. May they be filled with photographs, stories, discoveries, friendships, meals, adventures, and memories from Arkansas for many years to come." GUIDED JOURNAL PAGES Please create a page layout for each of these sections: The First Trip The Best Meal We Ate The Best Breakfast The Best Piece of Pie The Friendliest Person We Met The Road We Didn't Plan To Take The View That Made Us Pull Over The Place We Stayed An Extra Day The Most Surprising Town The Best Story We Heard People We Were Glad To See Again The Best Day Of The Trip The Place We Want To Return To Arkansas Discoveries Next Time... Each section should include: title short prompt room for notes room for photographs JOURNAL PAGES The remainder of the book should consist of journal pages. Each page should contain: Date: __________ Location: __________ The remainder of the page should be open space suitable for writing, notes, photographs, tickets, postcards, and memorabilia. CLOSING PAGE "Some journeys begin with a destination. The best ones become a collection of memories." FINAL PAGE "For Tommie & Patricia" IMPORTANT Please keep the design simple and elegant and use repeating page templates where possible. I am looking for a beautiful journal, not an overly complicated custom art project. Please provide examples of journals, planners, memory books, travel journals, or similar Canva projects you have designed. Prefrerence will be given to designers who have previously created journals, planners, workbooks, keepsake books or memory books.

Nonfiction Book Cover Design.d: I'm looking for a cover designer for a nonfiction book that combines an analysis of financial literacy, systemic power, and biblical wisdom. The tone is serious, prophetic, and urgent — think cultural critique, not Christian bookstore. The title biblical imagery and the content deals with converging crises: economic, technological, political, environmental, and eschatological. The cover needs to communicate weight and seriousness while remaining accessible to a general audience. It should feel like something you'd find at the front table of an independent bookstore — not a self-help book or a devotional. I'm open to dark and stark, literary, or something that plays with symbolic imagery. I know what I don't want: soft colors, generic religious imagery, anything that feels mass-market or safe. What I'm looking for in a designer: — A strong portfolio with nonfiction or literary cover work — Someone who can bring a distinct visual point of view, not just execute instructions — Willingness to present 2–3 initial concepts before finalizing Please include relevant samples with your proposal. Specifics on title, subtitle, and design direction will be discussed with selected candidates. This is a single book cover project. Print-ready files required (KDP and IngramSpark specs).

Experienced KDP Book Cover Designer Need.t: Experienced KDP Book Cover Designer Needed – Series Branding & Reusable Cover Template
Experienced KDP Book Cover Designer Need.d: Experienced KDP Book Cover Designer Needed – Series Branding & Reusable Cover Template Overview I am seeking an experienced book cover designer to create a professional, reusable cover system for an ongoing series of books. I have already created the AI artwork and will provide both the original image and my preferred front-cover crop. The successful candidate will professionally refine the artwork, prepare it for publication, and create a reusable series template that can be used across multiple future books. This is not a project to redesign the artwork from scratch. The character, colour palette, visual style, and overall concept have already been approved. About the Project The books are spiritual, metaphysical, self-development, and channeled works. The same central character will become the visual identity of the series, with different titles being applied to future books. The successful designer must understand Amazon KDP specifications and how cover dimensions change depending on page count and spine width. What I Need Artwork Refinement The supplied artwork is AI-generated and may require professional refinement, including: * Correcting AI-generated lettering and symbols * Correcting the spirit board lettering layout where required * Refining tattoos and small visual details * Cleaning up any AI-generated inconsistencies * Improving overall production quality while maintaining the existing style Wraparound Cover Creation The supplied image is currently designed as a front cover. The successful designer will: * Extend the artwork to create a full wraparound cover * Create a spine design * Prepare space for: * Book title * Subtitle * Author name * Back cover blurb * Author bio * Barcode The background artwork may need to be seamlessly extended across the spine and back cover. Typography & Readability Future titles will vary significantly in length. The designer should be comfortable: * Creating subtle gradients where necessary * Darkening or lightening areas of the artwork to improve readability * Creating a flexible title placement system * Maintaining a professional commercial appearance For example, the upper section of the spirit board may need to be subtly darkened to allow titles to stand out while preserving the artwork underneath. Deliverables * Amazon KDP Paperback Cover * Amazon KDP eBook Cover * Editable layered PSD files * Reusable series template * Font recommendations * Future title replacement instructions Ideal Candidate * Experienced with Amazon KDP * Strong Photoshop skills * Experience working with AI-generated artwork * Experience designing book series * Strong typography skills * Excellent attention to detail Ongoing Work This is intended to become an ongoing relationship. There will be multiple books within this series requiring future title changes, spine adjustments, and cover preparation. To Apply Please provide: * Examples of previous book covers * Examples of KDP projects * Examples of series branding work * Confirmation that you understand KDP spine calculations and varying cover dimensions Please include the words: "KDP Series Designer" in your application so I know you have read the entire brief.

Wedding Venue Sales Specialist - HIGH CO.d: We’re looking for a driven, polished, and highly personable sales professional to join our team part-time and help convert warm leads into confirmed bookings for a luxury resort and wedding venue experience. This is not a passive customer service role — we are specifically looking for someone who knows how to sell, follow up consistently, build relationships quickly, and confidently close bookings. Candidates with experience in wedding venues, event sales, hospitality sales, luxury travel, hotels, or short-term rentals are strongly preferred. You’ll be helping couples and guests plan some of the most important moments of their lives — from romantic getaways and proposals to weddings and special events — while representing a premium hospitality brand. Responsibilities Follow up quickly with inbound leads via phone, text, and email Convert wedding, lodging, and event inquiries into confirmed bookings Build excitement around the guest experience and venue offerings Guide potential guests through pricing, packages, availability, and next steps Maintain organized lead notes and pipeline follow-up Ensure no qualified lead falls through the cracks Deliver a high-end, professional, and memorable customer experience Help improve lead conversion and overall sales performance Ideal Candidate Proven sales experience with strong closing ability Wedding venue or event sales experience is a major plus Background in hospitality, hotels, luxury travel, or short-term rentals preferred Excellent communication skills — warm, confident, polished, and persuasive Strong follow-up and organizational habits Comfortable handling leads independently and working toward sales goals Able to create urgency and help move leads toward booking decisions Schedule Monday–Saturday 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM CST Compensation Base Hourly Commission on closed bookings Strong earning potential for high performers

Performance Marketer (Meta + Google) — T.t: Performance Marketer (Meta + Google) — Travel / Hospitality | Pay-per-SQL Model
Performance Marketer (Meta + Google) — T.d: About Us AKS Hospitality runs a premium Manali holiday business out of Delhi NCR — Volvo-included Manali packages starting from ₹9,900/person, paired with our own hotel inventory in Manali. Our funnel, Meta Pixel, CAPI, WhatsApp routing and Razorpay payments are all live and verified end-to-end. Website, offers, and product are in place. What we need now is a media buyer who has actually cracked the travel funnel — not a generalist trying it for the first time on us. What You'll Own Plan, launch, and run Meta Ads (Facebook + Instagram + WhatsApp) and Google Ads (Search, PMax, Demand Gen) for our Delhi NCR → Manali traveller funnel. Drive Sales Qualified Leads — defined together as travellers with intent to book within 30 days, the right travel dates, and budget fit for our package tiers. Daily delivery checks, creative testing cadence, weekly performance review with our team, and monthly strategy reset. Audience design: feeder cities, look-alike, exclusion lists (we already maintain a junk-lead exclusion audience), seasonality calendars. Tight feedback loop with our sales team on lead quality — we want a marketer who treats post-click data as oxygen, not an afterthought. Funnel/CRO suggestions for the booking page where it would lift conversion (we can hand them to our dev team). Must-Haves Verifiable travel or hospitality client work. Hotels, OTAs, holiday packages, resort brands, weekend getaway products, hill-station travel — case studies must include real numbers (spend, CAC, lead-to-booking ratio, ROAS). Meta Ads Manager + Google Ads at expert level — including conversion event setup, AEM priority lists, Custom Conversions, and Performance Max asset groups. Hands-on with Meta Pixel + Conversions API + GA4 + UTM hygiene. You can read a pixel debug log without help. Comfort working with click-to-WhatsApp ads and on-platform lead forms, not just landing page funnels. Communicate in plain English with a non-technical founder. No jargon dumps.

YouTube & short form Video Editor.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: More than 6 months, Less than 30 hrs/week
YouTube & short form Video Editor.d: We’re a luxury travel brand launching a YouTube channel taking viewers behind the scenes of the world’s finest hotel suites, resorts, and travel experiences. We shoot the footage ourselves and need a skilled editor to turn it into polished, cinematic content on an ongoing basis. What you’ll be editing: • Long-form hotel suite and resort walkthroughs • Behind-the-scenes and destination B-roll • Short-form vertical cutdowns from the same footage for YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and TikTok So alongside the main long-form videos, a big part of the role is repurposing footage into punchy vertical clips optimised for each platform — strong hooks in the first few seconds, captions, and pacing that suits short-form. What we’re looking for: • A cinematic, refined editing style — think elevated, calm, and aspirational, not fast-cut or gimmicky • Comfort working across both long-form and short-form formats • Strong sense of pacing, music selection, and colour grading • Clean motion graphics and tasteful text overlays • Excellent written and spoken English • Reliability and the ability to work to a consistent style guide and turnaround Engagement: Monthly retainer, with a paid trial edit first. Tell us your per-video rate and roughly how many videos per month you can comfortably handle (please note both long-form and short-form separately). To apply, please: 1. Share your portfolio or reel, ideally with travel, hospitality, or real-estate work — long-form and short-form examples both welcome 2. Name one travel or luxury YouTube channel whose editing you admire, and tell us in one or two sentences why 3. Start your reply with the word “Suite” so we know you read this

Luxury Hospitality Brand Narrative Strat.t: Luxury Hospitality Brand Narrative Strategist & Copywriter for Boutique Hotel Evolution
Luxury Hospitality Brand Narrative Strat.i: Hourly: $50.00 - $80.00 Expert Est. time: More than 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
Luxury Hospitality Brand Narrative Strat.d: Overview We are an independent luxury boutique hotel in a premier mountain resort destination in the American West. The property is entering an important new chapter, expanding from a highly regarded boutique hotel into a fuller destination hotel experience with new luxury suites, a new restaurant concept, an expanded spa and wellness center, elevated dining, curated guest experiences, and a stronger overall on-property amenity offering. We are looking for a senior creative strategist and copywriter who can help us weave these pieces into one clear, elevated brand story. This is not a basic copywriting project. We need someone who can help define the narrative, tone, positioning, and creative direction of the hotel as it evolves. The right person will understand luxury hospitality, boutique hotels, destination travel, editorial-style copy, and how to make a property feel distinct in a competitive market. The Challenge The hotel has historically been positioned as a luxury boutique property in the heart of a highly desirable mountain destination. With new suites, a new restaurant concept, a spa and wellness center, and more on-property amenities, we need to clarify the next chapter of the brand. We want the story to feel cohesive, refined, warm, and differentiated. We also need to better distinguish the property from a nearby lifestyle hotel competitor with strong brand visibility. The goal is not to become bigger, louder, or more corporate. The goal is to communicate that the hotel is now a more complete luxury destination while preserving the intimacy, warmth, independence, and service culture that make the property special. What We Need We are looking for someone to help create a brand narrative and messaging platform that can guide our website, copy, campaigns, photography direction, design updates, and guest-facing communications. Deliverables may include: Review of current website, copy, tone, and visual identity Review of competitor positioning Clear positioning recommendation for the hotel’s next chapter A refined brand narrative tying together the hotel, new suites, spa, wellness, dining, restaurant concepts, concierge, and destination experience Key differentiators and messaging pillars Tone-of-voice guide Homepage messaging hierarchy Sample rewritten homepage copy Suggested copy direction for core pages: suites, spa, dining, amenities, concierge, and experiences Creative direction notes for design, photography, mood, typography, and visual storytelling Practical recommendations our internal marketing team and website developer can execute Ideal Candidate You may be a fit if you have experience with: Luxury hospitality Boutique hotels Destination resorts High-end restaurants, spas, wellness, or travel brands Brand positioning Website copywriting Editorial-style luxury copy Creative direction Brand voice and messaging systems We are especially interested in someone who can write with restraint, clarity, and sophistication. The tone should feel elevated but not pretentious, warm but not casual, refined but not generic. What We Are Not Looking For We are not looking for a logo designer only. We are not looking for generic AI-generated copy. We are not looking for trendy, overly fluffy luxury language. We are not looking for someone who simply repeats phrases like “unforgettable experiences,” “hidden gem,” or “where luxury meets adventure.” We need someone who can think strategically and write beautifully. Project Structure We would like to begin with a paid discovery and brand narrative phase. If the fit is strong, this may lead to additional website copy, campaign copy, email copy, print collateral, and ongoing creative direction. Please include: Two or three examples of luxury, hospitality, travel, restaurant, real estate, wellness, or lifestyle brand work. A brief explanation of how you approach brand narrative projects. Your recommended process for a project like this. Your hourly rate or fixed project estimate. A short note on how you would begin differentiating an independent luxury boutique hotel from a larger lifestyle hotel competitor. We are looking for someone who can bring a refined, strategic, editorial eye to the next evolution of an independent luxury hotel.

As part of our growth plans, we are expanding beyond traditional accommodation services into a broader hospitality, lifestyle, wellness, concierge, guest experience, and technology-enabled ecosystem. We are looking for an experienced Brand Identity Expert to help elevate our visual identity and create a strong, premium, and scalable brand presence. Project Objective We require a complete review and refinement of our existing brand identity. This is not a logo-only project. We are seeking a professional who can help create a cohesive brand identity system that reflects premium hospitality, trust, modern luxury, professionalism, innovation, and exceptional guest experiences. The identity should be suitable for use across digital platforms, presentations, marketing materials, social media, partnerships, investor discussions, and future business initiatives. Scope of Work Brand Identity Review Review and assess our current: • Logo • Color palette • Typography • Website appearance • Marketing materials • Social media presence • Visual positioning Identify strengths, weaknesses, and opportunities for improvement. Brand Identity Development Develop or refine: • Logo system (if enhancements are recommended) • Color palette • Typography system • Visual identity framework • Supporting design elements • Iconography style • Photography and imagery direction • Overall brand look and feel The identity should communicate: • Premium hospitality • Trust and credibility • Modern and sophisticated design • Lifestyle-driven experiences • Scalability for future growth • International appeal Brand Guidelines Create a professional brand guideline document covering: • Logo usage • Clear space and sizing rules • Color specifications • Typography standards • Visual identity rules • Photography guidelines • Social media branding standards • Presentation branding standards • Marketing collateral standards Brand Architecture Recommendations Provide recommendations on how future collections, sub-brands, services, and business initiatives can remain visually connected to the master brand while maintaining clear differentiation. Deliverables • Brand Identity Assessment • Refined Brand Identity System • Logo Usage Guidelines • Color Palette • Typography Guidelines • Brand Style Guide • Visual Direction for Digital Platforms • Social Media Visual Direction • Brand Architecture Recommendations • Editable Source Files Ideal Candidate We are looking for professionals with experience in: • Hospitality • Luxury brands • Travel and tourism • Real estate • Lifestyle brands • Premium consumer brands Please provide: • Portfolio • Relevant branding projects • Case studies (if available) • Proposed approach • Estimated timeline • Estimated budget Confidentiality Requirement Due to the strategic and commercially sensitive nature of this project, shortlisted candidates will be required to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) before receiving detailed business information, internal documents, strategic plans, concepts, or other confidential materials. By applying for this project, candidates acknowledge that all information shared during the engagement remains confidential and may not be disclosed, reproduced, distributed, or used for any purpose other than evaluating and performing the project. Project Goal To create a distinctive, premium, and scalable brand identity that supports long-term growth, strengthens market positioning, and establishes a consistent visual presence across all customer, partner, investor, and digital touchpoints.

Rebranding an Existing Business.i: Hourly: $15.00 - $35.00 Intermediate Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Less than 30 hrs/
Rebranding an Existing Business.d: - I started Hotel Linen Services in July 2018. It is a commercial laundry facility that was built by hotel operators, for hotels. - We bought state of the art equipment that runs our purpose built plant. - we are circa 1000m2 under 1 x roof in Melbourne. - We currently wash circa 8000 hotel beds per day of hotel linen (circa 100,000 items per day) - We are privately owned (myself and a school friend). - Our staff (circa 60 pax) are all in house - not outsourced contractors. Multicultural (indian, nepalese, Vietnamese, Filipino, Columbian, and many others). - We process circa 100 tonnes of linen per week. - Our machinery is state of the art focusing on quality, signficant energy and water efficiencies. - We are currently growing with an ambitious plan to be 3 x our current size in 3 years. - HLS is different to other laundries as we dont just wash anything. Laundries own their own linen and hotels rent the linen as part of the wash cost from laundries. We at HLS only have 16 items of pooled stock, as we only service 4-5 star hotels. No motels, caravan parks, backpackers, aged care or hospitals. This is an intentional strategic decision to maintain quality and consitency and operational efficencies. Other laundries have closer to 100 line items. - We have 3 x core Values 1. WE GIVE A SHEET- CARE: We care about our people, customers, suppliers, assets and the business 2. WE MAKE SHEET HAPPEN - CAN DO ATTITUDE: We work hard, display positivity, and always look for solutions. 3. WE KEEP SHEETSIMPLE - SIMPLICITY: We always look to reduce complexity. - We have partnered with a 3rd party transport company that we have been with since inception. They have 4 x drivers,driving 4 trucks 12 hours a day, 6 days a week. 1 x van doing the same hours also. Plan is to have decals of the new branding on the transport vehicles. - The current website was meant to be short term and we update as we grew (but never got around to it). With the current push to potentially expand into a new premises, branding and style guides etc are key to lifting our game and seperating us as different from our competitors Identified HLS Target Market Great People! Great Brands! Shared values! Victorian hotels with upwards of 40 beds 80% of HLS volume utilise HLS' standard linen line Three Uniques 1. Service: Quality, Delivery, Responsive, Can-do 2. Same: We Are Hotel Owners 3. Specialisation: We Only Service Hotels and Accommodation The HLS Guarantee: If you need linen we will get it to you same day - WE MAKE SHEET HAPPEN The Market: - The market is small, and the customers are typically sticky. Changing linen suppliers once every 5-7 years typically. Its a non sexy industry, but a hotels 6th most expensive line item in the Profit and Loss. The contracts are often 3-5 years in length. There are only 4 x other main competitors in this space, and 2 x other outliers. 1. SPL (link removed)/ Private Equity Mega plant Market majority, losing share consistently as too big to care. 2. Gouge Private family owned (link removed)/ Mega plant. Market leaders. 3. Princess Linen Private Family owned (link removed)/ Main focus is hospitals, hotels secondary. 4. JT's (link removed)/ Similar size to us. 5. Geelong Surf Coast (link removed)/ Owned by a syndicate Based in Geelong and service that region predominatley and part metro melb 6. AAA Linen smaller player thats trying to obtain makret share. Privately owned (link removed)/ Rebrading Thoughts; - we want to continue disrupting the market. We are young owners, hotel operators, nimble, understading and CARE/Hospitable focused. - we rely heaviliy on technology for continious improvement - we like to think we are similar to 'who gives a crap toilet paper' Non sexy industry giving it a fresh look... Clever play on words, socially responsible, good vibes. (link removed)/ - I was also toying with the idea of a hotelesque website where you 'think' youre on a website to book a hotel, but you're changing your linen supplier. Playing on the built by hoteliers for hoteliers advantage we have. You will see the above laundry websites are all very similar - we want to stand out as different... I look forward to workshopping this with you...

Photo editor.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30 hrs/week
Photo editor.d: Real Estate Photo Editing Specialist – Photos (Vacation Rentals) About Us We are a vacation rental management company based in Saint-Martin (Caribbean) managing approximately 100 villas, apartments and luxury vacation rentals. We are looking for an experienced real estate photo editor who is already familiar with Nodalview HDR photography workflows. The objective is not simply to edit photos, but to create a consistent, premium visual identity across our entire portfolio. Source Material Photos are taken using: Nodalview HDR mode Villas and apartments Sea views Pools Tropical landscapes Luxury interiors Most photos are already correctly exposed through HDR processing. Your role is therefore to enhance and finalize the images while maintaining a natural and realistic look. Required Editing Perspective & Geometry Vertical correction Perspective correction Straightening Lens correction Exposure & Colors Fine exposure adjustments White balance correction Color consistency across the entire property Natural contrast Exterior Enhancement Pool water enhancement Sea color enhancement Sky improvement when necessary Vegetation enhancement Interior Enhancement Improve brightness and clarity Balance indoor/outdoor exposure through windows Correct color casts Create a clean and welcoming atmosphere Object Removal Occasionally remove: Electrical cables Reflections Minor stains Small distracting objects What We DO NOT Want ❌ Fake HDR look ❌ Over-saturated colors ❌ Unrealistic blue pools ❌ Artificial skies ❌ Excessive sharpening ❌ "Real estate brochure" look We prefer: Natural Elegant Premium Realistic Deliverables For each photo: High-resolution JPG Optimized for Airbnb, VRBO, Booking.com and direct website use Consistent visual style across all photos Volume We manage approximately 100 properties. Potential volume: 200 to 1,000 photos per month Continuous long-term collaboration Application Requirements Please provide: Real estate editing portfolio Before/after examples Examples specifically edited from Nodalview HDR photos (if available) Turnaround time Price: per photo per batch of 50 photos per batch of 100 photos Paid Test Shortlisted candidates will receive a paid test consisting of: Interior photos Sea view photos Pool photos Difficult window exposure photos The selected freelancer will become our preferred photo editing partner for long-term collaboration.

AI Visualization Specialist Needed – Lux.t: AI Visualization Specialist Needed – Luxury Residential Ship Project
AI Visualization Specialist Needed – Lux.d: AI Visualization Specialist Needed – Luxury Residential Ship Project We are seeking a talented AI visualization specialist to help transform existing renders of a large luxury residential ship into stunning, photorealistic marketing imagery. This is an opportunity to become an ongoing creative resource for a unique project that combines luxury living, wellness, sustainability, and global exploration. ## What We Need We already have: * Existing ship renders * Concept imagery * Design references * Marketing materials We are NOT looking for someone to redesign the ship. Instead, we are looking for someone who can take our existing imagery and make it significantly more realistic, compelling, and visually impressive. Examples of future assignments may include: * Enhancing exterior ship imagery * Creating more realistic lighting and reflections * Improving water, sky, and environmental realism * Adding realistic satellite domes, communications equipment, and exterior details * Developing elegant paint and graphics treatments that enhance the ship's appearance without changing its structure * Creating cinematic destination scenes * Producing marketing-quality hero images for websites, brochures, social media, and investor presentations ## The Ideal Candidate You may be a rising star rather than a veteran. We care much more about your eye for realism and quality than how many years of experience you have. You should: * Be highly skilled with AI image-generation tools * Have a strong sense of architecture, perspective, lighting, and realism * Understand luxury branding and presentation * Be comfortable working from existing images and references * Be responsive and easy to collaborate with * Be interested in a long-term working relationship ## Preferred Tools Experience with some or all of the following: * MidJourney * Flux * Stable Diffusion * Photoshop * ComfyUI * Other AI visualization workflows ## Preferred Experience Any of the following is a plus: * Architectural visualization * Luxury real estate marketing * Resorts and hospitality * Yachts and marine projects * High-end lifestyle marketing ## To Apply Please provide: 1. Your three best examples of photorealistic AI-generated imagery. 2. Any examples involving architecture, luxury real estate, hospitality, transportation, yachts, or similar projects. 3. A brief description of your workflow. 4. Your typical pricing per completed image. 5. Your availability. To confirm that you have read this posting, please begin your proposal with the phrase: "366 Meters" ## Paid Test Assignment Qualified applicants may be invited to complete a small paid test assignment using existing project imagery. Our goal is to identify one outstanding individual who can become our go-to visualization partner as the project develops.

Framer Expert Needed – Luxury Real Estat.t: Framer Expert Needed – Luxury Real Estate Website, Lead Generation Landing & Page Mobile compatible
Framer Expert Needed – Luxury Real Estat.d: # Framer Expert Required – Luxury Real Estate Website & Lead Generation Landing Page ## Project Overview I am seeking an experienced Framer designer/developer to design and build a premium luxury real estate website and dedicated lead generation landing page for an exclusive townhouse development. The website must have a modern, luxury and cinematic feel similar to high-end property developer websites and be built in Framer. It must be fully responsive across desktop, tablet and mobile devices and capable of displaying high-resolution renders, images and marketing videos. The primary objective of the website is to generate qualified buyer enquiries, build a purchaser database and professionally showcase the development. ## Reference Website Design The overall design quality, presentation, user experience, animations and premium feel should be similar to: https://estelittlebay.com.au/ This website is provided as a design reference only, there will additional changes. Pearl Residences will have its own branding, colours, content, layout improvements and additional functionality. However, the overall luxury presentation, cinematic feel, quality of animations and user experience should be of a similar standard. --- ## Client Will Provide The client already has: • Active Framer subscription • Domain name • Project branding and logo • High-resolution architectural renders • Interior and exterior imagery • Aerial visuals • Promotional videos • Marketing brochure • Project content and information The freelancer is not required to create branding, renders, videos or project content from scratch. --- ## Scope of Works ### Website Design & Development • Design and build website in Framer • Premium luxury design and presentation • Fully responsive across desktop, tablet and mobile • Optimised for iPhone and Android devices • Modern animations and transitions • Cinematic full-screen sections • High-resolution image and video integration • Fast loading performance • SEO-ready structure • Easy content management through Framer CMS • Professional typography and layout ### Website Sections • Homepage • Project Overview • Interactive Site Plan • Gallery & Renders • Lifestyle Section • Location & Amenities • Construction Updates • Contact Page • Dedicated Lead Generation Landing Page ### Homepage Features • Full-screen hero video or aerial footage • Project introduction • Stage 1 & Stage 2 Sold Out banner • Stage 3 Now Selling banner • Register Interest button • Request Brochure button • Request Price List button • Book Private Appointment button --- ## Interactive Site Plan • Interactive townhouse site plan • Available residences • Under Contract residences • Sold residences User functionality: • Hover over townhouse to display information • Click townhouse to display property information • Easy status updates through CMS --- ## CMS Database Create a simple Framer CMS database allowing easy updates to: • Residence Number • Status (Available / Under Contract / Sold) • Key Features • Images • Property Description The CMS structure should allow future addition of residences, future stages and project updates. The colour scheme and residence status must be easily editable. --- ## Render Gallery • High-resolution image gallery • Exterior renders • Interior renders • Kitchen, living and bedroom imagery • Courtyard and pool imagery • Image sliders • Lightbox viewing • Hover effects --- ## Lifestyle & Amenities Create a premium lifestyle section highlighting: • Beaches • Cafes • Restaurants • Parks • Golf Course • Nature Reserves • Shopping Facilities • Schools • Nambucca Heads CBD Include a premium "Amenities Within 5km" section showing nearby attractions, lifestyle facilities and key destinations. --- ## Construction & Developer Section Include a section highlighting: • Reinforced concrete construction • Superior acoustic performance • Energy efficiency • Durability and low maintenance • NSW Home Warranty Insurance • Builder and developer credentials --- ## Lead Generation System The website's primary purpose is lead generation. Project brochures, pricing information and detailed sales material must not be publicly accessible. Visitors must first complete an enquiry form. ### Required Fields • Full Name • Email Address • Confirm Email Address • Mobile Number ### Optional Fields • Buyer Type • Budget Range • Preferred Residence ### Email Verification Process 1. User submits enquiry form 2. Verification email is automatically sent 3. User confirms email address 4. Brochure and project information are automatically emailed An automated thank-you email must also be sent confirming receipt of the enquiry. All verified enquiries, brochure requests and price list requests must be automatically sent to both the client and the nominated real estate agent via email. ### Lead Management All enquiries must: • Be automatically emailed to the client immediately upon submission • Be automatically emailed to the nominated real estate agent immediately upon submission • Be stored in a lead database • Be exportable to Excel/CSV • Integrate with CRM systems where possible • Record brochure requests, price list requests and appointment requests • Record traffic source and UTM tracking data Lead notification emails must contain: • Full Name • Email Address • Mobile Number • Buyer Type (if completed) • Budget Range (if completed) • Preferred Residence (if completed) • Enquiry Source • Date & Time Submitted • Landing Page Submitted From --- ## Landing Page Create a dedicated lead generation landing page featuring: • Hero render or video • Project overview • Key project features • Strong call-to-action • Lead capture form • Mobile optimisation --- ## Integrations • Google Analytics 4 • Google Search Console • Microsoft Clarity • Meta/Facebook Pixel • Mailchimp • HubSpot • Zapier • Airtable --- ## Analytics & Tracking Track: • Website visitors • Lead conversions • Brochure requests • Price list requests • Traffic sources • Visitor locations • Device types • User behaviour • Scroll depth • Click tracking • Most visited pages Track enquiry sources including: • Google Organic Search • Google Ads • Facebook • Instagram • LinkedIn • Realestate.com.au • Domain.com.au • Ray White • Referral Websites • Direct Traffic UTM tracking must be configured so all enquiries can be traced back to their source. --- ## Additional Features • Sticky Call-to-Action Button • Brochure Request Popup • Smooth Page Transitions • Scroll Animations • Hover Effects • Image Sliders • Expandable Content Sections • Dropdown Menus • Pop-ups and Modals • Interactive Menus • Social Media Integration --- ## Performance Optimisation • Image Optimisation • Video Optimisation • Lazy Loading • Mobile Optimisation • Fast Page Speed --- ## Website Publishing • Connect website to the client's domain • Publish website live on Framer • Confirm SSL certificate is active • Ensure website is indexed correctly by Google --- ## SEO Setup • Meta Titles • Meta Descriptions • Open Graph setup for social sharing • Favicon setup • Basic SEO setup for project-related keywords --- ## Ownership & Handover Requirements Upon completion, the successful freelancer must provide: • Full Framer project transfer • Transfer of website to the client's Framer account • Full ownership of the website • Full administrator access • Transfer of all CMS collections • Transfer of all content and assets • Transfer of all integrations • Transfer of all analytics and tracking configurations • Transfer of any custom code used • No Framer remix links or developer ownership rights are to be retained after project completion. • Client will have exclusive ownership and control of the project. The website must not be dependent on the developer's personal accounts after handover. --- ## Handover Training Provide a short handover guide or video showing how to: • Edit website content • Update residence information • Change residence status • Add images and content • Access leads and enquiries • Access analytics and tracking systems --- ## Testing Requirements The website must be fully tested on: • Desktop • Mobile • Tablet • Chrome • Safari • Edge • Firefox Verify that: • Forms are working correctly • Email verification works correctly • Brochure delivery works correctly • Lead notifications are functioning correctly • Analytics tracking is functioning correctly • Mobile responsiveness is functioning correctly • All links and buttons work correctly --- ## Acceptance Criteria The project will be considered complete when: • Website is live on the client's domain • Framer project has been transferred to the client's Framer account • All forms are functioning correctly • Email verification process is functioning correctly • Brochure delivery system is functioning correctly • Lead notifications are being received by both the client and nominated real estate agent • Analytics and tracking systems are installed and operating correctly • Website is fully responsive across desktop, tablet and mobile devices • All agreed sections and functionality have been completed • Client has reviewed and approved the final website --- ## Timeline • Start Immediately • Completion within 7 Days • Daily progress updates and prompt communication are required throughout the project --- ## Budget **Budget: $250 USD (Fixed Price)** Bonus at successful completion may be considered and 5 star review --- ## Revisions • Unlimited revisions until client satisfaction is achieved --- ## Post-Launch Support • 15 days post-launch support included • Bug fixes during the support period • Assistance with minor adjustments and refinements if required • Support to ensure all forms, integrations, analytics and website functionality continue operating correctly after launch --- ## Preferred Experience • Proven Framer experience • Luxury website design experience • Real estate or property development website experience preferred • Lead generation experience • SEO and analytics implementation experience Preference will be given to freelancers who can demonstrate previous Framer real estate or property development websites. Please provide examples of similar Framer websites you have personally designed and developed. Only apply if you are confident you can complete the project within the budget and timeframe.

Sr. Director of Instructional Design w/A.t: Sr. Director of Instructional Design w/Agency
Sr. Director of Instructional Design w/A.d: CourseCREEK is a remote e-learning agency that partners with EdTech vendors and enterprises to deliver instructional design, LMS services, marketing, and enterprise sales. We create scalable learning for healthcare, logistics, fintech, executive coaching, manufacturing, associations, government programs, and corporate L&D. The role Lead instructional design delivery for enterprise clients, qualify and scope complex opportunities, provide occasional oversight and QA for junior designers, and support sales and business. You will join the CEO and team on group sales calls as an SME to help close deals. Key responsibilities - Design and develop courses using SCORM, Articulate Rise, Storyline, and other authoring tools. - Scope, estimate, and propose solutions for complex instructional design engagements. - Provide QA, style/standards guidance, and occasional mentorship for junior IDs. - Support pre-sales: proposals, RFP responses, and sales calls. - Execute LMS admin tasks, implementations, migrations, reporting, and user/workflow configuration. - Contribute to client onboarding and, when required, to ongoing client success. Required - The contractor will have a well-established instructional design agency with a proven track record. We are less interested in freelancers; we are seeking team members who have previously operated their own eLearning agencies and possess the maturity and experience that accompany that experience. - Senior-level instructional design experience with SCORM and Articulate Rise/Storyline (plus other authoring tools). - Proven work on enterprise L&D projects (healthcare, logistics, fintech, manufacturing, associations, government, or similar). - Strong consulting, stakeholder management, and project-scoping skills. - Experience providing guidance to Jr. level designers. - Sales/business development experience or comfort participating in client-facing sales activities. - Excellent written and verbal communication and time/prioritization skills. Nice to have - Migrations, configuration, and reporting expertise in LMS administration and implementation across major platforms. - Experience in video production and editing. - Certifications in LMS platforms or authoring tools. Why CourseCREEK - Remote, flexible contract role (part‑time) with varied enterprise clients. - Steady lead flow and opportunities to shape high-impact learning programs. - Growing company (~26% annual growth) with strong partner relationships. - We accept anyone with a budget, and we offer a fun international team and very interesting projects. From global manufacturers to brand-name logistics to hospitals to artisan cowboy hats.

eLearning Course Producer - Instructiona.t: eLearning Course Producer - Instructional Video and Workbook Creation
eLearning Course Producer - Instructiona.d: ABOUT THE PROJECT We are building a forex trading education platform for serious retail traders and are looking for an experienced eLearning video producer to handle the full production process for our first structured online course. We will start with a paid sample module before committing to the full course. Payment for the sample is negotiable. COURSE DETAILS Course name: Forex Foundations, From Zero to First Trade Purpose: Entry-level forex education for complete beginners Total modules: 8 Length per module: 3 to 5 minutes of finished video Format: Faceless. No presenter on camera. Delivery: Digital download or hosted link WHAT YOU WILL PRODUCE PER MODULE 1. Branded slide presentation Each module opens and runs on professionally designed slides. Slides must match our brand exactly. Near black background, gold accents, white text. Full brand kit provided including colour codes, fonts, and logo files. 2. Screen recording integration Certain modules require screen recordings of the MetaTrader 5 trading platform showing charts, indicators, and trade setups. You must be able to capture, edit, and integrate these recordings into the final video. 3. Voiceover sync We will supply all voiceover audio files. You are responsible for syncing the audio to slides and screen recordings, editing for timing, and adding transitions where necessary. 4. PDF workbook per module Each module requires a matching branded PDF workbook covering key takeaways, fill-in exercises, and a checklist. Must match the slide design visually. 5. Final delivery One MP4 video file at 1080p minimum and one PDF workbook per module, named clearly by module number. MODULE OVERVIEW Module 1: What is Forex, markets, pairs, sessions Module 2: Reading charts, timeframes, candlesticks, structure Module 3: Support and Resistance, zones not lines Module 4: Liquidity and Institutional vs Retail thinking Module 5: Risk Management, position sizing, the 1 percent rule, daily limits Module 6: Trading Psychology, discipline, emotions, and realistic expectations Module 7: Your First Trade, setup checklist, execution, journaling Module 8: What comes next, intermediate path and passive options BRAND GUIDELINES Background: Near black Accent colour: Gold Body text: Off white Fonts: Cormorant Garamond for headings, Barlow for body text Tone: Premium, serious, and direct. Not beginner YouTube style. Think institutional with an accessible voice. Logo: Horizontal and icon versions provided in PNG format. Reference: 10 existing strategy documents built in this design system are provided as visual benchmark. WHAT WE PROVIDE Complete written script for each module All voiceover audio files labelled by module and section Full brand kit including logos, colour codes, and font specifications Visual reference documents matching the production standard required Feedback on the sample module within 48 hours of delivery One revision round per module WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR Demonstrable experience producing eLearning or online course content is required. Finance or trading niche experience is an advantage but not essential if your portfolio shows high quality branded video education. You must be comfortable with slide design to a supplied brand standard, screen recording capture and editing of financial software, audio sync and basic sound editing, and PDF workbook design matching video slides. Please do not apply if you have no eLearning portfolio samples, cannot follow a strict brand system, or are not comfortable working from a technical script in a subject you are not already familiar with. SAMPLE PROJECT We will begin with Module 1 only. Full script will be provided. If the sample meets our standard, we proceed to the remaining 7 modules immediately. BUDGET We are looking for a long-term working relationship across three courses in total. The right producer will be our go-to for all production work. Please quote your rate per module including video and PDF workbook, your estimated turnaround per module, and whether you offer a package rate for the full 8-module course. TO APPLY Please include one or two samples of eLearning or course video content you have produced, a brief description of your production process from script to final video, your quote for the sample module and the full course, your estimated turnaround per module, and any questions you have before starting. Proposals without portfolio samples will not be reviewed.

Visual Identity Designer for App-Based L.t: Visual Identity Designer for App-Based Learning Platform (Brand, Templates & Course Content System)
Visual Identity Designer for App-Based L.d: Design Brief: Visual Identity Development: PR for Beginners Project Overview: PR for Beginners is a new educational platform designed to simplify public relations for a beginner audience. The visual identity will form the foundation of an app-based learning ecosystem and must scale seamlessly across educational content, presentations, video learning modules, downloadable resources, social media, and future digital products. The visual identity should feel vibrant, approachable, and typographically strong, reflecting the bold and educational nature of the platform. We are seeking a modern, scalable design system that feels energetic, intelligent, and highly engaging while remaining simple enough to apply consistently across an evolving digital environment. Objective: Create a distinctive visual identity that is: • bold, bright, and memorable • approachable and beginner-friendly • typographically led • professional without feeling corporate • flexible enough to scale across an app-based learning experience, social content, presentations, educational resources, and future digital products Target Audience: Individuals new to PR, including: • small business owners • entrepreneurs and founders • content creators • consultants • students and early-career professionals Typically aged 25–55, they are digitally savvy, time-poor, and looking for practical, accessible guidance presented in an engaging and modern way. Creative Direction: The identity should feel: • bold • contemporary • energetic • intelligent • optimistic • approachable We are particularly drawn to brands that use typography as the hero element, supported by strong colour, dynamic layouts, and simple graphic devices that can extend across app / digital and motion applications. Design Elements: Typography: Typography should be central to the identity, using bold, modern sans-serif typefaces with strong hierarchy, excellent readability, and plenty of personality. Colour Palette: A bright and confident colour palette inspired by the references below. The system should feel distinctive, energetic, and work effectively across app, digital, social, presentation, and print environments. Logo & Identity System: A typographically driven logo and flexible visual system that can adapt across multiple touchpoints. Please explore: • horizontal and stacked lock-ups • icon/avatar treatments • creative typographic interactions • simple graphic and motion graphic devices that can scale across the brand Deliverables: Logo design (horizontal and stacked formats) Icon/avatar treatment Colour palette with HEX values Typography recommendations (headline, subhead, and body fonts). Basic brand guidelines (how to use the elements cohesively). Motion-ready elements, such as an animated logo concept or recommendations on how elements should move when animated. Application Examples: Examples demonstrating the identity applied across: • app-style learning screens • social media templates • educational content presentation emplates • presentation and course slide templates (such as Google Slides, PowerPoint or Canva) • workbook templates • website headers • video and motion frames The presentation templates should be designed as a scalable learning system that can be used to create educational content within the platform. Templates should consider hierarchy, readability, animation potential, and consistency across multiple course modules. Design Process & Feedback: We would like to see three initial creative directions for review, exploring different typography styles, colour approaches, and visual systems. Following concept selection, we anticipate up to three rounds of refinement before final delivery. Timeline: Preferred timeline: 2–3 weeks from commencement. References & Inspiration Links to web / social media visual inspiration / references: https://koto.studio/work/bolt/ https://koto.studio/work/fitbit-ace-lte/ https://www.instagram.com/dnco/ https://www.instagram.com/thisiscollins/ https://dnco.com/work/florentia-village https://dnco.com/work/city-nation-place https://dnco.com/work/national-galleries-of-scotland Final Notes: We are looking for a visual identity that feels fresh, modern, scalable, and motion-ready. Most importantly, it should help transform PR for Beginners into a recognisable and engaging digital learning brand.

Logo Refresh for Established Medical Cli.t: Logo Refresh for Established Medical Clinic — Brand Identity Expert Needed
Logo Refresh for Established Medical Cli.d: We are a medical clinic that has proudly served our community for 15 years. We are relocating to a new facility next month and need a skilled graphic designer to modernize our existing logo — without losing the brand recognition and identity we have built with our patients. This is NOT a full rebrand. We need a thoughtful evolution: refining and updating our logo for today's digital and print environments while preserving the core look our community already knows and trusts. PROJECT SCOPE - Review our existing logo and identify what to preserve vs. what to modernize - We want the logo to clearly communicate that we help people with allergies - Deliver 2–3 refreshed logo concepts with a brief explanation of each direction - Up to 2 rounds of revisions on the selected concept - Final files in all formats: SVG, EPS, PNG (transparent background), PDF, and JPEG - Deliver full color, white/reversed, and black & white versions - Include a simplified icon/mark version (no text) if appropriate - Provide a simple one-page brand guide: color codes (Hex, RGB, CMYK), fonts, and spacing rules - All files must be print-ready for signage and stationery at our new location WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR - 5+ years of logo and brand identity design experience - Strong portfolio showing logo refresh or evolution work — before and after examples required - Proficiency in Adobe Illustrator (vector source files are mandatory) - Understanding of healthcare design: trust, professionalism, and approachability - Experience designing for both digital use and large-format print (signage, banners) - Clear communicator who can explain design decisions to non-designers - Ability to meet a firm 3–4 week deadline Preferred but not required: - Prior experience with medical clinics, hospitals, or healthcare organizations - Familiarity with accessibility and color contrast standards - Experience preparing files for print vendors (bleed, crop marks, color profiles) --- DESIGN PRINCIPLES — IMPORTANT Any refresh must respect the following: 1. Identity continuity: Our existing patients must recognize the updated logo immediately. A dramatic redesign is not what we need. 2. Trust and professionalism: The design must convey clinical competence and warmth. 3. Timelessness: We want enduring, not trendy. 4. Versatility: Must work from a business card to large exterior building signage. 5. Legibility: Clean, readable typography at all sizes. --- TIMELINE We need final deliverables within 3–4 weeks.
