Amazon Listing & 3D Packaging Visualizer.t: Amazon Listing & 3D Packaging Visualizer (Luxury / Minimalist Aesthetic)
Amazon Listing & 3D Packaging Visualizer.d: About the Project We are launching a premium health optimization brand on Amazon UK called Candor Nutrition. Our flagship product is a 100% Grass-Fed New Zealand Bovine Liver supplement. Priced at £34.99, this is a high-end, zero-excipient product targeted at biohackers and health optimizers. We are seeking a high-caliber graphic designer and 3D visualizer to build out our complete suite of 7 Amazon listing images and a full set of Amazon A+ Content modules. The Aesthetic: "Apothecary Brutalism" Our brand identity is the exact opposite of typical Amazon supplements. We do not want loud graphics, neon colors, drop shadows, or generic "gym-bro" badges. Our aesthetic is "Clinic Organic" and highly editorial. Our physical packaging relies entirely on strict grid geometry and absolute typographic restraint using Pantone 9082 and Black. PDF Our digital assets utilize clean serif headers and highly clinical, premium layouts. PDF The visual tone must feel like a luxury skincare line, a high-end cosmetic brand, or a premium scientific journal. We use stark "Mist" off-white backgrounds, raw natural textures (stone, matte glass), and precise typography (Inter and Newsreader fonts). Project Scope & Deliverables 1x Pure White Background Hero Image (3D Render) 6x Infographic / Lifestyle Images (Purity, Clinical Potency, Traceability QR, Daily Protocol, Packaging Architecture, Nutritional Panel) Standard Amazon A+ Content Design (Layouts and high-res assets) The Phased "Modular" Workflow Our physical packaging is currently in mass production in the UK. Because we operate remotely on UTC-5 hours, we require a designer capable of a split, parallel workflow: Phase 1 (Immediate): Build the structural layout templates for all slides. Render the primary component (a 250ml frosted amber glass jar with a black ribbed lid and matte label). Leave precise, blank placeholders for the outer carton. Phase 2 (In 3-4 Weeks): We will provide you with high-resolution, natural-light macro photography of the final, physical "soft-touch" carton from our manufacturer. You will use these photos to perfectly map the real-world texture onto your 3D carton render, and seamlessly drop it into the completed layouts. The Ideal Candidate Specializes in minimalist, brutalist, or luxury e-commerce design. Has a portfolio heavily weighted toward premium cosmetics, high-end skincare, or boutique wellness (not mass-market energy drinks or weight loss pills). Demonstrates exceptional typographic discipline and grid-based layout skills. Possesses advanced 3D rendering capabilities, specifically making frosted glass and matte paper look physical, heavy, and realistic (not like cheap plastic).

graphic designer / print designer to rec.t: graphic designer / print designer to recreate a two-sided DIN A5 package
graphic designer / print designer to rec.d: We need a professional graphic designer / print designer to recreate a two-sided DIN A5 package insert for Blausweta-Rasur. The current visual references are included in the asset package. Please do not simply upscale the AI drafts. The task is to rebuild the front and back side cleanly in Illustrator/InDesign as a proper print-ready layout with editable text, real vector logo, clean icons, correct bleed, and final PDF export for SAXOPRINT. Blausweta-Rasur is a German specialist retailer / online shop for shaving, grooming, razors, razor blades, care products and barber supplies. The shop serves private customers, barbers, salons and resellers. Blausweta-Rasur is already established on eBay and now wants to motivate marketplace customers to order directly in the official online shop. The insert should communicate: professional specialist retailer, reliable shipping, fair prices, direct shop advantages, loyalty/bonus points, referral benefits and shop-exclusive discount codes. Main goal: Customers who may know Blausweta from eBay or another marketplace should switch to the official online shop. Important campaign message: - Customers can save more directly in the Blausweta online shop. - First direct shop order after switching from eBay or another marketplace: 7% discount with code SHOP7. - Follow-up order / next direct shop order: 5% discount with code DANKE5. - Bonus points are collected additionally and can be redeemed. - Referral discounts are also available. Important: All discounts are percentages. Do not use € discount wording. Required deliverables: 1. Editable source file: Adobe Illustrator .ai preferred, or InDesign .indd with linked assets. 2. Print-ready two-sided PDF for SAXOPRINT, CMYK, PDF/X-4 preferred. 3. Front side and back side as separate high-resolution PNG/JPG previews. 4. All text must remain editable in the source file. 5. Use the official logo from the provided logo files. Do not trace/recreate the logo from the AI draft. 6. Use the clean warehouse header image without any baked-in logo. The logo will be positioned manually in the layout. Format / print setup: - DIN A5 flyer / package insert, double-sided, portrait orientation. - Final trim size: 148 × 210 mm. - Artwork size with bleed: 152 × 214 mm. - Bleed: 2 mm on every side. - Keep all important text/logos/icons at least 3 mm inside the final trim edge. Prefer 5 mm for safety. - Background images that touch the edge must extend into the bleed. - CMYK color mode. - Minimum image resolution: 250 dpi at final size, ideally 300 dpi. - Embed all fonts or convert to outlines in the print PDF. Design direction: Keep the style very close to the references: - Clean, modern, professional specialist retailer look. - White background with deep navy / purple accents. - No colorful mix. Stay in the Blausweta navy/purple world. - No gold, no green, no Trustpilot badge. - Avoid wedding/thank-you-card feeling. - Avoid playful hearts and handwritten romantic scripts. - The back side should feel like a professional shop benefit page. - The front side can remain warmer and personal, but still businesslike. Coupon details: SHOP7 = 7% welcome/switch discount for first direct shop order after switching from eBay or another platform. One-time use only. DANKE5 = 5% discount for the next direct shop order / follow-up order.

Digital Marketing & Executive Support Co.t: Digital Marketing & Executive Support Coordinator Remote
Digital Marketing & Executive Support Co.d: Role Overview The client is seeking a highly organized and proactive Digital Marketing & Executive Support Coordinator to provide direct support to the Managing Director across website management, product content updates, SharePoint administration, marketing collateral, social media, digital organization, and general business administration. This role serves as a key behind-the-scenes support function, ensuring that business operations, product information, marketing materials, and digital platforms remain accurate, current, and professionally maintained. The successful candidate will work closely with the Managing Director and internal travel team. The ideal person will be technically capable, detail-oriented, self-motivated, and comfortable managing a diverse range of digital, marketing, and administrative responsibilities. This multifaceted role combines WordPress website management, Flatsome/UX Builder content updates, SharePoint administration, supplier and product updates, marketing collateral creation, social media support, digital organization, and general business administration. Key Responsibilities 1. Executive Support Provide day-to-day administrative and operational support to the Managing Director. Assist with business projects, marketing initiatives, supplier communications, and internal administration. Prepare, format, and update documents, spreadsheets, reports, and business resources. Maintain action lists, monitor outstanding tasks, and help ensure projects progress to completion. Assist with email drafting, document formatting, file management, and general coordination 2. Website & WordPress Management Update and maintain company websites using WordPress. Manage website content using Flatsome and UX Builder. Create, edit, and maintain resort pages, holiday package pages, wedding pages, landing pages, and general website content. Upload and format images, PDFs, flyers, and promotional materials. Perform basic website modifications using HTML and CSS. Review website functionality, including links, formatting, page layouts, and mobile responsiveness. Implement basic SEO updates, including: Page titles Meta descriptions Image alt text Internal linking Identify outdated content, broken links, formatting issues, and missing information, escalating concerns to the Managing Director. Ensure all website content remains accurate, current, and aligned with brand standards. 3. SharePoint Intranet & Product Information Management Update and maintain product information within Microsoft SharePoint. Upload, organize, and manage supplier updates, resort information, rate sheets, product flyers, and training materials. Ensure internal product information remains current and accessible for the travel consultant team. Create and update product summaries, supplier notes, and reference documentation. Maintain consistency between website content, SharePoint resources, and marketing collateral. Ensure product information is clearly organized and easily accessible. Support version control and digital file management across SharePoint and OneDrive. 4. Supplier & Product Administration Review supplier and resort updates and convert them into clear website, intranet, and marketing collateral updates. Maintain accurate product information across both internal and external platforms. Update offers, resort inclusions, package details, supplier notes, and product documentation. Support the Managing Director with supplier-related administration and communications. Help ensure the travel team has access to accurate and up-to-date product information. 5. Marketing Collateral Development Create, update, and maintain marketing materials, including: Wedding flyers Resort flyers Holiday package flyers Agent flyers Client-facing documents Product update sheets Promotional PDFs Internal reference materials Additional responsibilities include: Updating existing templates and creating new collateral as required. Preparing materials for digital distribution, email campaigns, website uploads, and print production. Ensuring all collateral is accurate, visually professional, and aligned with company branding. Assisting with image resizing, PDF preparation, and document presentation. 6. Social Media Support Assist with creating, scheduling, and publishing content across social media platforms. Develop basic graphics, captions, and promotional posts. Support campaigns focused on holidays, resorts, weddings, honeymoons, and special offers. Maintain a consistent brand presence across: Facebook Instagram LinkedIn Other relevant platforms Monitor basic engagement and escalate enquiries to the appropriate team members. Repurpose website and supplier content for social media use. 7. Administration & Digital Organization Maintain organized digital files across Microsoft 365, SharePoint, and OneDrive. Assist with supplier information management, product records, marketing databases, and business documentation. Support ad hoc business, marketing, and administrative projects. Improve internal templates, filing systems, and administrative processes where appropriate. Ensure documents are properly named, filed, and easy to retrieve. Perform general administrative duties as requested by the Managing Director. Essential Skills & Experience Candidates must possess: Strong WordPress experience. Experience with Flatsome / UX Builder, or extensive experience with WordPress page builders and the ability to learn quickly. Basic HTML and CSS skills. Strong Microsoft Office skills, particularly Excel. Proficiency in: Outlook Word Teams SharePoint OneDrive Experience using Amadeus GDS. Experience managing website content, digital assets, and marketing materials. Strong document formatting and presentation skills. Excellent attention to detail. Strong written English communication skills. Ability to follow instructions accurately. Ability to work independently and effectively manage priorities. Strong organizational and time-management skills. Ability to manage multiple projects, tasks, and deadlines simultaneously. Confidence working across multiple brands, platforms, and business functions. Highly Desirable Skills Experience with Adobe Creative Suite, including: InDesign Photoshop Illustrator Acrobat Canva experience. Experience creating: Flyers Brochures Promotional PDFs Client-facing documents Social media content creation experience. Basic SEO knowledge. Image resizing and optimization experience. Experience maintaining a SharePoint intranet. Experience building WordPress landing pages and online forms. Experience within the travel, tourism, wedding, or hospitality industries. Previous experience supporting a business owner, Managing Director, or senior executive. Key Systems & Platforms The role may involve regular use of: WordPress Flatsome / UX Builder Amadeus GDS Microsoft SharePoint Microsoft 365 Excel Outlook Teams OneDrive Adobe Creative Suite Canva Facebook Instagram LinkedIn PDF editing tools Website image and file management tools Personal Attributes The ideal candidate will be: Highly organized Detail-oriented and accurate Proactive and solution-focused Reliable and responsive Technically capable and confident with digital platforms Creative yet practical Able to follow instructions carefully Comfortable asking questions when clarification is required Effective at managing multiple priorities Capable of working independently once trained Professional, discreet, and trustworthy Comfortable taking ownership of recurring responsibilities Able to identify issues and recommend practical improvements Adaptable within a small business environment where priorities may change Role Priorities The primary priorities of this role are: Providing reliable executive support to the Managing Director. Maintaining accurate, current, and professionally presented websites. Keeping SharePoint and internal product information up to date. Producing and maintaining professional marketing collateral. Supporting social media and broader marketing activities. Maintaining organized business documentation and digital resources. Ensuring projects and tasks are progressed through to completion. 15 hours/week

Ongoing Digital Marketing, Social Media,.t: Ongoing Digital Marketing, Social Media, Google & PR Manager for Luxury Golf Lodge in Australia
Ongoing Digital Marketing, Social Media,.i: Hourly: $5.00 - $10.00 Entry Level Est. time: More than 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
Ongoing Digital Marketing, Social Media,.d: # Ongoing Digital Marketing, Social Media, Google & PR Manager for Luxury Golf Lodge in Australia We are looking for an experienced digital marketing and social media professional or small agency to take ownership of the marketing strategy and execution for King Island Lodge, a luxury accommodation and golf lodge located on King Island, Tasmania, Australia. This is not a small “post a few times on Instagram” job. We are looking for someone who can take a comprehensive view of our marketing, rebuild the foundations properly, and then manage the ongoing execution. Ideally, we would like to work with someone based in Vietnam or with a strong Vietnamese marketing team, as we believe there is excellent digital marketing and social media talent in that region. However, we are open to outstanding applicants from elsewhere if the fit is right. ## About King Island Lodge King Island Lodge is a premium accommodation business servicing visitors to King Island, particularly golf groups, corporate groups, food and wine travellers, and people looking for a high-end Tasmanian island experience. King Island is internationally known for its golf, produce, rugged coastline, seafood, beef, cheese, and unique remote-island feel. Our lodge is positioned as a premium, boutique destination for groups looking for a memorable stay. We need our online presence to reflect that properly. ## What We Need We are looking for someone who can take over the full digital marketing function and build a serious, structured strategy. This includes: ## 1. Google Account Setup & Optimisation We need help reviewing, fixing, setting up, and optimising our Google ecosystem, including: - Google Business Profile - Google Ads account structure - Google Analytics - Google Search Console - Google Tag Manager - Conversion tracking - Google reviews strategy - Local SEO - Search visibility for King Island accommodation, golf trips, luxury lodge stays, group accommodation, corporate retreats, and related searches Our current Google presence is not where it needs to be. We want someone who can audit it, clean it up, and create a proper structure. ## 2. Meta & Social Media Management We need ongoing management of our social media presence, particularly: - Instagram - Facebook - Meta Business Suite - Paid Meta advertising - Retargeting campaigns - Campaign strategy - Audience testing - Creative direction - Copywriting - Content scheduling - Reporting We have strong visual material and can create more, including AI-assisted images, video, property imagery, golf-related content, and King Island destination content. However, we need someone who can turn this into a consistent marketing machine. We are not looking for cheesy or generic social media posts. The tone needs to feel premium, understated, authentic, and commercially effective. ## 3. Broader Platform Strategy We would like advice and management across any other platforms that may help generate bookings and awareness, such as: - YouTube - TikTok - LinkedIn - Pinterest - TripAdvisor - Tourism platforms - Travel publications - Golf travel platforms - Email marketing platforms - CRM or lead capture systems We do not want to be on every platform just for the sake of it. We want a clear strategy that identifies where the opportunity actually is. ## 4. PR & Organic Media Coverage A major part of this role is helping us generate organic publicity and media coverage. We are interested in being featured in: - Newspapers - Travel magazines - Golf publications - Food and wine publications - Tasmanian tourism media - Australian travel media - Luxury accommodation guides - Corporate retreat and event publications - Podcasts or interviews where relevant We need someone who understands how to create angles, write press releases or pitches, identify journalists/publications, and follow up properly. Possible story angles include: - King Island as a world-class golf destination - Luxury group accommodation on King Island - Golf, food, wine, seafood and remote island travel - Corporate retreats in unique Australian locations - The rise of boutique regional luxury tourism - King Island Lodge as a base for golf groups playing Cape Wickham and Ocean Dunes ## 5. Strategy First, Then Execution We are looking for someone who can start by creating a proper marketing plan. The initial phase should include: - Audit of our current online presence - Review of Google, Meta, website, SEO and content - Competitor research - Identification of target markets - Campaign recommendations - PR opportunities - Advertising structure - Content pillars - Reporting framework - Budget recommendations - 30-day, 90-day and 6-month action plan Once the strategy is agreed, we would like the right person or team to continue managing the execution on an ongoing basis. ## Our Target Customers Our target market includes: - Golf groups travelling to King Island - Groups playing Cape Wickham and Ocean Dunes - Corporate groups and executive retreats - High-end leisure travellers - Food, wine and nature travellers - Australian domestic travellers - Potential international golf travellers - Groups from Melbourne, Sydney, Adelaide, Brisbane and Tasmania - Travel agents, golf tour operators and corporate event organisers ## Skills Required The ideal applicant will have experience with: - Google Business Profile optimisation - Google Ads - Meta Ads - Facebook and Instagram management - SEO - Content strategy - Copywriting - PR outreach - Tourism, accommodation, hospitality or luxury brand marketing - Lead generation - Conversion tracking - Analytics and reporting - Email marketing - Campaign planning - Brand positioning - Working independently and taking ownership Experience in travel, accommodation, tourism, golf, luxury hospitality or destination marketing would be highly regarded. ## What We Are Looking For We want someone who is: - Strategic, not just task-based - Commercially minded - Very organised - Able to communicate clearly - Strong with written English - Comfortable making recommendations - Able to manage both strategy and execution - Honest about what will and will not work - Capable of building systems, not just creating individual posts - Experienced enough to take ownership rather than waiting for instructions on every small task ## Tone & Brand Direction The King Island Lodge brand should feel: - Premium - Authentic - Relaxed - Sophisticated - Warm - Golf-focused without being too narrow - Tasmanian / island / coastal - High-end but not pretentious - Natural, not corporate - Compelling without being cheesy The content should avoid generic hospitality clichés. We want marketing that feels intelligent, understated and effective. ## Initial Deliverables For the first stage, we would like you to provide: 1. A full audit of our current digital presence 2. A recommended marketing strategy 3. A Google setup and optimisation plan 4. A Meta and social media plan 5. A PR and media outreach plan 6. A content calendar framework 7. Paid advertising recommendations 8. Reporting structure and KPIs 9. Suggested monthly management plan After that, we would like to move into ongoing monthly management. ## Ongoing Work This is intended to be an ongoing role, not a one-off project. Ongoing responsibilities may include: - Managing social media accounts - Creating and scheduling posts - Managing paid campaigns - Improving Google visibility - Monitoring performance - Writing content - Coordinating PR outreach - Finding partnership opportunities - Reporting monthly results - Recommending improvements - Helping build King Island Lodge into a recognised premium accommodation brand ## Application Requirements When applying, please include: 1. A brief overview of your experience 2. Examples of similar businesses you have worked with 3. Experience with Google Business Profile, Google Ads and Meta Ads 4. Experience with PR or media outreach 5. Your suggested approach for the first 30 days 6. Whether you are an individual freelancer or an agency/team 7. Your location and working hours 8. Your monthly management fee range 9. Any examples of tourism, accommodation, hospitality, golf, luxury or destination marketing work Please do not send a generic application. We are looking for someone who has read this brief and can explain how they would approach the project. ## Budget We are open to discussing the right structure. Initially, we expect a strategy/audit phase, followed by ongoing monthly management. Please provide your recommendation for how you would price this. We are happy to pay properly for the right person or team, but we want someone who is practical, commercial and capable of delivering measurable outcomes. ## Final Note We are not looking for someone to simply make nice-looking posts. We are looking for a serious marketing partner who can help us build the digital presence, advertising structure, PR exposure and booking pipeline for King Island Lodge over the long term.

What You'll Be Editing Weddings and large-scale events (galas, graduations, corporate events) Event highlight reels for schools and nonprofits Vlogs, behind-the-scenes, and travel content Cinematic short films and storytelling pieces Pay Structure Pay is per project. Rates scale with project complexity — the base rates below are the floor, not the ceiling. Weddings, travel projects, and large-scale productions pay significantly more. Base Editing Rates (starting at): $75 per finished minute of edited content $15 per hour of footage sifted and organized What that looks like in practice: A 3-minute event highlight reel = $225+ editing pay A 5-minute wedding highlight film = $375+ (more for complex multi-camera, long-form projects) Travel and destination content = negotiated per project, always above base rate Consistent work from day one — this is not a "wait for jobs to come in" situation Growth path: Months 1-3: Per-project basis as you learn the style and workflow Months 4-6: Increased volume and project complexity Month 6+: Full salaried position at $3,000-$4,000/month based on performance What We're Looking For Required: Proficiency in DaVinci Resolve (non-negotiable — we do not use Premiere Pro) Strong shot selection — you understand why one clip works and another doesn't Basic to intermediate color grading — clean, consistent, cinematic Sound design at a working level (7/10 minimum) — dialogue clarity, music balance, ambient fill Basic motion graphics — lower thirds, text overlays, simple transitions Ability to take direction and match an established style Reliable turnaround — deadlines are not suggestions Preferred: Experience editing weddings, events, or travel content Familiarity with cinematic editing styles beyond quick social cuts Experience working with LOG footage and color science Most Important: You love editing. Not as a job — as a craft. You watch great content and study why it works. You have opinions about shot selection. You want to get better and you're willing to be coached to get there. What This Is NOT This is not a passive gig where you edit however you want This is not for editors who want to impose their own style over the project This is not for editors who can't take feedback or direction This is not for editors who disappear after taking a project How to Apply We receive a lot of applications. To make sure we're connecting with people who are serious, here's what we need from you: Step 1 — Record a short Loom video (2-3 minutes max) Go to loom.com, record yourself on camera, and answer these three questions: Who are you, what kind of content do you love editing, and why do you want this role specifically? Walk us through one edit you're proud of — show us the finished product and tell us what decisions you made and why. What's your current skill level in DaVinci Resolve and what are you actively working to get better at? Don't overthink it. Don't script it. Just talk to us like a real person. Step 2 — Submit the following through Upwork: Your Loom video link A link to your reel or 2-3 work samples Your typical turnaround time for a 3-minute edit Subject line: EDITOR APPLICATION — [Your Name] Applications without a Loom video will not be considered. This is the filter. If you can't take 3 minutes to show us who you are, we're not the right fit for each other. Shortlisted candidates will receive a paid test edit — raw footage and a style reference provided. We pay for your time even on the test. Mixson Media Inc — Brooklyn, NY | Remote editing role | Long-term opportunity

Youtube thumbnails for AI-drama series a.d: Title: Thumbnail design — premium cinematic documentary series (set of 6 + style system) I'm launching a cinematic documentary YouTube series about the history of the AI industry (the OpenAI story — Sam Altman, Elon Musk, the boardroom drama). The visual register is prestige documentary / film poster, NOT typical YouTube. Restrained, cinematic, intelligent. Think New Yorker cover or a Netflix limited-series key art — premium, moody, composed. Avoid: giant glowing text, red circles/arrows, shocked faces, neon, clutter. What I need: A reusable style system for the series — a consistent visual identity (palette, type, layout grid, treatment) that makes every episode instantly recognizable as the same brand, with room to vary per episode. This is the most important deliverable. The first 2 episode thumbnails designed to that system (with the option to continue for later episodes as the series rolls out). Brand constants (must appear / hold across all): A single-word wordmark, "SYNTHETIC," used as the series signature (I can provide the logotype, or you can propose one — see the matching banner brief). Palette: cold, near-black background (#0a0a0f range), off-white text, a single cold-indigo accent (#3b5bdb). Cinematic color grading. Imagery: stylized cinematic recreations of real scenes/people (I provide the source frames from my production) — treated to look like film stills, NOT photos, NOT cartoons, NOT obvious AI. Restrained typography: at most a few words. Legible at small (mobile feed) size. Episode 1 specifics: subject is the 2015 founding / the Musk–Altman relationship. Concept direction: the two figures in tension (recreation stills provided), cinematic and composed, with the SYNTHETIC wordmark and a small restrained current-news cue (a subtle gavel/courtroom motif — the story ties to a recent verdict). Working titles I'm testing: "The Dinner Where Elon Musk and Sam Altman Built OpenAI" / "Elon Musk Just Lost to Sam Altman. This Is How It Started." Deliverables: layered source files (PSD/Figma), 1280×720, plus the style-system doc/template so I can brief you (or maintain it myself) for future episodes. Designed to read at thumbnail scale — show me a feed-size mockup. Note: I'll likely commission episodes in batches as the series proves out, so I value a designer who wants an ongoing relationship and builds me a system, not just one-off art. Also need (same visual system): the SYNTHETIC channel wordmark/logotype, and YouTube channel banner art (2560×1440, with the safe/visible central zone designed for — text and key elements must survive the mobile/TV crop). Banner should establish the series as a premium media brand on first visit: the SYNTHETIC wordmark, a tagline ("the human drama of the AI era" — or propose), cinematic and restrained, same palette and grade as the thumbnails. I want the banner, wordmark, and thumbnail system to be one coherent identity, designed together. Wordmark direction: single-word logotype, geometric sans-serif, cinematic, dignified — "Kubrick title cards meets BBC documentary." Some examples of videos for reference. replicate the cinematic-restrained mood and composition of #1–3, explicitly not the cartoon/illustrated look (#4) or the loud-title energy (#6) — and every Synthetic thumbnail carries the SYNTHETIC wordmark, cold near-black/indigo palette, and recreated-scene imagery (never stock/cartoon/webcam) More Perfect Union — "What Sam Altman Doesn't Want You To Know" (4.2M) — https://youtu.be/l0K4XPu3Qhg — the closest register comp: cinematic, restrained, exposé-clean. Bloomberg Originals — "Inside OpenAI's Stargate Megafactory" (6.4M) — https://youtu.be/GhIJs4zbH0o — premium legacy-media cinematic; the dignified end of the spectrum. BBC World Service — "AI2027: Is this how AI destroys humanity?" (11.3M) — https://youtu.be/1UufaK3pQMg — doom-neighbourhood, but restrained and broadcast-grade, not cartoon-loud. Kurzgesagt — "A.I. — Humanity's Final Invention?" (11.9M) — https://youtu.be/fa8k8IQ1_X0 — include as the "we are NOT this" reference — illustrated/animated, the opposite of recreation. Useful contrast for the brief. MagnatesMedia — "The INSANE Truth About OpenAI" (1.15M) — https://youtu.be/dsDcQmJHwQc — story-documentary channel; figure + cinematic treatment, single subject focus. Moon — "OpenAI is Completely F*cked" (645K) — https://youtu.be/wJo1PV9ErM4 — darker video-essay grade; good for the moody-tone reference

The brand is inspired by the idea that life should be filled with meaningful experiences, challenges, and "side quests" rather than simply existing on autopilot. The aesthetic leans toward modern streetwear with strong identity and subtle storytelling rather than loud, generic motivational slogans. ## What I'm Looking For I'm seeking a designer who: * Has experience with streetwear or apparel design * Can contribute ideas rather than simply follow instructions * Understands branding and visual identity * Can create designs that feel premium and wearable * Can work collaboratively and provide creative input * Is comfortable designing for T-shirts, hoodies, and other apparel ## Initial Scope * 5 original apparel designs * Front, back, or combined designs as appropriate * Print-ready files * Source files included * Designs consistent with the Auderion brand identity ## To Apply Please include: 1. Examples of your previous apparel or streetwear work 2. A brief explanation of your design style 3. Any brands or projects you've worked on that are similar 4. Your pricing structure for apparel design work 5. Why you think you'd be a good fit for this project Applications without relevant portfolio examples will not be considered. I'm looking for quality over quantity and would like to build a long-term relationship with the right designer as the brand grows. ## Current Direction & Inspiration I have already created several logos, concepts, and early designs for Auderion. These are not necessarily final products, but they represent the direction, style, and feeling I want the brand to have. Examples will be provided to shortlisted applicants. I am looking for a designer who can: * Build upon these ideas * Improve and refine them where needed * Bring fresh ideas that still fit the brand identity * Help create a consistent visual language across future releases I am not looking for someone to simply recreate my existing designs. I want a designer who understands the vision and can contribute creatively while remaining aligned with the brand.

Texas Boiler Expert/Mechanical Engineer.d: We are looking for a qualified boiler specialist, facilities design engineer, or mechanical engineer with Texas boiler code / TDI experience to review the compliance path for a packaged boiler system prior to manufacturing release. The boiler is approximately 2.1 MW input, and our current understanding is that it is below the typical NFPA 85 applicability threshold. We need a specialist to confirm whether ASME CSD-1, along with EN 676 burner compliance, ASME S-stamp, National Board registration, and proper inspection documentation, is an acceptable path for obtaining a Texas TDI Certificate of Operation. Scope of Work Please review and provide a written opinion covering: Whether the boiler is below the Texas TDI / NFPA 85 jurisdictional threshold. Whether the threshold is evaluated per boiler unit or based on aggregate site boiler capacity. Whether ASME CSD-1 is acceptable in lieu of NFPA 85 for this boiler size and application. Whether the proposed documentation package is sufficient for TDI Certificate of Operation approval, including: Relief valve certificates Manufacturer’s Data Report / NB Form P National Board registration Hydrostatic test certificate Safety control test records O&M manual Installation drawings Low-water cutoff testing Feedwater pump / sizing documentation Whether two independent low-water cutoffs are required. Whether burner management requirements are properly covered under CSD-1, including flame supervision, purge sequencing, double block and bleed fuel shutoff valves, and proof-of-closure testing. Whether the burner certification needs to account for non-pipeline gas composition, including Wobbe index, H2S, and moisture after scrubbing. Whether burner emissions data should be provided for air permitting coordination. Whether a China-fabricated pressure vessel can be accepted through the ASME S-stamp / National Board / Authorized Inspector pathway, and whether any shop or hydro testing must be witnessed for Texas acceptance. Deliverable A concise written technical memo or email summary confirming: Applicable standards Any compliance gaps Required documentation Recommended next steps before manufacturing release Required Qualifications Boiler code, packaged boiler, or facilities mechanical design experience Familiarity with ASME CSD-1, ASME BPVC, National Board registration, and boiler inspection documentation Experience with Texas Department of Insurance boiler requirements preferred Experience with burner management systems and fuel gas-fired boiler packages preferred Please include in your proposal: Relevant boiler / code compliance experience Any Texas TDI experience Whether you have reviewed similar packaged boiler compliance packages Estimated hours to complete the review Availability to start

Logo Design for Illinois JCAR.i: Hourly: $15.00 - $35.00 Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, Less than 30
Logo Design for Illinois JCAR.d: Create a sophisticated and modern logo for the Illinois Joint Committee on Administrative Rules (JCAR) that reflects bipartisan legislative oversight, governmental authority, balance, and procedural integrity. The design should symbolize the committee’s unique structure of 12 legislators evenly divided between the two major political parties—6 Democrats and 6 Republicans—with balanced representation from each of the four legislative caucuses (3 members from each caucus). Incorporate visual elements that subtly convey symmetry, collaboration, checks and balances, and the rulemaking process, such as mirrored shapes, divided pillars, interlocking forms, a circular arrangement of twelve elements, or balanced architectural motifs inspired by the Illinois State Capitol. Use a refined and professional color palette featuring deep navy, silver, white, and restrained bipartisan accents of muted blue and red. Typography should feel official, timeless, and legislative—clean serif or modern sans-serif fonts that project credibility and stability. The overall aesthetic should be polished, institutional, and distinctly Illinois-focused while remaining modern enough for digital and print use.

Premium AI Company Website Design & Deve.t: Premium AI Company Website Design & Development Project
Premium AI Company Website Design & Deve.i: Hourly Expert Est. time: 1 to 3 months, Not sure
Premium AI Company Website Design & Deve.d: Premium AI Company Website Design & Development Project Next.js + Headless CMS + Advanced Motion UI/UX: We are currently looking for experienced freelancers or agencies to submit proposals and price offers for the complete design and development of a premium AI company website. This is a project-based outsourcing request with a clearly defined final deliverable. We are NOT looking to hire employees or long-term in-house staff at this stage. We are looking for: - complete project delivery, - technical proposals, - design concepts, - project timelines, - and competitive price-quality offers. The final decision will be based on the overall quality-to-price ratio, portfolio quality, technical expertise, visual design capabilities, and experience with premium modern websites. Reference/inspiration websites can be shared privately upon request. Project Overview We require the design and development of a modern, visually impressive, premium AI company website for a Germany-based Artificial Intelligence company specialized in: Computer Vision Camera Intelligence AI Agents Enterprise AI Solutions GDPR-compliant AI Systems On-Premise AI Infrastructure The website should have a premium “luxury tech company” appearance with advanced animations, interactive visual effects, cinematic transitions, and high-end UI/UX. This is NOT a simple corporate website. We are looking for a highly modern and visually interactive web experience comparable to premium international AI/SaaS companies. Preferred Technology Stack Preferred technologies include: Next.js (React) Modern Headless CMS (Strapi, Sanity, Payload CMS, Directus, Contentful, or equivalent) Tailwind CSS or equivalent Framer Motion / GSAP or similar animation libraries The final platform should support: multilingual architecture (EN/DE), self-hosting, SEO optimization, CDN-ready media delivery, scalable architecture, and production-ready clean code. We are open to suggestions regarding the most suitable self-hosted CMS architecture. Design & UX Expectations We are looking for a highly visual, modern, interactive, and premium design language. The website should include advanced motion effects such as: zoom in/out scroll effects, smooth scrolling, cinematic transitions, animated product sections, scroll-triggered animations, mouse interaction effects, expanding cards, animated counters/statistics, interactive content sections, dynamic layouts, premium hover effects, visual storytelling, animated logo banners, and modern luxury-style UI components. The final result should feel: futuristic, premium, enterprise-grade, visually impressive, innovative, interactive, and technologically advanced. We strongly prefer: less text, more visuals, more animations, more interaction, and modern premium UI components instead of generic templates. Website Structure Homepage The homepage should act as a visual summary of the entire company and all subpages. Required homepage sections include: cinematic hero carousel/banner, company trailer section, AI/technology highlights, animated statistics section, solutions overview, about us summary, awards section, strategic partners section, trusted customer logos, FAQ section, recent funded projects section, recent company news/blog section, newsletter subscription section, CTA sections, and premium footer. The homepage should heavily focus on visual presentation and motion design. Solutions / Product Pages We currently have four major solution categories. 1. Retail AI Solution Features include: age verification, product recognition, shelf compliance, inventory intelligence, loss prevention. 2. Automotive AI Solution Features include: vehicle damage detection, fleet inspection, leasing analysis, insurance support, vehicle condition assessment. 3. Meeting AI Solution Features include: audio transcription, meeting protocol generation, AI-generated summaries, public administration workflows. 4. Custom AI Solutions Custom enterprise AI development including: individual AI systems, GDPR-compliant AI, on-premise deployment, API-driven architecture, hardware/software-independent AI solutions, made-in-Germany enterprise AI solutions. Each solution page should: have a unique premium layout, include interactive and animated sections, contain animated statistics, include logo/reference banners, include CTA sections, use visual placeholders, and avoid generic SaaS-style layouts. We especially want product pages to feel visually impressive and interactive. Funded Projects Section A dedicated funded projects page should include: active projects, completed projects, filtering/categories, partner logos, funding agency logos, project summaries, timelines, detailed project pages, and external links. Filtering categories may include: EU, Erasmus+, BMBF, DFG, national projects, and other funding programs. Additional Required Pages The website should include: About Us, Contact, Career, Blog / News, Solutions Overview, Projects Overview. No team page is required. Navigation & Hamburger Menu The hamburger menu is an important design component of this project. Requirements include: large premium animated hamburger menu, left-aligned navigation, split-layout navigation concept, animated submenu behavior, EN/DE language switch, social media integration, company contact information inside menu, smooth transitions and interactions, modern premium visual style. We prefer the menu to feel modern, visually impressive, and interactive rather than minimal/basic. Content & Media Availability Most textual content already exists in draft form and will be provided during the project. We can provide: customer logos, partner logos, award logos, funding agency logos, company branding assets, product information, and project descriptions. However, we expect the freelancer/agency to: create a highly visual presentation concept, suggest visual storytelling approaches, use modern placeholders where required, integrate premium stock materials where suitable, and recommend additional visual improvements. We are open to: AI-generated visuals, motion graphics, premium stock assets, and custom animations. Please specify clearly: which assets you expect from us, and which visual/design elements are included in your proposal. Career Page Requirements At this stage, the career page can initially be implemented as: a modern application/contact form, without mandatory ATS integration. Optional ATS integrations such as: Personio, Recruitee, or similar systems may be proposed separately as optional extensions. Contact & Lead Generation The contact page should include: contact form, Google Maps integration, company contact information, and optional booking/calendar integrations. Optional integrations such as: Calendly, HubSpot, Pipedrive, CRM systems, or newsletter systems can be proposed separately. CMS Requirements The website should be fully manageable through the selected CMS, including: pages, projects, blog/news, FAQ, awards, logos, statistics, CTA sections, newsletter sections, and solution content. SEO & Performance Requirements The final website should include: technical SEO optimization, fast loading performance, schema markup, Open Graph support, multilingual SEO, image optimization, lazy loading, and optimized frontend performance. Browser, Device & Accessibility Support The website should support: modern desktop browsers, tablets, and mobile devices. Please specify: browser compatibility, responsive testing approach, and accessibility considerations. Experience with: WCAG accessibility standards, and German/EU compliance requirements is considered a plus. Infrastructure & Deployment We already operate: our own hosting infrastructure, and our own CDN infrastructure. The final project should therefore support: self-hosting, external CDN integration, optimized media delivery, and production-ready deployment. Please specify: preferred deployment architecture, Docker/container support, CI/CD recommendations, and server requirements. Legal & Compliance Requirements The website must support German/EU legal requirements, including: Impressum, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy, Cookie Consent Banner, GDPR-compliant integrations. As GDPR compliance is one of our company’s core values, privacy-friendly architecture is highly preferred. Project Workflow & Milestones We expect a structured project workflow with milestones. Preferred workflow: Wireframes / Concept Phase UI/UX Design Approval Frontend Development CMS Integration Staging Deployment Feedback & Revision Phase Final Delivery / Go-Live Please specify: estimated project timeline, milestone structure, included revision rounds, and how scope changes are handled. Expected Deliverables Expected deliverables include: complete UI/UX design, fully developed frontend, CMS integration, responsive implementation, animation implementation, SEO setup, deployment support, documentation, and production-ready codebase. What We Expect From You Please include in your proposal: complete project price offer, estimated timeline, technical proposal, portfolio/references, similar projects, recommended technology stack, CMS recommendation, optional maintenance/support packages, and revision policy. Preferred Experience Preference will be given to freelancers/agencies experienced with: AI company websites, SaaS platforms, motion design, premium enterprise UI/UX, Next.js ecosystems, Headless CMS systems, Framer Motion / GSAP, and interactive web experiences. Bonus skills: Three.js, WebGL, interactive storytelling, luxury tech branding. Important We are NOT looking for: generic templates, basic corporate websites, standard SaaS themes, or minimal-animation websites. We ARE looking for: premium visual storytelling, advanced interactions, luxury AI-tech branding, cinematic web experiences, enterprise-level presentation quality, and modern high-end UI/UX. The final result should feel comparable to premium international AI technology companies.

Canva Designer Needed to Create Branded.t: Canva Designer Needed to Create Branded Client-Facing Medicare Journey PDFs
Canva Designer Needed to Create Branded.d: I’m looking for a talented Canva designer/content creator to help build a set of polished, client-facing PDF templates for my insurance agency. We are Sileo Insurance Group, a Medicare-focused insurance agency in the U.S. We are building a branded client experience called the SIG Medicare Confidence & Support System. The goal is to help adults turning 65 feel clear, organized, supported, and confident as they move through the Medicare process. I already have the copy and strategy prepared. I need someone who can turn the copy into beautiful, professional, easy-to-read Canva documents. These documents will be used as “client receipts” or milestone summaries throughout the client journey. They should feel clean, premium, trustworthy, and easy for older adults to understand. What You’ll Create You will design a set of Canva templates for client-facing PDFs, including: Your Medicare Confidence Review Is Reserved Your Medicare Confidence Roadmap Your Medicare Starting Point Snapshot Medicare Countdown & Readiness Receipt Whole-Life Medicare Fit Profile Receipt Strategy-Before-Plan Receipt Umbrella Protection Recommendation Receipt Smooth-Start Enrollment Receipt Client Success Handoff Confirmation First 100 Days Medicare Setup Checklist SIG Total Care Support Guide Family Peace of Mind Summary Final Medicare Confidence Summary Some documents will be 1 page. A few may be 2–4 pages. I will provide the copy for each one. Design Style The style should be: Clean and professional Trustworthy and calm Easy to read for adults age 60+ Branded consistently Not cluttered Not overly flashy Strong use of white space Clear headings, checkboxes, icons, and simple layouts Designed in Canva so my team can edit later I already have one sample PDF completed that you can use as a style reference. Skills Needed Please apply if you are strong with: Canva document design PDF layouts Branded templates Client-facing guides/checklists Healthcare, insurance, financial, or professional services design Clean typography Layout consistency Turning long copy into simple visual pages Bonus if you have experience designing materials for U.S. healthcare, Medicare, insurance, finance, or senior-focused audiences. What I Will Provide I will provide: Brand logo Brand colors Existing sample PDF Copy for each receipt Document purpose and notes Feedback after each draft What I Need From You Please provide: Canva editable links PDF exports Consistent design system across all templates Icons or visual elements where helpful Clean layout that my team can reuse Fast communication and willingness to revise Project Workflow First, I would like to start with a paid test project: Test Project: Design 1–2 receipt templates based on copy and brand direction. If the test goes well, I will hire you to complete the full set. Budget Please quote either: A fixed price for the full template set, or Your hourly rate and estimated number of hours My expected budget for the full set is approximately $200, maybe higher depending on quality, experience, and how many revisions are included. To Apply Please include: A short note about your Canva design experience 3–5 samples of professional PDF, brochure, guide, checklist, or presentation designs Your estimated price for the full set Your estimated turnaround time Confirmation that you can provide editable Canva templates

Full Bilingual Website Redesign for Insu.t: Full Bilingual Website Redesign for Insurance Agency — WordPress + Elementor Pro (4–6 weeks)
Full Bilingual Website Redesign for Insu.d: About the project: Omega Insurance Agency LLC, an independent insurance agency with 25+ years in South Carolina, USA, and 3 physical offices is looking for an experienced freelancer to redesign omegainsurancellc.com. The current site was built using an unofficial Elementor clone (PRO Elements) and broke after a plugin update. This isn't just a repair job. It's a strategic rebuild of the site to serve as the central hub of an integrated digital ecosystem. What we're building: A website where traffic from every channel converges (Google Ads, Meta Ads, organic SEO, Google Business Profile, organic social media, WhatsApp Business) and systematically captures leads into our CRM: Clientify. The site must convert visitors into qualified leads through smart forms, click-to-call, and WhatsApp, with full conversion tracking to optimize paid campaigns and fuel automated email nurture sequences. We're looking for a website that generates measurable leads from day one and scales with the ecosystem we're building. Technical requirements, non-negotiable: - WordPress on Hostinger (already set up). -Elementor Pro official license, NO PRO Elements or any other clone. -Polylang or WPML for full EN/ES bilingualism. -Yoast SEO, WPForms or Fluent Forms with conditional logic. -Wordfence, UpdraftPlus, WP Rocket or LiteSpeed Cache. Scope: 32 unique pages in English, with priority pages bilingual at launch: - 4 main pages: Homepage, About, Contact, Get a Quote - 10 service pages: General Liability, Workers Comp, Commercial Auto, BOP, Professional Liability, Truckers Insurance, Personal Auto, Homeowners, Life Insurance, LLC Formation. - 6 industry pages: Contractors, Truckers, Restaurants, Beauty Salons, Lawn Care, Auto Repair. - 3 location pages: Greenville, Taylors, Laurens, NAP must match Google Business Profile exactly. - 7 dedicated campaign landing pages, no nav menu, single CTA, short form. - Structured blog system organized in content hubs, content production NOT included. - Functional pages: Privacy, Terms, segmented Thank You pages, custom 404. Key integrations: - Clientify CRM: native webhook integration from every form, no Zapier. - Google Analytics 4: custom events, Lead, Contact, WhatsApp Click, Call Click. - Google Tag Manager: Centralized tag management. - Meta Pixel: Standard and custom events. - Microsoft Clarity: Heatmaps and session recordings. - WhatsApp Business: Floating button with context-aware pre-filled messages and GA4 click tracking. - Google Business Profile: Static review badge, no paid plugin required. - Social media: Open Graph and Twitter Cards on all pages, share buttons on blog posts, social profile links in footer. Technical SEO: - Full schema markup: LocalBusiness, InsuranceAgency, FAQ, Article, Service, Review. - Proper hreflang implementation. - Separate URL structure for each language: /en/ and /es/. - Core Web Vitals in the green: LCP under 2.5s, CLS under 0.1, FID under 100ms. - Lighthouse 90+ across all categories, mobile and desktop. Timeline: 4 to 6 weeks: Discovery, Design, Development, Integrations, QA. Payment structure: 40/40/20 via the platform: kickoff, staging delivery, production launch NOT included in V1: Comparative rater tool, client self-service portal, online policy payments, mobile app, blog content production, gated lead magnets. What the client provides: Full technical brief in PDF (shared upon match) page copy in both languages, visual assets: logo, team photos, office photos, Clientify API credentials, access to Hostinger, domain, and Google Workspace. Who we're looking for: A professional with a real portfolio of bilingual websites, ideally with prior experience in insurance, finance, or legal. The following are non-negotiable: - Proven command of official Elementor Pro, not clones. - Real-world experience with Polylang or WPML on live sites. - Solid understanding of bilingual technical SEO: hreflang, schema, URL architecture. - Experience with native CRM integrations, not just Zapier - Clear communication in English and/or Spanish - Availability for weekly check-in calls via Zoom or Google Meet. How to apply. Please include in your proposal: - 3 to 5 portfolio links of real, live sites we can visit and test - Itemized quote: design, development, integrations, QA, post-launch support. - Realistic timeline based on your current availability - 2 client references we can contact - Your technical approach: which exact plugins you'd use and why. - Any question about the technical requirements or project scope. Proposals that only submit a number without addressing the technical specs will be disqualified. Selection within 7 to 10 days. Point of contact: Tomas Nattes — Project Manager / Marketing Lead.

GHL Expert — Full Agency White-Label Bui.t: GHL Expert — Full Agency White-Label Build | 3 Snapshots | Automations | CASL Compliance | Canada
GHL Expert — Full Agency White-Label Bui.d: This is a full GoHighLevel agency system build. Not a template deployment. Not a partial setup. A complete, tested, documented, client-ready system built on the Agency Unlimited plan ($297 USD/month). READ THE FULL SCOPE DISCERPTION BEFORE APPLYING. ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── WHAT IS BEING BUILT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Phase 1 — White-Label Agency Setup Custom domain: i have zovioh branding: logo, colours, favicon across full agency portal Custom SMTP via Google Workspace with SPF and DKIM configured Lead Connector configured for SMS and calls under Zovioh brand or if needed, transferred number to GHL Lead Connector for SMS, calls, or WhatsApp. WhatsApp Business API connected via LC Phone User roles and permissions configured Full credentials checklist sent before work begins — no mid-build requests Phase 2 — Lead Capture Funnels Landing pages for each niche connected to GHL CRM CASL-compliant consent checkbox on every form (unchecked by default, mandatory) Lead form integrations: Facebook Ads, Google Ads, WhatsApp, lending pages, website contact forms All contacts tagged by lead source automatically on entry Phase 3 — 3 Full Pipelines with Stage-Based Auto-Triggers Realtor: New Lead → Contacted → Showing Booked → Offer Made → Closed Won → Closed Lost Mortgage Professional: Inquiry → Pre-Qualification → Application → Processing → Funded → Not Qualified Insurance Advisor: New Inquiry → Consultation Booked → Needs Analysis → Proposal Sent → Policy Issued → Not Interested Opportunity value tracking, lead source visible on every contact record, smart lists per stage Phase 4 — 9 Automation Workflows (full trigger and exit logic in attached document) Instant lead response within 5 minutes — SMS + Email + WhatsApp simultaneously Appointment reminders at 24hr, 3hr, and 1hr — SMS + Email No-show re-booking — fires within 60 minutes of missed appointment Missed call text-back — fires within 60 seconds of missed call Multi-step follow-up sequence — Day 1 SMS / Day 3 Email / Day 7 SMS / Day 14 Email Long-term nurture — monthly email, quarterly SMS for cold/unconverted leads Re-engagement campaign — 3 messages over 14 days for contacts inactive 30+ days Post-transaction review request — fires on Closed Won / Funded / Policy Issued Internal notification and task creation — alerts on new leads, missed appointments, key pipeline stages Phase 5 — 5 Email Sequences (placeholder copy written by developer, reviewed by me before going live) Lead nurture — 5 emails over 14 days Post-call follow-up — 3 emails for calls that do not convert Proposal/application follow-up — Day 1, Day 3, Day 7 Re-engagement — 3 emails for inactive contacts Post-close welcome — welcome + referral ask at 2 weeks Phase 6 — CASL Compliance Across All Snapshots Consent checkbox: unchecked by default, mandatory, explicit language naming email and SMS GHL consent logging: timestamp, IP, form source on every submission Unsubscribe footer on every email (GHL native) STOP opt-out in first SMS of every sequence WhatsApp opt-out in first message Opt-out/STOP immediately halts all sequences and applies correct tags Phase 7 — Integrations Facebook Lead Ads, Google Ads, WhatsApp, website forms → GHL CRM Google Calendar sync Google Business Profile review links in post-close workflows Zapier/n8n as fallback where native integration is unavailable Phase 8 — Full Tag Taxonomy (consistent across all 3 snapshots) Source: source_facebook, source_google, source_whatsapp, source_website, source_organic, source_referral Niche: niche_realtor, niche_mortgage, niche_insurance Status: status_new, status_contacted, status_booked, status_noshow, status_nurture, status_cold, status_closed_won, status_closed_lost Engagement: engaged_email, engaged_sms, engaged_whatsapp, unsubscribed, opted_out_sms, opted_out_whatsapp Phase 9 — Custom Fields on Every Contact Record Lead source, Niche, Assigned agent/advisor, Opportunity value, Last contacted date (auto-updated), Consent given (yes/no), Consent timestamp, Notes Phase 10 — Reporting Dashboards and Smart Lists Pipeline snapshot: total leads, appointments booked, proposals sent, deals closed Lead source breakdown report Conversion rate at each pipeline stage Smart lists: New Leads (7 days), Booked Appointments, No-Shows, Cold Leads, Closed Won, Unsubscribed/Opted Out, Re-engagement Eligible Phase 11 — 3 Fully Deployable Snapshots zovioh — Realtor Snapshot zovioh — Mortgage Snapshot zovioh — Insurance Snapshot Each snapshot is fully self-contained — all pipelines, automations, funnels, calendars, tags, sequences baked in — deployable to a new sub-account in a single action without manual reconfiguration Phase 12 — Mandatory Testing Before Milestone Release Full test log or Loom walkthrough video required covering: test lead through all funnels, all automation timing, STOP/unsubscribe functioning, pipeline stage triggers, snapshot deployment to blank sub-account. Milestone does not release without this. Phase 13 — Handover Full agency admin access transferred to email Written documentation covering all phases Loom walkthrough video (minimum 15 minutes — agency overview + snapshot deployment demo) 30 days post-handover support for bugs and broken integrations at no additional cost ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── IMPORTANT — INCLUDED UNDER FIXED PRICE, NO EXCEPTIONS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── The fixed price agreed covers everything required to make this system fully functional and operational. This includes: Any workflow, automation, or integration that does not function correctly after delivery Any adjustment needed to make a snapshot deploy cleanly to a sub-account Any tag, custom field, or pipeline configuration that does not work as documented Any CASL consent mechanism that is found to be non-compliant with the requirements stated Any email sequence trigger or exit condition that does not fire as specified Any revision to placeholder copy structure (not the words — the sequence logic and timing) Any integration that breaks or fails to sync correctly within the 7-day support window Any configuration change required because a built element does not perform its documented function If something in the build does not work as documented, it is fixed under the same price. If something new is requested beyond the documented scope, it is quoted separately before any work begins on it. ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── DO NOT APPLY IF: ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── You have not built a GHL white-label agency account with a custom domain for a real client You cannot show a live example of a GHL snapshot you have built and deployed You do not know what CASL is or have not built consent-capture for a Canadian client You plan to use unmodified GHL marketplace snapshots and call it a custom build You require full payment upfront You send a proposal without answering all 5 screening questions below You have never configured and transferred number to GHL Lead Connector for SMS, calls, or WhatsApp ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PAYMENT STRUCTURE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Fixed price. Upwork milestone escrow only. Milestone 1 — 50%: White-label setup + Realtor snapshot delivered, tested, and approved Milestone 2 — 50%: All remaining phases delivered, tested, documented, and approved Milestone funds release only after I review and approve delivery. Both milestones require approval before release. ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ANSWER ALL 5 QUESTIONS IN YOUR PROPOSAL — IN ORDER ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── QUESTION 1 — VERIFICATION Have you built a GoHighLevel white-label agency account with a custom domain for a real client? Share the domain or a Loom showing the white-label agency portal. Do not share client data — agency portal only. QUESTION 2 — TECHNICAL TEST (most important — answer in detail) Walk me through exactly how you would build the no-show re-booking workflow in GHL. What is the trigger? What is the wait condition? What action fires and on which channels? What happens if the contact does not respond within 48 hours? Name the specific GHL workflow elements you would use. QUESTION 3 — SNAPSHOT EXPERIENCE Have you built and deployed a GHL snapshot to a client sub-account? What was inside it and how did you test it before deployment? QUESTION 4 — CASL KNOWLEDGE What is CASL and what does it require from a GHL build for a Canadian client? What specific elements in GHL do you configure to ensure CASL compliance on lead capture forms and automated SMS and email sequences? QUESTION 5 — PRICE AND TIMELINE What is your fixed price for this exact scope as documented in the attached file? Provide a phase-by-phase breakdown. What is your confirmed completion date for Milestone 1 and Milestone 2 from the day work begins? ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── WHAT TO INCLUDE IN YOUR PROPOSAL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Answers to all 5 questions numbered in order Fixed price with phase-by-phase breakdown suggestion to make it most efficient workable system based on your experience as it can be long term client work for you Confirmed timeline for each milestone One live example of a completed GHL build — domain, Loom, or screenshot with explanation Confirmation that you have read the full attached scope document Proposals missing any of the above will not receive a response.

What This Role Is Not This is not a community management role. You will not be expected to manage comments, DMs, posting calendars, influencer outreach, customer support, or general social media operations. Ideal Fit You may be a strong fit if you: * Are a U.S.-based woman or deeply understand the U.S. female beauty customer * Have created content for beauty, skincare, nails, cosmetics, haircare, wellness beauty, or adjacent categories * Understand paid social creative, not just pretty organic posts * Can write hooks that sound like real women talking * Know what looks current on TikTok, Reels, Meta, Pinterest, and beauty ecommerce * Can make content that feels casual, warm, current, and product-focused * Can create both images and short-form video assets * Can work from raw UGC and product footage * Can tell when copy sounds awkward, translated, too clinical, or not U.S. beauty-native * Can move quickly without needing perfect conditions Examples of Content Angles For BareRepair™ Nutrient Gels, examples of the kind of content we want to make: * “Press-ons damaged your nails? Don’t go bare.” * “Your nails need a break, but your hands still need to look cute.” * “Need a mani, but not the $100 appointment?” * “Gym. Shower. Pilates. Still polished.” * “The at-home manicure that also helps your nails heal.” * “No glue, no UV lamp, no random pop-off panic.” * “Matching mani + pedi, no appointment.” The tone should feel like a modern American beauty customer talking to another woman, not corporate copy, not translated English, not generic agency language. Weekly Output The exact scope can be discussed, but ideally we want someone who can help produce: * 5 to 10 paid creative concepts or iterations per week * Static ad designs * Short-form video edits or edit directions * Hook and angle lists * Social media content assets * On-screen text and captions * Creative briefs for UGC/influencer content * Fast feedback on what feels current, what feels awkward, and what will not sell What We’ll Provide We can provide: * Raw product photography * Raw influencer content * UGC * Product footage * Brand guidelines * Product details * Customer pain points * Existing landing pages * Paid media direction * A designer/dev team if needed for implementation You do not need to start from nothing. You need to turn the raw material into beauty-native content that can actually sell. Please Include In Your Reply Please send: * Beauty brands or beauty-adjacent brands you’ve created content for * Examples of paid ad creatives, UGC-style content, statics, or short-form videos * Examples of hooks, captions, or on-screen text you’ve written * Your experience with Meta/TikTok ad creatives * Your hourly rate or monthly retainer range * Your weekly availability * A quick take on this angle: “Press-ons damaged your nails? Don’t go bare.” Bonus if your portfolio feels modern, casual, beauty-native, and conversion-focused. Working Style We move fast. Build backend for social finance NFT tra.t: Build backend for social finance NFT trading app
Build backend for social finance NFT tra.d: Type: Long-term contract, multi-phase build. Open only to solo senior devs About the Project Fans.tech is a Web3 creator platform launching on Base L2. Creators launch "clubs" with bonding-curve ClubKeys that fans buy in $FANS (our native ERC-20) to unlock token-gated chats, content, and governance rights. Frontend design and prototype are complete. We need engineering to build the contracts, backend, indexer, and integrations end to end. Full feature spec, contract architecture, and infrastructure choices are documented and will be shared with shortlisted applicants. This post summarizes the scope so you can self-qualify. Scope of Work 1. Smart Contracts (Foundry + OpenZeppelin, Base L2) Ten core contracts to design, implement, test, and deploy: PlatformToken — $FANS ERC-20, supply model TBD ClubFactory — deploys a ClubKey per club, maintains registry, emits ClubCreated ClubKey — bonding-curve buy/sell, supply tracking, $FANS payment, first key free to creator, emits KeyBought / KeySold CurveLibrary — shared fixed-point math for linear (basePrice + slope × n) and exponential (basePrice × multiplier^n) presets FeeDistributor — 1.5% fee per trade split between protocol treasury and farmer rewards GovernanceModule — per-club president voting (vote weight = keys held), moderator assignment GiftKey — keyholder-to-address transfers with Gift event FansSwap — ETH ↔ $FANS via Uniswap V3 SwapRouter wrap or custom constant-product AMM, exposes getQuote LPFarm — ETH/$FANS liquidity pool, LP token mint/burn, share tracking LPRewardDistributor — accumulates 1.5% FANS-ETH swap fees + 1.5% Club key fees + 12M $FANS linear emission over 12 months; MasterChef-style per-second accrual; claimRewards / pendingRewards Hard requirements: fixed-point arithmetic (no floats), reentrancy guards on all value-moving functions, atomic trades (price + transfer + fee in one tx), events on every state change for the indexer, upgradeable proxy pattern with timelock on core contracts, full test coverage before mainnet deploy. 2. Backend Services (Node/TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Redis) Auth Service — JWT + wallet signature (SIWE) Posts & Feed Service — CRUD, scheduling, bookmarks, paginated home feed with All / Posts / Transactions / Trending / Discover tabs Social Platform Integration — OAuth 2.0 connect/disconnect for X, Instagram, OnlyFans (OFAPI or link-based), LinkedIn, Farcaster, TikTok; encrypted token storage; refresh management; per-platform publishing adapters Scheduler Service — cron job polling scheduled_posts, publishing via platform adapters, retry with exponential backoff, BullMQ-backed Notification Service — Firebase Cloud Messaging for messages, key trades, price milestones, governance events, reward claims, swap completions, scheduled post status Analytics & Feed Service — trending algorithm (volume × unique buyers, 24h window), global activity feed from indexer, creator stats endpoint, portfolio endpoint with P&L Chat Service — Socket.io (self-hosted) or Ably, token-gated room access enforced against on-chain key balances Media Pipeline — upload, resize/transcode per platform requirements, store in S3 or Cloudflare R2 3. Indexer The Graph subgraph (or custom event listener via Alchemy/QuickNode) covering all ClubKey, FeeDistributor, Governance, GiftKey, FansSwap, LPFarm, and LPRewardDistributor events. Powers feed, leaderboards, portfolio, and trending. 4. AI Agent Creator Framework (Phased) ERC-8004 Identity Registry + Reputation Registry integration. Three permitted agent models (transparent AI characters, creator-deployed agents, AI-curated index communities) with on-chain identity, reputation scoring, and marketplace badging. V1 ships with Model B only (creator-deployed agents); V2 and V3 add Models A and C. 5. Infrastructure Base mainnet + Sepolia, Alchemy or QuickNode RPC, PostgreSQL, Redis, Firebase Cloud Messaging, Coinbase Pay on-ramp, S3/R2 media, BullMQ job queue. Open Design Questions A senior engineer should expect to weigh in on, not just execute, the following. The spec flags ~20 open decisions including: $FANS supply (fixed vs mintable) and initial allocation, farmer reward recipient (stakers vs keyholders), president voting threshold, moderator storage location, message storage strategy (on-chain history vs off-chain DB), GiftKey fee, supply cap policy, OnlyFans integration approach, swap pool type (Uniswap V3 concentrated vs custom AMM), bridge integration (official Base bridge vs LayerZero), LP reward split exact percentages, and $FANS emission tail policy after 12 months. Strong opinions, backed by experience, are welcome and expected. Required Experience 4+ years shipping production Solidity on an EVM L2 (Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, zkSync). Send verified contract addresses. Foundry, OpenZeppelin, fixed-point math libraries, upgradeable proxies, timelock governance. Demonstrable experience with at least two of: bonding curves, AMMs / Uniswap V3 integration, staking + emission contracts, ERC-20 + ERC-721 design, MasterChef-style reward distribution. Production TypeScript backend at scale — Postgres, Redis, BullMQ or equivalent, OAuth flows, indexer integration. The Graph subgraph development or equivalent custom indexing. Security awareness — reentrancy, oracle manipulation, MEV, proxy storage collisions, integer overflow in curve math. Nice to have: prior social-token or creator-economy product experience, ERC-8004 familiarity, OAuth integrations for X / Instagram / TikTok / Farcaster, Socket.io or Ably at scale. How We'll Work Phase 0 — Paid Trial. First small milestone (one contract or one backend service) at agreed scope. Used to evaluate code quality, communication, and judgment before committing to the full build. Phase 1 — Contracts. All core contracts deployed to Base Sepolia with full Foundry test coverage. Optional: third-party audit before mainnet. Phase 2 — Backend + Indexer. Auth, feed, chat, indexer, notifications. Wired to existing frontend. Phase 3 — Social Integrations + Scheduler. Platform-by-platform rollout, X first. Phase 4 — LP Farming, Governance, Agent Framework V1. Expectations across all phases: clean PRs with descriptive commits, written design docs before any non-trivial contract or service, tests alongside code, async-friendly communication, weekly written status.
