Web3 Onchain Analytics Dashboard — Produ.t: Web3 Onchain Analytics Dashboard — Product Research & Technical Task
Web3 Onchain Analytics Dashboard — Produ.i: Hourly: $45.00 - $75.00 Expert Est. time: 3 to 6 months, 30+ hrs/week
Web3 Onchain Analytics Dashboard — Produ.d: We are looking for a highly skilled Web3 developer, product engineer, or onchain analytics specialist to help design and potentially build a next-generation multi-chain analytics platform focused on onchain intelligence, wallet analytics, DeFi monitoring, smart money tracking, trading analytics, liquidity visualization, protocol analytics, and real-time blockchain activity. The platform should combine ideas and workflows similar to modern crypto analytics terminals while significantly improving usability, flexibility, real-time capabilities, and workflow efficiency for traders, analysts, DeFi users, funds, and crypto-native teams. The main goal is to create a unified analytics environment capable of aggregating, indexing, analyzing, and visualizing blockchain activity across multiple ecosystems. The product should function as a professional onchain intelligence terminal for traders, crypto funds, researchers, protocol teams, analysts, DeFi users, compliance/risk teams, and ecosystem operators. Users should be able to monitor smart money wallets, track capital flows, analyze token ecosystems, visualize liquidity movement, monitor TVL and protocol activity, identify trading opportunities, create custom dashboards, receive alerts for important onchain events, and analyze wallet behavior across multiple chains. Unlike simple TVL aggregators or isolated blockchain explorers, the platform should provide unified multi-chain visibility, advanced filtering, wallet intelligence, real-time analytics, customizable workflows, SQL-style query exploration, and interactive visualizations. Core use cases include smart money monitoring, DeFi analytics, wallet intelligence, and research workflows. Traders should be able to track whale wallets, monitor inflows and outflows, identify accumulation/distribution patterns, monitor perp positioning and liquidity, detect unusual activity, and receive real-time alerts when large wallets accumulate tokens or move capital across ecosystems. DeFi users should be able to analyze TVL changes, compare protocols, monitor yield opportunities, track liquidity rotations, visualize bridge flows, and analyze LP activity. Wallet intelligence tools should support wallet search, tagged address analysis, transaction history analysis, wallet clustering, behavioral tracking, and portfolio exposure analysis. Research users should be able to build custom dashboards, run SQL-style queries, analyze ecosystem metrics, compare chains and protocols, export data, and generate reports. The MVP should initially support Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Solana, BNB Chain, Hyperliquid, and Bitcoin, while keeping the architecture chain-agnostic for future scaling. Main dashboard modules should include Portfolio Overview, Wallet Profiler, Smart Money Tracker, Protocol TVL Dashboard, Token Flow Monitor, Whale Activity Feed, Liquidity Movement Visualization, DeFi Yield Monitor, and Ecosystem Heatmaps. Wallet profiling should support tagged wallet identification, balance tracking, historical activity, token exposure, counterparty analysis, and recent transaction feeds. The analytics layer should allow users to run custom SQL-style queries, create saved dashboards, filter by chain/token/protocol, export analytics, and generate charts and tables. The visualization layer should support line charts, heatmaps, network graphs, wallet relationship mapping, flow diagrams, candlestick charts, liquidity maps, and real-time feeds. Version 1 features may include Telegram, Discord, Email, and Webhook alerts for whale movements, smart money entries, TVL spikes, liquidity shifts, token unlocks, bridge inflows, and unusual activity. Advanced analytics may later include AI-generated summaries, narrative detection, wallet scoring, ecosystem trend analysis, perp funding analytics, liquidation tracking, and copy-trading signals. The platform should also support REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, websocket streaming, CSV/JSON exports, RBAC permissions, shared dashboards, private workspaces, and collaboration tools. Potential data sources include blockchain nodes, RPC providers, indexers, subgraphs, protocol APIs, market data providers, and oracle feeds. The architecture should support EVM chains, Solana, Bitcoin, Hyperliquid, and future integrations. The expected data pipeline is Node/RPC → Ingestion → Decoding → Indexing → Storage → API Layer → Frontend Dashboards. Key requirements include near real-time updates, scalable indexing, historical backfilling, ABI decoding, event normalization, and a multi-chain abstraction layer. Potential infrastructure stack includes ClickHouse, PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB, Redis, and Kafka. Preferred frontend stack includes React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, and D3.js/ECharts/TradingView integrations. The user experience should feel closer to institutional trading terminals and modern analytics platforms with fast navigation, dense information layouts, modular widgets, customizable workspaces, drill-down interactions, responsive design, and real-time updates. Expected infrastructure includes Docker, Kubernetes, AWS/GCP, CI/CD pipelines, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring, centralized logging, backups, monitoring systems, and autoscaling. Security requirements include HTTPS, RBAC, secure API handling, secrets management, audit logging, and rate limiting. Potential monetization models include free tiers, premium subscriptions, API access plans, institutional licenses, white-label analytics, enterprise dashboards, paid alerts/signals, and advanced research tools. Target users may include traders, crypto funds, market makers, protocol teams, researchers, and analytics firms. As part of the evaluation process, candidates should complete a practical product/research task. Candidates should design a prototype concept for a smart money dashboard, DeFi analytics terminal, wallet intelligence platform, liquidity flow tracker, perp analytics dashboard, or token ecosystem explorer. Deliverables should include a product concept explaining target users, the problem being solved, daily utility, and competitive advantage; dashboard mockups or UI concepts; explanation of required data and indexing logic; example SQL queries, filters, dashboards, metrics, and visualizations; and optionally architecture diagrams, backend ideas, API concepts, AI integrations, alert systems, scoring logic, or monetization ideas. You need to understand how to do both. What we mean by AI-native We are not interested in people who use AI to write a first draft and then lightly edit it. We want someone who uses AI as a genuine workflow tool for keyword clustering, content briefs, SERP analysis, schema generation, internal linking maps, and content production at pace. In your application, tell us specifically which tools you use and how. If you can not give a concrete answer, you are not what we are looking for. What we mean by proven work We will not commission work from anyone who cannot show us verifiable ranking results. We want to see: • At least two examples of condition or service pages you built that rank in the top 20 for competitive health, medical, or clinic keywords • The keyword, the URL, the position, and when you built it • GSC screenshots or Ahrefs/Semrush data preferred • Bonus: examples in Shopify specifically, or in a YMYL (Your Money Your Life) health niche where Google applies stricter quality standards If your examples are NDA-protected, tell us that upfront and describe the results without the URL. We will verify via follow-up. The site wearenaya.com — built on Shopify. UK English throughout. No em dashes. Plain-spoken, warm, clinically credible tone. Brand palette is maroon, gold, and cream. You will work to written brand guidelines. What good looks like You read the brief, you ask one or two sharp questions, and you come back with a clear page structure and keyword map before writing a word. You understand that a condition page for a private London clinic is not the same as a listicle. You know the difference between E-E-A-T signals and word count padding. You have worked on Shopify before and know its SEO constraints. Engagement This starts as a project-based engagement covering the initial page build. If the work is good it becomes ongoing. We move fast and we expect the same. Budget: Open to proposals. Include your rate per page and an estimate for the initial scope. Timeline: First deliverable within two weeks of briefing. To apply 1. Confirm you have read this in full by starting your message with the word “Neuromodulation” 2. Share two to three examples of ranked health or clinic pages you have built, with keyword and position data 3. Describe your AI workflow in two to three sentences — tools, how you use them, what it changes about your output 4. Your rate per page and overall estimate for the scope described above Applications without ranking examples will not be considered.

Developing the existing service book sys.t: Developing the existing service book system
Developing the existing service book sys.i: Hourly Intermediate Est. time: Less than 1 month, 30+ hrs/week
Developing the existing service book sys.d: Project Description — Extension of Existing Online Service Book System I currently have an existing online service book / service documentation system. I want to develop it step by step into a more complete business management system for service, construction, installation, maintenance and technical companies. The system should help companies manage clients, projects, employees, work orders, working hours, breaks, photos, project changes, reports, offers, invoices and client access. The main goal is to keep everything documented in one place: working hours, breaks, photos, changes, messages, decisions, documents and reports. 1. Main Idea of the System The system should support the full workflow of a company: The company creates a client. The company creates a project or work order. The planner/office adds plans, work description, materials, prices and notes. The foreman or office assigns an employee to the job. The employee sees their daily work plan on the phone. The employee starts work, records working time, breaks, before/after photos and comments. If the architect/planner changes something in the project, the employee receives a notification and must confirm whether they understand the change. The employee can report a problem, add a photo, add a comment or ask a question. The office/foreman approves the completed work. The system generates a PDF work report. The client receives limited access to the report, photos, offer or invoice. The company has a full history of all changes, working hours, photos, messages and decisions. Main value of the system: Every working hour, break, photo, change, message, decision and document is stored in one place. 2. User Roles and Permissions The system should have role-based access control. Admin / Company Owner The admin can see and manage everything: projects, clients, employees, working hours, vacations, sick leave, prices, materials, invoices, reports, messages, settings, change history, audit log. Office / Back Office The office user can: create clients, create projects, plan employees, assign tasks, check working hours, approve reports, create invoices, manage documentation. Planner / Designer The planner can: create projects, add plans, add documents, add materials, add prices, create tasks, make project changes, send notes to the foreman or employee. Architect / Construction Manager The architect can have internal or external project access. The architect can: view the project, view project status, add a change, add a comment, approve or reject completed work, add documents, change priority, optionally view prices if the company allows it. Foreman The foreman can: see their team, see assigned projects, manage employee tasks, approve working time, check before/after photos, react to problems, send changes to employees, contact the planner, office and employee. Employee / Worker The employee can only see: their own work plan, their own tasks, address, work description, documents required for the job, notes, photos assigned to the task, changes that concern them. The employee can: start work, finish work, start a break, end a break, add before photos, add after photos, add problem photos, add comments, report a problem, confirm a change, request vacation, report sick leave, upload a medical certificate as photo or PDF. The employee should not see: purchase prices, margins, internal company data, data of other clients, full data of other employees, invoices, unless the company gives permission. Client The client has very limited access. The client can see only: their own projects, general project status, approved photos, approved report, offer, invoice, items requiring approval, communication addressed directly to the client. The client must not see: internal conversations, purchase prices, margins, internal notes, employee break times, unless the company decides to show them, data of other clients, data of other employees. 3. System Modules Module 1: Companies The system must support multiple companies. Each company should have its own account and its own data. Functions: company details, logo, address, UID/VAT/MWST number, VAT settings, users, roles, permissions, report templates, invoice templates, working time settings, vacation settings, notification settings. The system should be multi-tenant. Data from one company must never be visible to another company. Module 2: Employees Each employee should have a profile. Employee data: first name and last name, phone number, e-mail, position, role, optional hourly rate, employment percentage / workload, number of vacation days, used vacation days, remaining vacation days, active/inactive status, documents, working time history, sick leave history, vacation history. Module 3: Working Time The employee must be able to record: work start, work end, break start, break end, time on a specific project, time on a specific task, travel time optionally, overtime, comment for the time entry. The system should show: daily hours, weekly hours, monthly hours, hours per project, hours per client, overtime, missing breaks, manually changed entries, who approved the time. The foreman or office should be able to approve or reject working hours. Every manual change to working time must be saved in the history: who changed it, when it was changed, what the previous value was, what the new value is, reason for the change. Module 4: Vacation and Absences The employee should be able to report: vacation, sick leave, accident, doctor appointment, unpaid leave, late arrival, early leave, other absence. Vacation Request The employee selects: date from, date to, number of days, comment. The system shows: how many vacation days the employee has, how many were used, how many are left, whether another employee has vacation at the same time. The office/admin can: approve, reject, add a comment. Sick Leave The employee reports: date from, expected date to, percentage of incapacity for work, comment, medical certificate as photo or PDF. The system should have statuses: reported, document required, approved, rejected, completed. Module 5: Clients Each client should have a client profile. Client data: name/company name, address, phone, e-mail, contact person, object/location addresses, project history, invoice history, documents, photos, notes. For business clients: UID/VAT/MWST number, billing contact, technical contact, payment terms, optional discount. Module 6: Projects A project is a larger unit of work. Examples: bathroom renovation, installation work, building service, cleaning service, heating replacement, garden work, technical maintenance. Each project should have: project name, project number, client, address, description, status, start date, end date, responsible person, planner, architect, foreman, employees, PDF plans, photos, documents, budget, prices, materials, tasks, messages, change history. Project statuses: Draft, Planned, Approved, In Progress, Waiting for Client, Waiting for Material, Reported as Completed, Checked, Invoiced, Completed, Complaint. Module 7: Tasks / Work Orders Each project can have multiple tasks or work orders. Each task should have: name, description, project, client, address, date, time, priority, assigned employee, assigned foreman, checklist, materials, photos, documents, comments, required before/after photos, planned time, actual time, status. Task statuses: Draft, Planned, Assigned, In Progress, Break, Question, Change Open, Reported as Completed, Checked, Billed, Completed. Module 8: Work Planning / Scheduling The company should have a work calendar. Views: day, week, month, employee, project, team, optional map view. Functions: assign tasks to employees, drag & drop planning, change time, change employee, notify employee about changes, detect scheduling conflicts, vacation overview, sick leave overview, employee availability overview. The employee should only see their own work plan in the mobile app/PWA. Module 9: Photos and Documentation The employee must be able to upload photos. Photo categories: before work, during work, after work, problem, damage, material, receipt, document, hidden installation, approval/acceptance. Each photo should have: author, date, time, project, task, category, comment, visibility status: internal / visible to client, optional GPS location. Premium feature for later: drawing on photos, arrows, circles, text annotations. Photos should not be permanently deleted by normal users. They can be hidden, but the system must save who hid the photo and why. Module 10: Internal Communication Instead of WhatsApp The system should include built-in communication. Messages should be assigned to: project, task, client, change request, problem report. Conversation types: internal company conversation, task conversation, project conversation, client conversation, architect conversation, problem report. Important rule: Messages cannot be permanently deleted. Possible rules: user can edit a message only for a few minutes, after editing, the version history remains saved, user cannot permanently delete a message, admin can hide a message with a reason, the system saves who hid the message, when and why. Functions: mention users with @name, attachments, photos, PDF files, read status, thread replies, notifications, urgent messages, read confirmation. Module 11: Changes / Change Management This is a very important module. If the architect, planner, office or foreman changes something in the project, the system must save it. A change can concern: plan, material, price, deadline, work scope, employee, priority, document, instruction. Each change must include: author, date, time, old value, new value, reason, affected persons, confirmation status. The employee receives a notification: “A change has been made in this task. Please confirm whether you understand it.” The employee must click: I understand I do not understand If the employee clicks “I do not understand”, they must add a comment. Example comments: “I do not have this material.” “The plan is not clear.” “The situation on site is different.” “I need a decision from the foreman.” “The client said something different.” The system should show a list of unconfirmed changes. Module 12: Materials, Prices and Costs The system should allow adding materials to a project/task. Fields: material name, quantity, unit, purchase price, selling price, margin, supplier, item/article number, receipt photo, whether it should be added to the invoice. Price visibility should depend on the user role. The employee can add material, but does not have to see the price. Admin/office can see: purchase cost, selling price, margin, project profitability. The client sees only approved items on the offer/invoice. Module 13: PDF Report After completing a task, the system should generate a PDF report. The report should include: company logo, company details, client details, project number, task number, address, date, employees, working time, breaks optionally, description of completed work, before photos, after photos, problem photos, used materials, comments, recommendations, additional work to be done, employee signature, client signature optionally. The company should be able to choose what the client can see in the report. There should be two types of reports: internal report, client report. Module 14: Client Portal The client receives access via link or login. The client can see: their own projects, status, approved photos, reports, offers, invoices, recommendations, items requiring approval. The client can: accept an offer, reject an offer, add a comment, report a complaint, download PDF, see the history of approved work. The client must not have access to internal company communication. Module 15: Offers and Invoices In the future, the system should support: offers, offer acceptance, invoices, QR invoice / QR-Rechnung for Switzerland, invoice line items, VAT/MWST, discounts, payment status, payment reminders, export to accounting. At the beginning, invoices can be simpler, but the system should be designed so that a full invoicing module can be added later. Module 16: Notifications The system should have notifications: in-app, e-mail, push notifications, SMS optionally. Notifications should be sent when: a new task is added, the plan is changed, the architect adds a change, the employee must confirm a change, the employee clicks “I do not understand”, the employee adds a photo, the employee finishes work, the employee reports a problem, the employee requests vacation, the employee reports sick leave, a task is delayed, the client accepts an offer, the client adds a comment. For urgent events, SMS can be added as a paid option. Module 17: Change History / Audit Log The system must have a full audit log. The audit log should record: who logged in, who created a project, who changed a task, who changed a price, who added a photo, who hid a photo, who sent a message, who edited a message, who changed working hours, who approved working time, who approved vacation, who added sick leave, who generated a report, who sent a document to the client. For every important change, the system must save: user, date, time, old value, new value, reason, if required. Development Approach I do not want to build the full enterprise system at once. I want to develop this in stages. Stage 1 — Audit of Existing System First, I need you to check my current online service book system and tell me: Can it be extended? Is the code quality good enough? Is the database structure usable? Is the photo/document storage good enough? Can roles and permissions be added? Would you recommend extending it or rebuilding it? How many hours would the first MVP take? What would be the fixed price for the first stage? Stage 2 — First MVP The first MVP should include: companies, users, roles and permissions, clients, employees, projects, tasks/work orders, working time, breaks, vacation requests, sick leave, before/after photos, comments, change confirmation: “I understand / I do not understand”, PDF report, simple client portal, basic audit log. Stage 3 — Later Features Later, I want to add: offers, invoices, Swiss QR invoice / QR-Rechnung, materials and costs, SMS notifications, full client approval workflow, GPS/maps, accounting export, more advanced reports, native mobile app if needed. Technical Expectations The system should preferably be: web-based, mobile-friendly, usable as PWA on phones, multi-tenant, secure, scalable, easy to extend, with role-based permissions, with photo/document storage, with PDF generation, with audit log, with backup, with future API possibility. For the first version, I do not need a native iOS/Android app. A responsive web app / PWA is enough. Questions for the Developer Please answer: Can you extend my existing online service book system? Do you need access to the source code to check it? What tech stack do you recommend? Would you extend the existing system or rebuild it? How many hours do you estimate for Stage 1? How many hours do you estimate for the MVP? Can you work with milestones? Can you provide a fixed price for the first stage? Can you create a clean database structure for future expansion? Can you build this as a SaaS system for multiple companies? Can you make the worker interface simple and mobile-friendly? Can you add audit log and role-based permissions? Can the system later be extended with offers, invoices, QR-Rechnung and SMS?
